And all the land of Egypt was hungry, and the people appealed unto Pharaoh for bread, and Pharaoh said to all the Egyptians, “Go to Yoseph, and anything that he says to you, you will do.”
And famine was on the face of all the Earth, and Yoseph opened the granaries and he sold to the Egyptians, and the famine prevailed in the land of Egypt.
And all the Earth came to Egypt to buy from Yoseph, because the famine was severe in all the land of Egypt.
And Yaquuv saw that there was grain in Egypt, and Yaquuv said to his sons, “You shall not be afraid.
Behold, I heard that there is grain in Egypt; go down there and buy for us there, and we will live and we will not die.”
And ten brothers of Yoseph went down to buy grain from Egypt.
And Yaquuv did not send Benyamin, the brother of Yoseph, with his brothers, because he said, “Lest calamity will come to him.”
And the sons of Israel came to buy with those who were going, because the famine was severe in the land of Canaan.
And Yoseph was authorized over the land, and he sold to all the people of the land and the brothers of Yoseph came and bowed down to him on their faces on the ground.

And Yoseph saw his brothers and he recognized them, and he acted deceitfully toward them and he spoke with them harshly and said to them, “From where are you?” And they said, “From the land of Canaan; we come to buy grain.”
And Yoseph recognized his brothers and they did not recognize him.
And Yoseph remembered the dream that he dreamt of them and said to them, “You are spies and you have come to notice the report of the land.”
And they said, “No, our Lord, your Servants have come to buy grain.
All of us are sons of one man, and we are righteous, and your Servants are not spies.”
Yoseph said to them, “It is not so; you have come to show the report of the land.”
And they said to him, “We are twelve brothers; your Servants are the sons of one man in the land of Canaan, and behold, the youngest is with our father today, and one is not.”
Yoseph said to them, “It is what I said to you; you are spies!
In this you will be proven; by the life of Pharaoh, you shall not go out from here unless your little brother comes here.
Send one of you to bring your brother and you will all be bound and your words will be tested, if you have spoken the truth, otherwise, by the life of Pharaoh, you are spies!”
And he cast them into the prison house three days.
And Yoseph said to them on the third day, “Do this and live; I worship God:
If you are just, your one brother will be bound in your prison house, and you go bring out grain to the hungry that are in your house:
And bring your little brother to me, and your words will be believed, and you shall not die”, and they did so.

And they said, each man to his brothers, “Truly we are condemned concerning our brother, for we did see the anxiety of his soul when he was making supplication to us and we did not listen to him, because of this, this distress has come upon us.”
And Rubil answered and said to them, “Did I not say to you, ‘Do not sin against the boy’, and you did not listen? Even now, behold, his blood is required.
And they were not aware that Yoseph heard, because an interpreter was standing among them.
And he turned from their presence and wept, and returned to them and he spoke with them, and he took Shemon from them and bound him in their sight.
And Yoseph commanded and they filled their vessels with grain, and to return their money to each man in his sack, and to give them provisions for the road, and they did so for them.
And they took their food on their donkeys and they went from there.
And one of them opened his sack to throw hay for his donkey, at the lodging house, and he saw his money laid in the mouth of his pack.

And he said to his brother, “My money is returned and behold, it is in the mouth of my pack”, and their heart was moved, and each man marveled with his brother and they said, “What is this that God has done to us?”
And they came to Yaquuv their father, to the land of Canaan and told him everything that had occurred to them, and they said to him.
“The man, the Lord of the land, spoke with us severely and he considered us as spies of the land.
And we were saying to him, “We are just and we are not spies:
We are twelve brothers, sons of our father, and one is not, and the youngest is with our father today in the land of Canaan.
And the man, the Lord of the land, said to us, ‘In this I shall know that you are righteous: leave your one brother with me and take the grain for the hungry that are in your house and go:
And bring your little brother to me, and I shall know that you are not spies, but you are good, and I shall give your brother to you and you will do business in the land.’”

And it was that when they emptied their sacks, behold, the moneybag of the man’s money was in the mouth of his pack, and they saw the moneybags of their money, they and their father, and they were afraid.
Yaquuv their father said to them, “You have bereaved me; Yoseph is not, and Shemon is not, and you will take Benyamin; all these things are the end of me!”
And Rubil said to his father, “Put my two sons to death if I shall not bring him to you; deliver him to me and I shall return him to you.”

And he said, “My son will not go down with you, because his brother is dead, and he alone is left to his mother, and calamity will come to him on the road that you will travel, and my old age will go down in misery to Sheol.”
And the famine was severe in the land.
And when they had finished eating the grain that they had brought from Egypt, Yaquuv their father said to them, “Go down; buy a little grain for us.”
Yehuda said to him, “The man swore and testified to us and said, “You will not see my face unless your brother is with you.
If you send our brother with us, we will go down and we will buy grain for us.
And if you do not send, we are not going down, because of what the man said to us: ‘You will not see my face unless your brother is with you.’
Israel, their father, said to them, “Why did you afflict me and tell the man that you surely have a brother?”

And they said, “The man pointedly asked about us and about our family, and he said, “Is your father still living and do you have a brother?”, and we told him, because of these words. Were we to know that he would say, ‘Bring down your brother?.’
And Yehuda said to Israel, his father, “Send the boy with us, and we will arise and go and we will live and not die, neither we, nor you, neither our little ones.
I pledge security for him from my hands; you may require it; if I do not bring him to you and set him before you, I shall be sinning against my father all my days
Also, if we had not delayed, doubtless we would have returned a second time.”

And Israel their father said to them, “If it is so, do this: take from the good of the land in your vessels and bring down a gift to the man, a little balsam and a little honey and balm and pistachios and terebinth berries and almonds:
And take double money in your hands and give back the money that he returned to the mouth of your sacks in your hands, lest it was an error
And take your brother and arise and return to the man
And God Almighty give you mercy before the man, and he shall send with you your other brother and Benyamin, and inasmuch as I am bereaved, I am bereaved.”
And the men took this gift and they took double the money in their hands, and they drove Benyamin with them, and they arose and they went down to Egypt and they stood before Yoseph.
And Yoseph saw Benyamin with them, and he said to his Custodian, “Bring the men to the house; kill an animal and prepare it, because the men will eat with me at noonday.”
And the Servant did as Yoseph said and brought the men to the house of Yoseph.

And they were afraid when they brought them to the house of Yoseph, and they said, “Because of the money that he returned in our sacks at the first, they are bringing us to magnify themselves against us, and to deal treacherously against us, and to enslave us and our donkeys as slaves.”
And they approached the man, the Steward of Yoseph, and they spoke with him in the gate of the house.
And they said to him, “We beg our Lord, truly we went down at the first to buy grain:
And when we went on to the lodging house, we opened our sacks, and behold, the money of each man was in the mouth of his sack; we are returning our money by its weight in our hands.
And we bring down other money with us to buy grain; we do not know who put our money in the mouth of our sacks.”
He said to them, “Peace to you; you shall not be afraid; your God, the God of your father, put a treasure for you in your sacks; your money came with me”; and he brought out Shemon to them.
And the Servant brought the men to the house of Yoseph and he gave them water and they washed their feet and he set fodder for their animals.
And they prepared the gift until Yoseph entered at noonday, because they heard that they were eating bread there.
And Yoseph entered the house and they brought the gift that was in their hands to the house and they bowed to him on the ground.
And he invoked their peace and said to them, “Is your old father well of whom you told me? Is he still living?”
And they said, “Your Servant our father is well”, and they said to him, “He is still living” and they knelt and they bowed.

And he lifted his eyes and he saw Benyamin, his brother, son of his mother, and he said to them, “Is this your little brother of whom you have told me?”, and he said, “May God have favor upon you my son.”
And Yoseph made haste, because he showed his affections concerning his brother, and he wanted to weep, and entered the inner room, and he wept there.
And he washed his face and went out, and he was restrained and said that bread would be set out for them.

And they set for him alone, and for them by themselves, and for the Egyptians who eat with him by themselves, because that Egyptians cannot eat bread with Hebrews, because that is defilement to Egyptians.
And they were seated before him, the elder according to his age, and the younger according to his youth; and the men marveled, each man with his fellow.
And they took a portion for themselves from before him, and the portion of Benyamin was greater than the portion of all their five portions, and they drank and they became drunk with him.
And he commanded the Steward of his house, and he said, “Fill the sacks of the men with grain however much they can pick up and put the money of each man in the mouth of his sack.
And take my cup of silver and put it in the mouth of the sack of the little one, and the money of his purchase”, and the Servant did according to the word of Yoseph.
And when it was dawn, the men had started to go, they and their donkeys.
They went out from the city and had not gone far, and Yoseph said to his Custodian, “Arise, chase after the men and overtake them and say to them, ‘Why have you paid evil in place of good?
This is the cup in which my Lord drinks, also truly he divines with it; you have done evil in that which you have done.’”
And he overtook them, and he spoke to them according to these words.
They were saying to him, “Our Lord should not say these words. God forbid that your Servants would do this thing.
Behold the money that we found in the mouth of our sacks we returned it from the land of Canaan; how were we stealing gold or silver from the house of your Lord?
Whoever of your Servants is found with it should die, and we shall be Servants to our Lord.”
He said to them, “Also now according to your statement, so it shall be; whoever will be found with it, he will be to me a Servant and you will be innocent.”
And they hurried and they lowered each man his sack on the ground, and they opened each man his sack.
And they searched, beginning from the elder and finishing with the youngest, and the cup was found in the sack of Benyamin.
And they ripped open their garments, and each man packed up on his donkey and they returned to the city.
And Yehuda and his brothers came into Yoseph, and he was still there, and they fell before him on the ground.
Yoseph said to them, “What is this deed that you have done? Do you not know that a man like me surely divines?”

And Yehuda said, “What will we say to my Lord, and what will we speak, and in what will we be innocent before God? Sin was found with your Servants; behold we are Servants to my Lord, even we and he in whose hand the cup was found.”
He said to them, “God forbid me that I would do this; the man with whom the cup was found he shall be to me a Servant, and you go up in peace to your father.”
And Yehuda came near to him and said, “I beg of you my Lord, let your Servant speak a word before you my Lord, and let not your anger be hot against your Servant, because you are as Pharaoh.
My Lord asked his Servants and said, ‘Do you have a father or a brother?.’
And we were saying to my Lord, ‘We have an old father and a son, a little son of his old age, and his brother died, and he alone is left by his mother, and his father loves him.
And you said to your Servants, ‘Bring him down to me and I shall set my eyes upon him.’
And we were saying to my Lord, ‘The boy cannot leave his father, and if he leaves his father, he will die.’
And you said to your Servants, ‘If your little brother does not come down with you’ you shall not again see my face.’
And when we came up to your Servant, our father, and we told him the statement of my Lord.
Your Servant, our father, said to us, ‘Return, buy for us a little grain.’
And we were saying to our father, ‘We cannot go down; if our little brother will go down with us, we will go down, because we cannot see the face of the man when our little brother is not with us.’
And your Servant, our father, said to us, ‘You know that my wife gave to me two sons”:
And one went out from my presence, and I said, “Surely he is killed”, and I have not seen him until now:
And you take also this one from my presence, and should evil come to him, so you will bring down my old age in affliction to Sheol.’
And now when we go to your Servant, our father, and the boy is not with us, and his soul is beloved to him as himself;
And when he sees there is no boy with us, he will die, and we will bring your Servant in the old age of your Servant, our father, in wretchedness to Sheol.
Because your Servant pledged security for the boy from the presence of our father and I said, ‘If I do not bring him to you, I shall sin against my father always.’
And now, let your Servant stay for the sake of the boy as a Servant to my Lord, and let the boy go up with his brothers.
For how shall I go up to my father and the boy not be with me, lest I shall see the evil that will happen to my father?”

And Yoseph was not able to keep himself back from all who were standing before him, and he said, “Send everyone out from my presence”, and no man stayed with him when Yoseph was revealed to his brothers.
And he lifted his voice with weeping and the Egyptians heard, and it was heard in the house of Pharaoh.
And Yoseph said to his brothers, “I am Yoseph, your brother. Is my father still living?”, and his brothers were not able to give to him an answer, because they were afraid in his presence.
And Yoseph said to his brothers, “Come near to me”, and they approached him and he said to them, “I am Yoseph your brother whom you sold to the Egyptians.
And now you will not sorrow for yourselves and do not let it be evil in your eyes that you sold me here, because for sustenance God has sent me before you.
Because now there would have been two years of famine within the land, and again there would be five years when there is no one sowing or reaping.
And God sent me before you to establish you as a remnant and to save life for you with a great salvation in the earth.
And now, it was not you who sent me here, but God made me a father to Pharaoh and Lord over all his house and Ruler in all the land of Egypt.
Quickly go up to my father and say to him, “Thus says your son, Yoseph, ‘God has made me the Lord over all Egypt; come down to me and do not tarry:
And settle in the land of Geshan, and be near to me, you and your children, and the children of your house, and your flocks, and your cattle and everything that you have.
And I shall support you there, because the famine remains five years more, lest you and your household be destroyed, and everything that you have.
And behold, your eyes see and the eyes of my brother Benyamin, that my mouth is speaking with you.
And show my father all my glory that is in Egypt, and all that you see, and hurry, bring my father down here.’”
And he fell on the neck of Benyamin, his brother, and wept, and Benjamin wept on his neck.
And he kissed all his brothers and wept upon them, and after that his brothers spoke with him.
And a voice was heard in the house of Pharaoh, that said, “The brothers of Yoseph have come”, and the report was pleasing in the eyes of Pharaoh and in the eyes of his Servants.
And Pharaoh said to Yoseph, “Say to your brothers thus: ‘Load your animals with grain and go bring it to the land of Canaan;
And take your father and the people of your house and bring them to me, and I shall give you the good of the land of Egypt, and eat the fat of the land.’
And you now are authorized to say so to your brothers: ‘Take for yourselves wagons from the land of Egypt for your wives and for your little ones and take your father and come.
And do let not your eyes gaze on your property, because the blessing of all the land of Egypt is yours.’”
And the sons of Israel did so, and Yoseph gave wagons to them by the word of the mouth of Pharaoh, and he gave provisions for the road to all of them.
And he gave every man a pair of garments, and to Benyamin he gave three hundred silver coins and five pairs of garments.
And thus he sent to his father, and ten donkeys loaded with the good of the land of Egypt, and ten she donkeys loaded with grain and wine and provisions for his father on the road.
And he sent them and they went and he said to them, “Do not argue on the road.”
And they came up from Egypt and they came to the land of Canaan to Yaquuv their father.
And they told him and they said to him, “Yoseph is still alive and he is Ruler in all the land of Egypt”, and he turned away in his heart because he did not believe them.
And they told him all the words that Yoseph had said to them, and Yaquuv saw the wagons that Yoseph sent to take him, and the spirit of Yaquuv their father was comforted:
And he said, “This is great to me, that Yoseph my son is still alive; I shall go see him before I die.”
And Israel picked up, he and all that he had, and he came to Beersheba and offered a sacrifice to the God of his father Isaaq.
And God spoke to Israel in a vision of the night and said, “Yaquuv, Yaquuv”, and he said, “Here I am.”
And he said to him, “I AM Eil, the God of your father; do not fear to go down to Egypt, because I shall make you there a great people:
I shall go down with you and I shall bring you up, and Yoseph will lay his hands on your eyes.”
And Yaquuv arose from Beersheba, and Yaquuv took the sons of Israel, their father, and their little ones, and their wives in the wagons that Pharaoh had sent to carry him.
And they drove their cattle and their possessions that they had acquired in the land of Canaan, and they came to Egypt, Yaquuv and all his seed with him:
His sons and the sons of his sons with him, his daughters and the daughters of his sons, and all his seed he brought with him to Egypt.
And these are the names of the sons of Israel who entered Egypt: Yaquuv and his sons, the first born of Yaquuv, Rubil.
The sons of Rubil: Khenook and Palu and Khetsroon and Karmi.
And the sons of Shemon: Yemuyel and Yamin and Ahar and Yakeen and Tsakhar and Shawal son of a Canaanitess.
And the sons of Levi: Gershoon and Qahath and Merari.
And the sons of Yehuda: Eyr and Unaan and Shela and Pharets and Zarakh; and Eyr and Unaan died in the land of Canaan, and the sons of Pharets were Khetsruun and Khamuul.
And the sons of Isakaar: Tula and Phua and Uyab and Shemroon.
And the sons of Zabalaun: Sadar and Aluun and Nakhlayel.
These are the sons of Leah that she bore to Yaquuv in Padan of Aram plus Dinah his daughter; all the souls of his sons and of his daughters were thirty and three.
And the sons of Gad: Zephioon and Khagi and Shooni and Atsboon and Adi and Arood and Adri.
And the sons of Asheer: Yamna and Yashwa and Ishwi and Bria and Sarakh their sister; and the sons of Bria: Khaber and Malkeel.
These are the children of Zelpha whom Laban gave to Leah his daughter, and she bore these sixteen souls to Yaquuv.
And the sons of Rakhil, the wife of Yaquuv: Yoseph and Benjamin.
And Menasheh and Aphreim were born to Yoseph in the land of Egypt, to whom Asyath daughter of Putiphra, Priest of Oun, had given birth by him.
And the sons of Benyamin: Baala and Akbar and Ashkuul and Gaara and Naaman and Akhi and Aruush and Mapheem and Khapheem and Edar.
These are the sons of Rakhil who had borne fourteen souls to Yaquuv.
And the sons of Dan: Khushim.
And the sons of Naphtali: Nakhtsayel and Guni and Yatsar and Khatsar and Shalim.
These are the sons of Balha, whom Laban gave to Rakhil his daughter, and she bore all these seven souls to Yaquuv.
All the souls that entered with Yaquuv into Egypt, that came out from his loins, apart from the wives of the children of Yaquuv, all the souls were sixty and six;
And the sons of Yoseph that were born to him in Egypt were two souls; all the souls of the house of Yaquuv who entered into Egypt were seventy.
And he sent Yehuda before him to Yoseph to appear before him to Geshan, and he came to the land of Geshan.
And Yoseph harnessed his chariots and he came up for the meeting of Israel his father, to Geshan, and he was seen by him and he fell on his neck and was weeping on his neck repeatedly.
And Israel said to Yoseph, “I may die therefore after I have seen your face, my son, for you are still living.”

And Yoseph said to his brothers and to the house of his father, “I shall go up; I shall tell Pharaoh and say to him, ‘My brothers and the house of my father who were in the land of Canaan have come to me;
And the men are Shepherds of sheep, because the men are owners of cattle, and they have brought their flocks and their oxen and everything that they have.
And when Pharaoh will call you and he will say to you, ‘What is your work?.’

Say to him, ‘My Lord, your Servants have been cattle men from their youth until now, also we and also our fathers’, because you will dwell in the land of Geshan because Egyptians despise all Shepherds of sheep.’”

And Yoseph came and he told Pharaoh and said to him, “My father and my brothers and their flocks and their oxen and all that they have, have come from the land of Canaan, and behold, they are dwelling in the land of Geshan.”
And he took five men of his brothers and he stood them before Pharaoh.
And Pharaoh said to the brothers of Yoseph, “What is your work?”, and they said to Pharaoh: “Your Servants are Shepherds of sheep, even we and also our fathers from our youth.”

And they said to Pharaoh, “We have come to dwell in the land, because there is no pasture for the flocks of your Servants, because the famine is severe in the land of Canaan, and now your Servants will dwell in the land of Geshan.”
And Pharaoh said to Yoseph, “Your father and your brothers have come to you:

The land of Egypt is before you; settle in the best of the land; your father and your brothers will dwell in the land of Geshan, and if you know that there are capable men among them, make them Masters of cattle over all that I have.”
And Yoseph brought Yaquuv his father and stood him before Pharaoh, and Yaquuv blessed Pharaoh.
And Pharaoh said to Yaquuv, “How many are the days of the years of your life?”

And Yaquuv said to Pharaoh, “The days of the years of my pilgrimage are a hundred and thirty years; the days of the years of my life are few and evil and they have not arrived to the years of the life of my fathers in the days of their travel.”
And Yaquuv blessed Pharaoh and Yaquuv went out from before Pharaoh.
And Yoseph settled his father and his brothers, and he gave to them an inheritance in the land of Egypt, in the best of the land, in the land of Ramsis, as Pharaoh commanded.
And Yoseph sustained his father and his brothers and all the house of his father with grain, according to family.
And there was no grain in all the land because the famine was very severe and all the land of Egypt was laid waste, and the land of Canaan, in the presence of the famine.
And Yoseph collected all the money that was found in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan for the grain that they had bought, and Yoseph brought the money to the house of Pharaoh.

And the money from the land of Egypt and from the land of Canaan was spent, and all the Egyptians came to Yoseph and they were saying, “Give us grain and we will not die before you, because the money for it is spent.”
Yoseph said to them, “Give me your livestock and I shall give to you for your livestock if the money is spent.”

And they brought to him their livestock, and Yoseph gave to them grain for horses, and for livestock of sheep, and for livestock of oxen, and of donkeys, and he sustained them with bread that year for all their livestock.

And that year ended, and they came to him in the next year, and they said to him, “We will not hide from our Lord, because all money is spent and the cattle of oxen are with our Lord, and nothing is left before our Lord except our lives and our lands:

Why should we die before you, even we and our lands? Buy us and our lands for bread and we and our lands will be Servants for Pharaoh; and give us seed and we will live and we will not die, and the land will not be destroyed.”
And Yoseph bought all the land of Egypt for Pharaoh, because the Egyptians sold, each man, his field, because the famine was severe upon them and it became the land of Pharaoh.
And he moved the people from town to town from one end of the border of Egypt and unto the other.

Only he did not buy the land of the Priests, because there was a standing covenant with the Priests from the presence of Pharaoh, and they were eating their portion that Pharaoh had given them; because of this they did not sell their land.
And Yoseph said to the people, “Now I have bought you today, you and your lands for Pharaoh, behold, for you have seed that you may sow in the land.

And whenever crops come in, you will give one fifth to Pharaoh, and four parts will be for you, for the seed of the field and for your food, and for the food of your households, and for the food of your little ones.”
And they said, “You have saved us; let us find mercy in the eyes of our Lord and we will be Servants to Pharaoh.”
And Yoseph set it a law until today over the land of Egypt: one fifth is Pharaoh’s; only the land of the Priests was not Pharaoh’s.
And Israel dwelt in the land of Egypt and in the land of Geshan and they became strong in it and they increased and multiplied greatly.
And Yaquuv lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years; and the days of Yaquuv, the years of his life, were a hundred and forty and seven years.

And the days approached for Israel to die, and he called Yoseph, his son, and he said to him, “If I have found favor in your eyes, place your hand under my loins and swear to me by LORD JEHOVAH, and perform grace and truth with me, and do not bury me in Egypt:
And I shall sleep with my fathers, and carry me from Egypt and bury me in their burial place”; and Yoseph said, “I shall do according to your word.”
And he said, “Swear to me”, and he swore to him, and Israel bowed on the top of his staff.
And after these matters, it was told to Yoseph, “Behold, your father is ill”, and he took his two sons with him, Menasheh and Aphreim.
And they told Yaquuv, and they said to him, “Behold, your son Yoseph has come to you”, and Israel was encouraged and he sat up on the bed.
And Israel said to Yoseph, “God Almighty was revealed to me in Luz in the land of Canaan and he blessed me.

And he said to me, ‘Behold, I am blessing you and I am multiplying you, and I shall give you the multitudes of the nations, and I shall give this land to your seed after you as an inheritance for eternity.’

And now, your two children that were born to you in the land of Egypt, before I came to you to the land of Egypt, are mine; Aphreim and Menasheh shall be mine, like Rubil and like Shemon, they shall be mine.
And the sons that you beget after them they shall be yours; they shall be called for the name of their brothers in their inheritance.

And I, when I came from Padan of Aram, Rakhyl died by me in the land of Canaan on the road, on a journey of about a Pharsakh of the land, to the entrance of Ephrath, and I buried her there on the road of Ephrath, which is Bayth Lekhem.”
And Israel saw the children of Yoseph, and said to him, “Who are these?”
And Yoseph said to his father, “They are my sons whom God gave to me here”; and he said, “Bring them to me and I shall bless them.”
And the eyes of Israel were dim from old age, and he was not able to see well, and he brought them to him, and he kissed them and he embraced them.
And Israel said to Yoseph, “The appearance of your face I had not hoped for, and behold, God has shown me also your sons.”
And he removed them from before his knees and they bowed before him on their faces on the ground.
And Yoseph took both the children, Aphreim at his right from the left of Israel and Menasheh at his left, from the right of Israel and he brought them near to him.
And Israel reached his right hand and he placed it on the head of Aphreim, he is the younger, and his left on the head of Menasheh, and he changed his hands because Menasheh was the firstborn.
And Yaquuv blessed Yoseph his son and said, “God, before whom my fathers, Abraham and Isaac, were pleasing, God, who supported me from my youth and unto today

The Messenger who has delivered me from every evil, will bless the boys, and my name will be called in them, and the name of my fathers Abraham and Isaac, and they shall grow and they shall multiply within the Earth.

And Yoseph saw that his father placed his right hand on the head of Aphreim, and it was displeasing in his eyes, and he held the hand of his father to move it from the head of Aphreim and to place it on the head of Menasheh.
And Yoseph said to his father, “It is not in this way, my father, but this is the first born; lay your right hand on his head.”

And his father would not, and he said, “I know, my son, I also know he shall be a people, also he shall be great, however his little brother will be greater than he, and his seed shall be the fullness of the nations.”
And he blessed them that day and he said, “In you Israel shall be blessed and they will say, ‘God make you as Aphreim and like Manassheh’”, and he put Aphreim before Menasheh.
And Israel said to Yoseph, “Behold, I am dying and God shall be with you and he shall return you to the land of your fathers.
And I, behold, I have given you one part more than your brethren, which I took from the hand of the Amorites with my sword and with my bow.”
And Yaquuv called his sons and said to them, “Be assembled and I shall show you the thing that will happen to you in the last of days.
Be gathered and hear, sons of Yaquuv, hear Israel your father.
Rubil my first born, you are my power and beginning of my strength, the foundation of exaltation and the foundation of strength
You wandered as waters; you will not persist, for you went up to the bedroom of your father; truly you defiled my bed and you went up.
Shemon and Levi, brothers, are weapons of rage by their nature.
Into their secrets my soul has not entered, and I have not come down from my glory into their assembly, because in their rage they murdered men and in their anger they tore down a city wall.
Cursed is their rage, because it is severe, and their anger, because that was violent. I shall divide them in Yaquuv and I shall scatter them in Israel.
Yehuda, to you your brethren shall give praise; your hand will be on the necks of your enemies; the sons of your father shall bow down to you.
Yehuda is the whelp of a lion; you went up from the killed prey, my son; he knelt and crouched as a lion and like a whelp of a lion, and who is it that will raise him up?
The scepter will not depart from Yehuda nor a Prophet from between his feet, until he will come whose it is, and the nations will look for him.
He will bind his colt to the vine and the foal of his donkey to vine shoots; he will whiten his clothing with wine and his cloak with the blood of grapes:
His eyes are more wine color than wine and his teeth are whiter than milk.
Zabalaun will dwell on the shore of the sea and he is by the beauty of ships and his border is unto Tsidon.
The man Isakaar is a mighty man that crouches in the place of the roads:
And he saw his dwelling, that it is beautiful, and his land is good, and he bowed his shoulder to servitude and it was for tribute.
Dan will judge his people as one of the tribes of Israel.
Dan Shall be a serpent upon the road and a dragon upon the path, to injure a horse in its foot and throw its rider on his back.
For your salvation I have waited, LORD JEHOVAH.
Gad will go forth with robbers and he will strike the heels.
Ashir’s land is good and he will give nourishment to the King.
Naphtali is a speedy Messenger; he gives beautiful speech.
Yoseph is the son of increase; the son of increase goes up as a spring with a vine supported, going up on the wall:
And they contended with him, and they have increased and the Lords of armies envied him:
And his bow returned in strength and they distributed the arms of his hands from the strong hands of Yaquuv, and from The Name of The Shepherd who is the Stone of Israel

The God of your father will help you and God Almighty will bless you with the blessing of Heaven from above, the blessing of the deep of the spring rain from beneath, the blessing of the breasts and of the wombs:
The blessing of your father prevailed over the blessings of my forefathers unto the hope of the hills of eternity; let them be on the head of Yoseph and on the head and crown of his brothers.
Benyamin is a plundering wolf; at dawn he will eat the prey and in the evening he will divide whatever is snatched.”
All of these are the twelve tribes of Israel, and this is what Yaquuv their father said to them; he spoke to them and he blessed them, each man; according to his blessing he blessed them.
And he commanded them, and their father said to them: “I am gathered to my people; bury me with my fathers in the cave that is in the field of Aphron the Khethite.
In the cave in the field of Aphron, that is before Mamreh, that is in the land of Canaan, where Abraham bought the field from Aphron, the Khethite, for an inheritance of the tomb.
There they buried Abraham and Sara his wife, and there they buried Isaaq and Raphqa his wife, and there I buried Leah.
The purchase of the field, and the cave which was from the sons of Khayth.”
And when Yaquuv had finished charging his sons, he stretched out his feet in bed, and he grew weak and died, and was gathered to his people.
And Yoseph fell on the face of his father, and wept upon him, and he kissed him.
And Yoseph commanded his Servants the Physicians to embalm his father, and the Physicians embalmed Israel.
And forty days were fulfilled to him, because thus the days of embalmment are completed, and the Egyptians mourned him seventy days.
And the days of his mourning passed, and Yoseph said to those of the house of Pharaoh, “If I have found favor in your eyes, say before Pharaoh,

‘My father made me swear and said to me, ‘Behold, I am dying; you will bury me in my grave that I bought for me in the land of Canaan’; now I shall go up there; I shall bury my father and I shall return.”
And Pharaoh said to Yoseph, “Go up and bury your father as he made you swear.”
And Yoseph came up to bury his father, and all the Servants of Pharaoh came up with him, and the Elders of his house, and all the Elders of the land of Egypt,
And all in the house of Yoseph and his brothers, and the house of his father, came up with him; only their little ones and their flocks and their oxen they left in the land of Geshan.
And with him also the chariots and horsemen came up and there was a very mighty host.

And they came as far as to the threshing floor of Atar, which is at the crossing of Jordan, and they mourned there a great and powerful mourning, and he made for his father a house of weeping, seven days.

And the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites, saw the grieving at the threshing floor of Atar, and they said, “This grieving is great for the Egyptians;” because of this, they called its name Ebal Metsrein, which is at the crossing of Jordan.
And his sons did to him just as he commanded them

And they took him away and brought him to the land of Canaan, and they buried him in the cave that is in the double field, for Abraham bought the field for an inheritance of the tomb from Aphron, the Khethite, that is before Mamreh.
And Yoseph returned to Egypt, he and his brothers, and everyone who came up with him to bury his father.

And after he buried his father, the brothers of Yoseph saw that their father was dead, and they were afraid and they were saying, “Lest Yoseph will harm us, and lest he will pay us all the evil that we did to him.”
And they visited and they came near to Yoseph, and they said to him, “Your father commanded before his death and said:

‘In this way you will say to Yoseph: “Pray, forgive the offense of your brethren and their sins, the evil that they have done to you’, and now, forgive the offense of the Servants of the God of your father”; and Yoseph wept when they spoke to him.
And his brothers went also, and they fell before him, and they said, “Behold, we are your Servants.”
And Yoseph said to them, “You shall not be afraid, because I am under God.
You counseled evil against me and God counseled good, to do as it is today, and he will save many people.
And now you shall not be afraid; I shall nurture you and your little ones”, and he comforted them and he satisfied their hearts.
And Yoseph dwelt in Egypt, he and all the house of his father, and Yoseph lived a hundred and ten years.
And Yoseph saw Aphreim's children, the third generation; also the children of Makir, son of Manassheh, were born on the knees of Yoseph.
And Yoseph said to his brothers, “I am dying, and God certainly remembers you, and he brings you up from this land to the land that he swore to Abraham and to Isaac and to Yaquuv.”
And Yoseph made the sons of Israel swear and he said, “God certainly remembers you; bring up my bones from here with you.”
And Yoseph died, son of a hundred and ten years, and they embalmed him and laid him in a mummy case in Egypt.

name: Exodus
chapters: And these are the names of the sons of Israel who entered Egypt with Yaquuv; the man and his household entered:
Rubil and Shemon and Levi and Yehuda.
And Issakar and Zebulon and Benyamin.
And Dan and Naphtali and Gad and Ashir.
And all the souls that came out from the loins of Yaquuv were seventy souls, and Yoseph was in Egypt.
And Yoseph died and all his brothers and all that generation.
And the sons of Israel increased and they had children, and they multiplied and they grew very strong, and the land was filled by them.
And a new King arose over Egypt that had not known Yoseph.
And he said to his people, “Behold, the people of the sons of Israel are many and are mightier than we:

Grant that we will be wise concerning them before they are multiplied, lest we come to war and they are added also unto our haters, and they will fight with us and they will drive us out from the land.”
And they placed evil Rulers over them that would oppress them in their oppression and they were building cities, places of storage for Pharaoh, Pithom and Raamsis.
And as much as those oppressed them, so they were multiplying, and thus they were growing strong, and it was distressing them before the children of Israel.
And the Egyptians were oppressing the children of Israel with hardship:
And they were making their lives bitter with hard labor in clay and in bricks and in every work of the field, all the servitude that they were serving for them with hardship.
And the King of Egypt said to the Hebrew Midwives, the name of one of whom was Pua, and the name of another, Shupra:
And he said to them, “When you happen to deliver for a Hebrew woman, be looking, when kneeling; if it is a male, you kill him, and if it is a female, you save her.”
And the midwives feared God and did not do as the King of Egypt told them, and they were saving the boys.
And the King of Egypt called the Midwives and he said to them, “Why are you making this response and are saving the boys?”
And the Midwives said to Pharaoh, “The Hebrews are not like the Egyptian women, because they are Midwives, and when the Midwives are not near them, they give birth.”
And God blessed the Midwives because they made this response, and he multiplied the people and they grew very strong.
And it was that as the midwives were in awe of God, he made them households.
And Pharaoh commanded all the people and said, “Every son who will be born, you will throw him into the river, and every daughter you will save.”
And a man went on who was of the house of Levi, and he took a daughter of Levi.
And the woman conceived and she bore a son, and she saw him that he was beautiful, and she hid him three months.

And she was not able to hide him again and she took for him an ark of acacia wood and she smeared it with resin and with pitch and she set the boy in it and she set it in the shallows on the shore of the river.
And his sister stood from a distance that she would know what would be done to him.
And the daughter of Pharaoh came down that she may wash in the river and her maids were walking on the side of the river, and she saw the ark in the shallows and she sent her maids and they took it.
And she opened it and she saw the boy and behold, the boy cried, and she had pity upon him, and she said, “This is from the children of the Hebrews.”
And his sister said to the daughter of Pharaoh, “I shall go and I shall call for you a woman nurse from the Hebrew women, and she will nurse this boy for you.”
And the daughter of Pharaoh said to her, “Go.” And the girl went and she called the mother of the boy.
And the daughter of Pharaoh said to her, “Behold, this boy is yours; nurse him for me and I will give you your wages.” And the woman took the boy and she nursed him.
And the boy grew, and she brought him to the daughter of Pharaoh and he was a son to her, and she called his name Moshe (aka Moses), because she said, “I took him from the waters.”

And it was in those days and Moshe grew and he went out to his brethren and he saw their oppression, and he saw an Egyptian man when he struck a Hebrew man, one of his brethren of the children of Israel.
And he turned here and there and he saw that there was no man, and he killed the Egyptian, and he buried him in the sand.
And he went out the next day and he saw, and behold, two Hebrew men arguing and he said to the wrongdoer, “Why do you strike your companion?”

And he said to him, “Who made you a great man and a Judge over us? Do you intend to kill me as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?” And Moshe was afraid and he said, “Truly, the matter has been made known.”
And Pharaoh heard this account and he wanted to kill Moshe, and Moshe fled from before Pharaoh, and he went on to the land of Midian and sat down on a well.
And the Priest of Midian had seven daughters and they were coming and drawing out water and filled the water trough to water the flocks of their father.
And Shepherds were coming and chasing them away, and Moshe arose and he saved them and he gave drink to their flocks.
And they came to Ruil their father, and he asked and said to them, “How did you water so quickly today?”
They said to him, an Egyptian man saved us from the hands of the Shepherds, also he drew out to draw out for us and he gave drink to our sheep.”
And he said to his daughters, “Where is he? Why have you let the man go? Go invite him and we shall eat bread.”
And Moshe agreed to sit with the man, and he gave him Tsepura his daughter as a wife.

And she bore a son and he called his name Gershon, because he said, “I was a stranger in an alien land”, and she bore again a second son to Moshe, and he called his name, Eliazer, because “The God of my fathers is for my help and he delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh.”
And many days passed and the King of Egypt died and the children of Israel groaned from the hard oppression, and they prayed, and their cry came up to God from the oppression.
And God heard their groans and God remembered his covenant with Abraham and with Isaaq and with Yaquuv.
And God saw the children of Israel, and God indicated it.
And Moshe was feeding the flock of Yatherwan his father in Law, Priest of Midian, and he led the flock to the wilderness and came to the mountain of God at Khurib.
And the Angel of LORD JEHOVAH appeared to him in a flame of fire from within the bush, and he saw the bush that was on fire with fire in it, and the bush was not burned.
And Moshe said, “I shall turn aside, I shall see this great sight, why the bush does not burn.”
And LORD JEHOVAH saw that he turned aside to see, and God called to him from within the bush, and he said, “Moshe, Moshe”, and he said, “Here I am.”
And he said to him, “Do not come near here. Take off your shoes from your feet, because the place where you are standing is holy.”
And he said, “I AM the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, the God of Yaquuv”, and Moshe hid his face because he was afraid to gaze on the Presence of God.
And LORD JEHOVAH said: “Looking, I have seen the oppression of my people who are in Egypt and I have heard their groans from before their oppressors, because I know their suffering;

And I have come down for its salvation from the hand of the Egyptians and to bring them up from this land to a land that is enlarged and good, to a land that flows milk and honey, to the land of Canaanites and of Khethites and of Amorites and Perizites and of Khivites and of Yebusites.
And now, behold, the cries of the children of Israel have entered my Presence, also I have seen the suffering that the Egyptians are inflicting on them.
Now come, I shall send you to Pharaoh and I shall bring out my people, the children of Israel, from Egypt.”
And Moshe said to God, “Who am I that I should go to Pharaoh and bring out the house of Israel from Egypt?”
And God said to him, “I shall be with you, and this is the sign that I have sent you; when I shall bring out the people from Egypt, you will serve before God on this mountain.”

And Moshe said to God, “Behold, I shall go to the children of Israel and I shall say to them, LORD JEHOVAH, God of your fathers has sent me unto you”, and they will say to me, ‘What is his name?’ What shall I say to them?”
And God said to Moshe: Ahiah Ashra High“I AM what I AM”, and he said, thus say to the children of Israel: Ahiah – “I AM” has sent me unto you.”

And God said again to Moshe: “In this way speak to the children of Israel: ‘LORD JEHOVAH, the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, the God of Yaquuv has sent me unto you’; this is my Name to eternity and this is my memorial to a generation of generations.

Go gather the Elders of the children of Israel and say to them: ‘LORD JEHOVAH the God of your fathers was revealed to me, the God of Abraham and of Ishaq and of Yaquuv, saying, “Remembering, I have remembered you and whatever was done to you in Egypt

And I said, ‘I shall bring you from the oppression of the Egyptians to the land of Canaanites and of the Khethites and of the Amorites and of Perezites and of Khivites and of Yebusites, to the land that flows milk and honey.'

And they shall hear your voice and you shall enter, you and the Elders of the children of Israel, unto the King of Egypt, and you shall say to him, ‘LORD JEHOVAH God of the Hebrews was revealed to us; now let us go a journey of three days in the wilderness and we will sacrifice to LORD JEHOVAH our God.’
And I know that the King of Egypt will not allow you to go, and it is not in his power.
And I shall reach my hands and I shall strike the Egyptians to do all wonders among them, and after that they shall send you away.
And I shall give the people favor in the eyes of the Egyptians, and when you go, you shall not go with nothing:
A woman shall ask for a vessel of gold and a vessel of silver and garments from her neighbor and from the foreign woman in her house, and clothe your sons and your daughters and empty the Egyptians.”
And Moshe answered and said, “Behold, they will not believe me and will not hear my voice, because they will say, ‘LORD JEHOVAH has not appeared to you.’”
And LORD JEHOVAH said to him, “What is this that is in your hand?”, and he said, “A staff.”
And he said, “Throw it on the ground”, and he cast it on the ground, and it was a snake and Moshe fled from before it.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “Reach your hand and hold it by its tail”, and he reached his hand and held firmly on it and it was a staff in his hand:
“So that they will believe that LORD JEHOVAH appeared to you, the God of their fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, the God of Yaquuv.”
And LORD JEHOVAH said to him again, “Bring your hand to your chest”, and he brought his hand to his chest, and brought it out, and behold, his hand was leprous as snow.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to him, “Return your hand to your chest, and he returned his hand to his bosom and brought it out from his bosom and it was to him as his flesh.
And if they will not believe and they will not hear the voice of the former sign, they may believe the voice of the last sign.

And if they should not believe these two signs and they will not hear your voice, take some water of the river and pour it on the land, and those waters that you took from the river shall be blood on the ground.”

And Moshe said to LORD JEHOVAH, “I beg of you my Lord, I have not been a man who is articulate, even from yesterday or from the day before yesterday, also from the time that you have spoken with your Servant, because I am a stutterer in speaking and slow of tongue.”
And LORD JEHOVAH said to him, “Who made the mouth of man, or who made the dumb man, or the deaf man, or the opened, or the blind? Is it not I, LORD JEHOVAH?
And now go, and I shall be with your mouth and I shall teach you whatever you will say.”
And Moshe said, “I beg of you, my Lord, send by whomever you send.

And the anger of LORD JEHOVAH was provoked against Moshe, and he said to him, “Behold, Aaron your brother is a Levite; I know that he is a speaker; behold also he will come forth to meet you and he will see you and he will rejoice in his heart.
And you will speak to him and I shall put the answer in his mouth, and I shall be with your mouth and with his mouth, and I shall teach you what you shall do.
He shall speak for you with the people and he shall be an interpreter for you and you shall be for his God to him.
And take this staff in your hand to do signs with it.”

And Moshe returned and he went on to Yatherwan his Father in Law and said to him, “I shall return, I shall go to my brothers who are in Egypt, and I shall see if they are yet alive”, and Yatherwan said to Moshe, “Go in peace.”
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe in Midian, “Return; go to Egypt, because all of the men who were seeking your life have died.”
And Moshe took his wife and his children and mounted them on donkeys, and returned to go to Egypt, and Moshe took the staff of God in his hand.

And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “When you have turned to go to Egypt, see all the wonders that I have done by your hands; do them before Pharaoh, and I shall harden his heart and he will not send away the people.
And speak to Pharaoh in this way: LORD JEHOVAH says: “My son, my firstborn, is Israel:
I have said to you ‘Send out my son and he will serve me,’ and if you choose not to send out my son, behold, I am killing your son, your firstborn.”
And Moshe was in the road at the lodging house, and LORD JEHOVAH met him and sought to kill Moshe.
And Tsepura took a flint and circumcised the uncircumcision of her son and gripped on his feet, and she said “A groom of blood you are to me.”
And he departed from him, then she said, “A groom of blood”, because of the circumcision.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Aaron, “Go for a meeting of Moshe your brother”, and he went on to the wilderness and met him in the mountain of God in Khoreeb, and he kissed Him.
And Moshe showed to Aaron all the statements of LORD JEHOVAH who sent him and all the signs that He commanded him to do.
And they went, Moshe and Aaron, and they gathered all the Elders of the children of Israel,
And Aaron spoke to them all the words that LORD JEHOVAH told to Moshe, and he did the signs before the people.
And the people believed and heard that LORD JEHOVAH had remembered the children of Israel, and that He has seen their oppression, and the people knelt and worshipped before LORD JEHOVAH.
And after that, Moshe and Aaron entered and they said to Pharaoh, “Thus says LORD JEHOVAH the God of Israel: ‘Send my people out, and they will keep a feast to me in the wilderness.’”
And Pharaoh said, “Who is LORD JEHOVAH, that I should listen to his voice to send away Israel? I do not know LORD JEHOVAH, neither am I sending out Israel.”

And they said, “LORD JEHOVAH, God of the Hebrews, has been revealed unto us; let us go a journey of three days in the wilderness, and we will sacrifice to LORD JEHOVAH our God, lest he will attack us with a sword or with death.”
And the King of Egypt said to them, “Why do you, Moshe and Aaron, stop the people from their works? Go to your work.”
And Pharaoh said to them, “Behold, now the people of the land are many, and you stop them from their works.”
And Pharaoh commanded the Rulers of the people and their Scribes on that day, and he said to them.
“You may not add to give straw to the people to cast bricks as it has been done and like they were doing; they shall depart and they will collect straw for themselves.

And the number of the bricks that they were making as it was fulfilled, even so it is to be fulfilled; you will put it upon them, and you will not reduce them, because they are idle; because of this they complain and they are saying, ‘Let us go, we will sacrifice to our God.’
You will increase the work on the men and they will attend to it and they will not attend to empty words.”
And the Rulers of the people and its Scribes went out and they said to the people, “Thus says Pharaoh, ‘I am not giving you straw.
You go take yourselves straw from where you can and let not a thing be reduced from your work.’”
And the people were scattered in all the land of Egypt to collect dry grass of straw.
And the Rulers were pressing hard and they were saying, “Complete your work as always like when straw was given to you.”

And the Scribes of the children of Israel were chastised, whom the Rulers of Pharaoh had set over them, and they were saying, “Why have you not handed over the number of bricks as they were completed and as were being completed even yesterday and today?”
And the Scribes of the children of Israel came and they complained before Pharaoh, and they were saying, “Why is this done so to your Servants?
Straw is not given to your Servants, and they were saying to us, ‘Cast bricks’, and behold, your Servants are beaten and you sin against your people.”
And Pharaoh said to them, “Idle, you are idle, because of this you are saying, ‘Let us go, we will sacrifice to LORD JEHOVAH.’
Now go and work, and straw will not be given to you and you shall give the number of the bricks.”
And the Scribes saw that things went badly for the children of Israel and they were saying to them, “Do not reduce from your building, but continue as on every day before.”
And they met with Moshe and with Aaron when standing opposite them as they went out from before Pharaoh:

And they said to them, “May LORD JEHOVAH look upon you and judge that you have made our spirit evil in the eyes of Pharaoh, and in the eyes of his Servants, to make us as a sword in their hands to destroy us.”
And Moshe returned to LORD JEHOVAH and said, “My Lord, why have you done evil to this people and why have you sent me?
For from the hour I came to Pharaoh to speak in your name, evil comes to this people, and you surely have not delivered your people.”
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “Now you shall see what I shall do to Pharaoh, because with a mighty hand he will send them out and by an high arm he will bring them out from his land.”
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and he said to him, “I AM LORD JEHOVAH,
Who was revealed to Abraham and to Isaac and to Yaquuv, by Eil Shaddai God, and the Name of LORD JEHOVAH I had not shown them.
And also I have established my covenant with them to give to them the land of Canaan, the land of their pilgrimage where they dwell.
Also I heard the groaning of the children of Israel by the Egyptians subjecting them, and I was reminded of my covenant.

Because of this, say to the children of Israel, ‘I AM LORD JEHOVAH your God and I bring you out from the bondage of the Egyptians and I deliver you from their labors and I shall save you by a mighty hand and by an high arm and by great judgment.
And I shall take you to me as a people and I shall be to you God, and you shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH your God who brings you out from the bondage of the Egyptians.
And I shall bring you to the land where I raised my hands to give it to Abraham and to Isaac and to Yaquuv, and I shall give it to you an inheritance. I AM LORD JEHOVAH.”
And Moshe said thus to the children of Israel, and they did not listen to Moshe, from sorrow of spirit and from hard bondage.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe saying,
“Go speak to Pharaoh, King of Egypt, and he shall send the children of Israel from his land.”
And Moshe said, “LORD JEHOVAH, behold, the children of Israel do not listen to me. How will Pharaoh listen to me, for my tongue stutters?”

And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and with Aaron and commanded them in the presence of the children of Israel and in the presence of Pharaoh the King of Egypt to bring out the children of Israel from the land of Egypt.
And these are the heads of the house their fathers, the children of Rubil, firstborn of Israel: Henok and Palu and Khetsron and Karmi; these are the generations of Rubil.
And the sons of Shemon: Yamuyel and Yamin and Ahar and Yakin and Tsakhar and Shawal son of a Canaanitess; these are the generations of Shemon.
And these are the names of the sons of Levi for their generations: Gershon and Qahath and Merari; and the years of the life of Levi, a hundred and thirty and seven years.
And the sons of Gershon to the sons of Shemi in their generations.
And the children of Qahath: Amram and Itshar and Khebron and Uzyel, and the years of the life of Qahath were a hundred and thirty and three years.
And the children of Merari: Makhli and Mushi; these are the generations of Levi by their generations.
And Amram took a woman, Yokabar, daughter of his paternal uncle, and she bore to him Aaron and Maryam and Moshe, and the years of life of Amram were a hundred and thirty and seven years.
And the children of Itshar: Qurakh and Nephag and Zakri.
And the children of Uzil: Manshayel and Elitsphan and Sathri.
And Aaron took Elishba, daughter of Aminadab, the sister of Nakhshon, as wife, and she bore Nadab and Abihu and Eliazar and Ithamar.
And the children of Qorakh: Asir and Halqana and Akinsaph; these are the generations of Qorakh.
And Eliazar, son of Aaron, took to him a wife from the daughters of Phantiel, and she bore to him Phinkhes; these are the heads of genealogies of Levi with their generations.
This is the Moshe and Aaron to whom LORD JEHOVAH said, “Bring the children of Israel from the land of Egypt, all their hosts.”
Those said to Pharaoh, the King of Egypt, to send out the children of Israel from Egypt, Moshe and Aaron.
And it was in the day that LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe in the land of Egypt,
LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him, “I AM LORD JEHOVAH; say to Pharaoh the King of Egypt everything that I said to you.”
And Moshe said to LORD JEHOVAH, “My tongue stutters; how will Pharaoh listen to me?”
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “See that I have given you as a god to Pharaoh, and Aaron your brother shall be your Prophet.
You shall say everything that I command you, and Aaron your brother will speak to Pharaoh and he will send the children of Israel from his land.
And I shall harden the heart of Pharaoh and I shall multiply my signs and my wonders in the land of Egypt.
And Pharaoh will not listen to you and I shall give my hand in Egypt and I shall bring out my armies and my people, the children of Israel, from the land of Egypt in great judgment.
And the Egyptians shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH, whenever I have raised my hand against the Egyptians and I shall bring out the children of Israel from among them.
And Moshe and Aaron did as LORD JEHOVAH commanded them; in this way they did.
And Moshe was a son of eighty years, and Aaron was a son of eighty and three years when they spoke with Pharaoh.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe and to Aaron:
“If Pharaoh says to you, ‘Give me a sign’, say to Aaron, ‘Take your staff and cast before Pharaoh and it will be a dragonsea serpent.’”
And Moshe and Aaron came to Pharaoh and did in this way just as LORD JEHOVAH commanded them, and Aaron cast his staff before Pharaoh and before his Nobles, and it became a dragonsea serpent.
And Pharaoh called the wise men and sorcerers, and also those sorcerers of Egypt did likewise by their sorceries.
And the men cast, each one, his staff, before Pharaoh and they became dragonssea serpents, and the scepter of Aaron devoured their staves.
And the heart of Pharaoh was hardened and he did not send them away, just as LORD JEHOVAH said.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “The heart of Pharaoh is hardened and he does not want to send the people out.
Go to Pharaoh at dawn; behold, he will go out to the water; stand opposite him on the shore of the river and take the staff that became a serpent in your hand.
And say to him: ‘LORD JEHOVAH, God of the Hebrews, has sent Me to you, and he said: “Send out my people; they will serve me in the wilderness, and behold, until now you have not listened.

Thus says LORD JEHOVAH: “In this you will know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH; behold, I strike with the rod that is in my hand upon the waters that are in the river and they shall be turned and they shall become blood.
And the fish that are in the river will die and the river will stink and the Egyptians will loathe to drink waters from the river.’”

And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “Say to Aaron, ‘Take your staff and lift your hand over the waters of the Egyptians, over their rivers and over their reservoirs and over their pools and over the ponds of their waters and they shall be blood, and blood will be in all the land of Egypt, in the wood and in the stone.’”

And in this way Moshe and Aaron did just as LORD JEHOVAH commanded them, and Aaron lifted his staff and struck the water that was in the river before the eyes of Pharaoh and before the eyes of his Servants, and all of the waters that were in the river were blood for them.
And the fish that were in the river died, and the river stank, and the Egyptians did not find water to drink from the river, and it was blood in all the land of Egypt.
And thus the Sorcerers of Egypt did by their sorceries and the heart of Pharaoh was made dense and he did not hear them, just as LORD JEHOVAH had said.
And Pharaoh returned and he entered his house and he did not take this to his heart.
And all the Egyptians dug around the river to drink water, because they were not able to drink from the waters of the river.
And seven days were completed after LORD JEHOVAH struck the river.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “Go to Pharaoh and say to him, thus says LORD JEHOVAH: ‘Send out my people and they will serve me.
And if you choose not to send them out, behold I shall strike your entire border with frogs:

And the river shall crawl with frogs, and they shall come up and they shall enter your house, your bedroom and the place of your couch, your bed and in the house of your Servants and with your people and in your inner rooms and in your kneading troughs:
And among you and among all your people frogs will come up.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “Say to Aaron your brother: ‘Lift your hand with your staff over the rivers and over the hills and over the pools and bring up the frogs on the land of Egypt.’”
And Aaron lifted his hand over the waters of the Egyptians and the frogs came up and covered the land of Egypt.
And also the Sorcerers did likewise in their enchantments and they brought up frogs on the land of Egypt.

And Pharaoh called for Moshe and for Aaron and he said to them, “Pray before LORD JEHOVAH, and he will remove the frogs from me and from my people and I shall send out the people and they will sacrifice to LORD JEHOVAH.”
And Moshe said to Pharaoh: “Ask for yourself a time when I shall pray for you and for your Servants and for your people, and he will remove the frogs from you and from your house.”
And he said to him, “Tomorrow”, and he said to him, “According to your word, so that you may know there is none like LORD JEHOVAH our God.
And he will take away the frogs from you and from your house and from your Servants and from your people; only in the river shall they crawl.”
And Moshe and Aaron went out from the presence of Pharaoh, and Moshe prayed before LORD JEHOVAH because of the frogs that he had brought on Pharaoh.
And LORD JEHOVAH did according to the word of Moshe and the frogs died from the houses and from the enclosures and from the fields.
And they gathered them in heaps by heaps and the land stank.
And Pharaoh saw that relief came and he hardened his heart and did not listen to them, according to what LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “Say to Aaron, “Lift your staff and strike the dust of the ground, and lice will be in all the land of Egypt.”

And he did so, and Aaron lifted his hand with his staff and he struck the dust of the ground and there were lice on the children of men and on the animals; all the dust of the land became itself lice in all the land of Egypt.
And the Sorcerers did in this way also by their sorceries and they were not capable of removal of the lice, and there were lice on the children of men and on the animals.
And the Sorcerers said to Pharaoh, “It is the finger of God”, and the heart of Pharaoh was hardened, and he did not listen to them, just as LORD JEHOVAH had said.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “Arise at dawn and stand before Pharaoh; behold, he will go out to the water, and say to him: “Thus says LORD JEHOVAH, ‘Send out my people and they will serve me.

And if you choose not to send out my people, behold, I am sending upon you and upon your people and upon your houses swarms of insects of every kind and the houses of the Egyptians will be filled with the swarms, as the land of those upon it.
And I shall separate in that day the land of Gashan of my people who dwell in it, that swarms of insects shall not be there, so that you may know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH within the Earth.
And I shall make a distinction among my people and your people, and this sign will occur tomorrow.”
And LORD JEHOVAH did so and he brought powerful insect swarms to the house of Pharaoh and to the house of his Servants and in all the land of Egypt, and the land was destroyed because of the swarms.
And Pharaoh called Moshe and Aaron and said to them, “Go sacrifice before LORD JEHOVAH your God in the land.”

And Moshe said, “It is not good to do so, because we sacrifice that which is the abomination of the Egyptians to LORD JEHOVAH, and if we slaughter the worshipped creatures of Egyptians in their sight, they will stone us.
Let us go a journey of three days into the wilderness and we will sacrifice to LORD JEHOVAH our God, as he said to us.”
And Pharaoh said, “I shall send you and you will sacrifice to LORD JEHOVAH your God in the wilderness, only you shall not go far, and pray also for me.”

And Moshe said to Pharaoh, “Behold, I go out from your presence and I shall pray before LORD JEHOVAH and he will remove the swarms from Pharaoh and from his Servants and from his people tomorrow, only do not let Pharaoh continue to deceive without sending out the people, and we will sacrifice to LORD JEHOVAH.”
And Moshe went out from the presence of Pharaoh and he prayed before LORD JEHOVAH.
And LORD JEHOVAH did according to the word of Moshe, and he removed the swarms from Pharaoh and from his Servants and from his people, and not even one was left.
And Pharaoh hardened his heart also at this time and he did not send the people away.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “Go to Pharaoh and say to him, ‘Thus says LORD JEHOVAH, God of the Hebrews, “Send out my people and they will serve me.
And if you choose not to send away, and until now you are holding onto them,

Behold, the hand of LORD JEHOVAH will be on your cattle that are in the wilderness, on the horses, and on the donkeys, and on the camels, and on the oxen, and on the sheep, a plague that is very severe.
And LORD JEHOVAH will separate between the animals of Israel and the animals of the Egyptians, and no animal of the house of Israel will die, not even one.’”
And LORD JEHOVAH gave the time that LORD JEHOVAH would make this response in the land: “Tomorrow.”
And LORD JEHOVAH made this response the day after it, and all beasts of the Egyptians died, and of the beasts of the house of Israel, not even one died.
And Pharaoh sent and he saw that not even one of the beasts of Israel died, and the heart of Pharaoh was hardened and he did not send the people out.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe and Aaron: “Make full your handfuls of the ashes of a furnace, and let Moshe scatter them before the face of Heaven, before the eyes of Pharaoh.
And there will be dust in all the land of Egypt and there will be on the children of men and upon beast, abscesses of blisters that multiply in all the land of Egypt.”

And they took the ash of a furnace and they stood before Pharaoh, and Moshe scattered it toward the sky before the eyes of Pharaoh and there were abscesses of boils, and it flew among the children of men and among beasts.
And the Sorcerers were unable to stand before Moshe because of the abscesses that multiplied among the Sorcerers and in all Egypt.
And LORD JEHOVAH hardened the heart of Pharaoh and he did not listen to them, as LORD JEHOVAH had said to Moshe.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “Arise at dawn and stand before Pharaoh and say to him, “Thus says LORD JEHOVAH, God of the Hebrews: ‘Send out my people and they will serve me.
Because at this time I am sending my plague on your heart and upon your Servants and upon your people, so that you may know there is none like me in all the Earth.
Because now I stretch out my hands and I strike you and your people with slaughter and you will be destroyed from the Earth.
And moreover it is for this that I raised you up so that I shall show you my power and so that My Name will be proclaimed in all the Earth.
And until now you hold onto the people and you do not desire to send them away.
Tomorrow at this time I shall bring very strong hail that is unlike any in Egypt from the day that it was built even until now.

And now send and gather your animals and everything that you have in the wilderness, every son of man and the animals that are found in the field, and upon those not assembled within a house the hail will fall and they will die.
He who was in awe of the word of LORD JEHOVAH from the Servants of Pharaoh gathered his Servants and his animals into the house:
And he who did not take the word of LORD JEHOVAH to his heart left his Servants and his animals in the field.

And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “Lift your hand before the face of Heaven and there will be hail in all the land of Egypt, on children of men and upon beast and upon all grass that is in the field that is in the land of Egypt.”
And Moshe lifted his staff before the face of Heaven, and LORD JEHOVAH gave a noise, and hail and fire was walking on the ground, and LORD JEHOVAH brought down the hail on the land of Egypt.
And hail was coming down, and flaming fire was inside the hail; it was very severe, the likes of which there had not been in all the land of Egypt from the day that it became a nation.

And the hail beat on all the land of Egypt, all that is in the wilderness, from children of men and unto beast, and the hail destroyed all grass of the field and it broke all trees that were in the field.
Only in the land of Gashan where the children of Israel were dwelling did the hail not fall.
And Pharaoh sent and he called Moshe and Aaron and said to them, “I have sinned this time. LORD JEHOVAH is righteous and I and my people are wicked.
Pray before LORD JEHOVAH and there is much room before him that there will not be the voice of God and hail, and I shall send you out and you will not stay any longer!”

And Moshe said to him, “When I go out from the city, I shall stretch out my hands to LORD JEHOVAH; the noises shall depart and hail will fall no more, so that you may know that the Earth is LORD JEHOVAH’s.
And I know you and your Servants, that until now you do not worship before LORD JEHOVAH God.”
And the flax and barley were destroyed because the barley root was struck and the flax was bent.
And the wheat and the rye were not destroyed because they were late growing.
And Moshe went out from the city from the presence of Pharaoh, and he spread his hands to Heaven to LORD JEHOVAH and the noise departed, and hail and rain did not beat on the ground.
And Pharaoh saw that the rain and hail and the noise departed, and he continued to sin, and the heart of Pharaoh and of his Servants was hardened.
And the heart of Pharaoh was hardened and he did not send out the children of Israel, just as what LORD JEHOVAH sent by the hand of Moshe.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “Go to Pharaoh, because I have hardened his heart and the heart of his servants so that I would do these signs among them:
And so that you would narrate before your son and the son of your son what I have done in Egypt and the signs that I have done among them, and you shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH.”
And Moshe and Aaron came to Pharaoh and they said to him, “Thus says LORD JEHOVAH, God of the Hebrews, ‘How long will you choose not to fear me? Send my people out and they will serve me.
And if you choose not to send my people out, behold, tomorrow I shall bring the locust upon your entire border:

And it will cover the face of the land, and a son of man will be unable to see the land and it will eat the rest of anything that the hail has left for you and it will eat of all of the trees, those that have budded to you in the field:

And your houses will be filled and the houses of your servants and the houses of all Egyptians which your fathers and the fathers of your fathers did not see in this way, from the day that they have been on the Earth and until today’”, and they turned and they went out from before Pharaoh.
And his Servants said to Pharaoh, “How long will we have this scandal? Send away the men and they shall serve LORD JEHOVAH their God. Do you not know that you have destroyed Egypt?”
And they returned Moshe and Aaron to Pharaoh, and he said to them, “Go serve before LORD JEHOVAH your God, but who and what are going?”
Moshe said to him, “With our young and with our old, we shall go; with our sons and with our daughters, with our sheep and with our oxen we will go, because it is a feast of LORD JEHOVAH for us.”
And he said to them, “LORD JEHOVAH shall be with you in this way when I have sent you and your little ones, but see that there is no harm against your persons.
Not in this way; go now you men and serve before LORD JEHOVAH, because it is rest that you seek”, and they took them out from before the presence of Pharaoh.

And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “Lift your hand over the land of Egypt for the locust and it will come up over the land of Egypt, and it will eat the grass of the land and everything that the hail has left.”
And Moshe lifted his staff over the land of Egypt and LORD JEHOVAH brought out the wind of dry heat over the land all that day and all night. Dawn came and the wind of dry heat carried the locust.

And the locust came up on all the land of Egypt and it destroyed within the entire border of the Egyptians; it was very severe and there was not a locust plague like it before it, neither shall there be one like it after it.

And it covered the face of all the land and the land grew dark and it ate all the grass of the land and all fruit of the trees that the hail had left and it did not leave a leaf in a tree, neither grass in the field in all the land of Egypt.
And Pharaoh hurried to call Moshe and Aaron and he said to them, “I have sinned against LORD JEHOVAH your God and against you.
Also now forgive my injury this time and pray before LORD JEHOVAH your God and he will remove from me this death.
And Moshe went out from before Pharaoh and he prayed before LORD JEHOVAH.

And LORD JEHOVAH turned and he brought the west wind that was very strong and it carried the locust and cast it into the sea of Reeds, and there was not left even one locust in all the border of the Egyptians.
And LORD JEHOVAH hardened the heart of Pharaoh and he did not send out the children of Israel.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “Lift your hand to Heaven and there will be darkness over all the land of Egypt, and it will darken darkness.
And Moshe lifted his hand to Heaven and there was darkness and thick darkness in all the land of Egypt three days:
No man saw his brother, and no man arose from his place for three days, and for all the children of Israel there was light among their dwellings.
And Pharaoh called Moshe and said to him, “Go serve before LORD JEHOVAH, only leave your flocks and your oxen, and also your little ones will go with you.”
Moshe said to him: “Also you shall give into our hand sacrifices and offerings and we will sacrifice to LORD JEHOVAH our God.

Also our beast will go with us and there will not remain of us even one hoof here, because we are taking it to serve before LORD JEHOVAH our God, and we do not know what we will serve before LORD JEHOVAH when we arrive there.”
And LORD JEHOVAH hardened the heart of Pharaoh and he did not want to send them away.
And Pharaoh said to Moshe, “Go from my presence; beware that you do not again appear before me, because in the day that you appear to me, you will die!”
Moshe said to him, “You have said well; I shall not see your face again.”

And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “I shall bring one more plague on Pharaoh and upon the Egyptians, and then I shall send you out from here, and when I have sent you out, all of you get yourselves out of here.
And say before the people that they shall ask, each man from his neighbor, and a woman from her neighbor, an article of silver and an article of gold.”

And LORD JEHOVAH gave favor to the people in the eyes of the Egyptians, also the man Moshe was highly honored in the land of Egypt, in the eyes of the Servants of Pharaoh and in the eyes of the people.
