I AM LORD JEHOVAH your God who have brought you out from the land of Egypt that you would not be slaves to them and I have broken the yoke of your bondage and your steps are erect in stature.
But if you will not listen to me and you will not do all of these commandments;
If you will reject my Law and your souls will loathe my judgments and you will not do all of my commands and you shall nullify my covenant:

Also I in this way I shall do to you: I shall order upon you shock and leprosy and scabs and a discharge that destroys the eyes and decays the life and you will sow your seed for nothing and your enemies shall eat it.
And I shall put my wrath in you and you shall be broken before your enemies and your haters shall rule over you and you shall escape and you shall flee when there is none who chases you.
And if in these things you will not listen to me again, I shall continue to chastise you each sevenfold for your sins.
And I shall break the magnificence of your strength and I shall make your skies like iron and your ground like brass:
And your power shall be spent in futility and your land shall not give its harvests and the trees of the land shall not yield their fruit.
And if you will walk with me contentiously and you will not be willing to listen to me, I shall add upon you plagues, each by sevens, for your sins.
And I shall send upon you beasts of the field and they shall bereave you and shall destroy your domestic animals and they shall diminish you and your roads shall be desolate.
And if in these things, you again will not be instructed, and you will walk with me contentiously;
I shall walk also with you contentiously and I shall strike you also, each sevenfold for your sins.

And I shall bring upon you a sword to avenge with the vengeance of the covenant, and you shall flee to your cities and I shall send among you a plague, and you shall be given into the hand of your enemies.
And I shall break the stalk of your grain and ten women shall bake your bread in one oven and they shall give to you bread by weight, and you shall eat and you shall not be satisfied.
And if in these things you again will not listen to me and you will walk with me contentiously;
Also I shall walk with you in anger and in contention and I shall chasten you each sevenfold for your sins.
And you shall eat the flesh of your sons and you will eat the flesh of your daughters.
And I shall destroy your shrines and I shall destroy your idols and I shall cast your corpses on the bodies of your idols and my soul shall detest you.
And I shall make your cities into a wilderness and I shall lay waste your sanctuaries and I shall not smell the fragrance of your savors.
And I shall destroy also the land and your enemies who dwell in it shall be astonished over it.
And I shall scatter you among the nations and I shall unsheathe a sword after you, and destruction shall possess your land and your cities shall be a wilderness.
And then the land will be pleased in its Sabbaths all of the days of its desolation, when you are in the land of your enemies, and then the land shall rest and it shall be pleased in its Sabbaths.
All of the days of its desolation it shall rest because it did not rest on your Sabbaths when you were dwelling in it.

And into the hearts of those who are left among you I shall cast trepidation, in the lands of their enemies, and the sound of leaves stirring will chase them, and they will flee as escaping from before the sword, and they will fall when there is no one chasing them.
And a man shall be stumbled by his brother as one before a sword when there is no one chasing them, and there will be no standing before their enemies.
And you shall be destroyed among the nations and the land of your enemies shall consume you.
And those who remain among you will putrefy in their evil in the land of their enemies, also they will rot in the evil of their fathers.
And they shall confess their evil and the evil of their fathers in the evil that they have done to me; and because they walked with me in contention;
Also I shall walk with them in contention, and I shall bring them to the land of their enemies, and then their uncircumcised heart will be broken and then they will acknowledge their evil:
And I shall remember my covenant with Yaquuv and my covenant with Isaaq and I shall remember my covenant with Abraham and I shall remember the land.

And the land shall be forsaken of them and it shall be pleased in its Sabbaths whenever it is deserted by them, and they shall acknowledge their evil because they rejected my judgment and their souls loathed my commands.
Also when they were in the land of their enemies I did not despise them and I have not cast them off and I have not destroyed them and I have not discontinued my covenant with them.

But I have remembered for them the first covenant because I AM LORD JEHOVAH their God who brought them out from the land of Egypt before the eyes of the nations, and I have been their God; I AM LORD JEHOVAH."
These are the commandments and the laws and the judgments which LORD JEHOVAH gave between him and the children of Israel into the hand of Moshe in the mountain of Sinai.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
“Speak with the children of Israel and say to them: ‘When a man dedicates a vow with the price of persons to LORD JEHOVAH.
The price of a male from a son of twenty years and above and unto a son of sixty years shall be the price of fifty shekels of silver in the shekel of Holiness.
And if it is a female, her price shall be thirty shekels.
And if from a son of five years and unto a son of twenty years, the price of males shall be twenty shekels.
And of the females, ten shekels, and if from a son of a month and unto a son of five years the price of males shall be five shekels of silver, and of females, three shekels of silver.
And if it is a male from a son of sixty years and above, the price shall be fifteen shekels, and of the female, ten shekels.

And if he is poorer than the price, he shall present himself before the Priest and the Priest will cut down the price according to what is enough in the hands of him who vows a vow; in this way the Priest will cut down the price.
And if it is a beast from which they bring a gift to LORD JEHOVAH, all that he gives of it to LORD JEHOVAH shall be holy.
He shall not exchange a good one for a bad one, or an afflicted one for good one, and if he will exchange a beast to be exchanged for a beast, it and its exchange shall be holy to LORD JEHOVAH.
And if a beast is unclean that they do not accept from it as an offering to LORD JEHOVAH, he shall present the animal before the Priest:
And the Priest will cut its price between good and bad, and according to that price to which the Priest cut, so it shall be.
And if he will certainly redeem it, he shall add one fifth to its price.
And if a man will hallow his household as holiness to LORD JEHOVAH, the Priest will cut its price between the good and the bad, and according to that price to which the Priest cut, so it shall stand.
And if he who hallows will repurchase his household, he will add one fifth the money of its price to it and it will be his.
And if a man will hallow from the field of his inheritance to LORD JEHOVAH, its price shall be in proportion to his seed among the seed of a Cor eleven bushels of barley, with fifty shekels of money.
And if from the year of restoration he will hallow his field according to its price, thus it shall stand.

And if after the restoration he will hallow the field, the Priest will account to him the money in proportion to the years that are left until the year of restoration, and he shall deduct from its price.
And if he will surely redeem that field from him who hallows it, he will add onefifth the money of the proceeds for it and it shall be his.
And if he will not repurchase the field and will sell it to another man again, it shall not be redeemed.
But the field, when it will go out in the restoration, will be holy to LORD JEHOVAH as a field that is devoted; it will be the inheritance of the Priest.
And if the field is of a purchase and was not the field of an inheritance a man hallows to LORD JEHOVAH;
The Priest will figure its price until the year of restoration and he will give its price in that day, holy to LORD JEHOVAH.
And in the year of restoration, the field shall be returned to him who had sold it, whose inheritance of land it is.
And its whole price shall be in shekels; twenty farthings is a shekel.
However the first born which is first born to LORD JEHOVAH from an animal, a man shall not hallow; if it is an ox or a sheep, it is LORD JEHOVAH’s.
And if a beast is unclean, it will be bought with a price and will add one fifth to it and if it is not repurchased, it shall be sold with a price.

However every devoted thing that a man devoted to LORD JEHOVAH from everything that he has, of men and of beast and from the field of his inheritance, will not be sold and will not be redeemed; every devoted thing is Holy of Holy things to LORD JEHOVAH.
And no devoted thing devoted of men shall be repurchased but surely shall be killed.
And every tenth of the land of the seed of the land and of the fruit of the trees of LORD JEHOVAH, it is holiness to LORD JEHOVAH.
And if a man will redeem from his tithe, he will add to it one fifth.
And every tenth of oxen and of sheep, everything that passes under the staff that grazes shall be holy to LORD JEHOVAH.
He shall not inquire between good and bad and between bad and good and he shall not exchange it, and if he will exchange it, that and its exchange shall be holy and it shall not be repurchased.'"
These are the commandments that LORD JEHOVAH commanded Moshe concerning the children of Israel in the mountain of Sinai.

name: Numbers
chapters: And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe in the wilderness of Sinai in the Time Tabernacle, in the first day, in the second month, in the second year when the children of Israel went out from the land of Egypt, and he said to him:

“And take the count of the number of the leaders of all of the assembly of the children of Israel for their tribes to the house of their fathers in the number of their names, every male for their leaders.
From a son of twenty years and above, everyone who goes out in the army in Israel, you shall number them in their armies, you and Ahron your brother.
And each head man from the tribe of the house of his father shall be with you.
And these are the names of the men who will stand with you: of Rubil: Elitsur son of Shedaur.
Of Shemon: Shelamwayel, son of Shurishaday.
Of Yehuda: Nahshon, son of Aminadab.
Of Issakar: Nathaniel, son of Tsuar.
Of Zebulun: Elyab, son of Khelon.
Of the sons of Yoseph: of Aphreim: Elishma, son of Amihud of Menasheh: Gamaliel, son of Partsur.
Of Benyamin: Abidan, son of Geduni.
Of Dan: Akhiazar, son of Amishaday.
Of Ashayr: Pagayel, son of Akran.
Of Gad: Eliasaph, son of Rawayel.
Of Naphtali: Akhida, son of Aynan.
These are the prominent of the assembly, Princes of the tribes of the house of their fathers; they are the Heads of the thousands of Israel.”
And Moshe and Ahron took these men who were separated by names.

And they gathered all of the assembly on the first in the second month and they were numbered for their tribes with the house of their fathers in the number of the names of a son of twenty years and above for their Leaders.
As LORD JEHOVAH commanded Moshe and he numbered them in the wilderness of Sinai.

And there were the children of Rubil, the firstborn of Israel in their families, for their tribes, for the house of their fathers, in the number of the names, every male for their Leaders, from a son of twenty years and above, everyone who goes out in the army in Israel.
The number of them of the tribe of Rubil: forty and six thousand five hundred.

And the sons of Shemon in their families for their tribes, for the house of their fathers in the number of the names, every male for their Leaders, of a son of twenty years and above, everyone who goes out in the army in Israel.
The number of them of the tribe of Shemon: fifty and nine thousand and three hundred.

And the sons of Gad in their families, for their tribes for the house of their fathers, in the number of the names, every male for their Leaders, from a son of twenty years and above, everyone who proceeds in the army in Israel.
The number of them of the tribe of Gad: forty and five thousand and six hundred and fifty.
And the sons of Yehuda in their families, for their tribes for the house of their fathers, in the number of the names of a son of twenty years and above, everyone who goes out in the army in Israel.
The number of them of the tribe of Yehuda: seventy and four thousand and six hundred.
And the sons of Issakar in their families for their tribes for the house of their fathers, in the number of the names of a son of twenty years and above, everyone who goes out in the army in Israel.
The number of them of the tribe of Issakar: fifty and four thousand and four hundred.
And the sons of Zebulon in their families, for their tribes for the house of their fathers, in the number of the names of a son of twenty years and above, everyone who goes out in the army in Israel.
The number of them of the tribe of Zebulon: fifty and seven thousand and four hundred.

And the sons of Yoseph the sons of Aphreim in their families for their tribes to the house their fathers, in the number of the names of a son of twenty years and above, everyone who goes out in the army in Israel.
The number of them of the tribe of Aphreim: forty thousand and five hundred.

And the sons of Menasheh in their families for their tribes, for the house of their fathers, in the number of the names of a son of twenty years and above, everyone who goes out in the army in Israel.
The number of them of the tribe of Menasheh: thirty and two thousand and two hundred.
And the sons of Benyamin in their families for their tribes for the house of their fathers, in the number of the names of a son of twenty years and above, everyone who goes out in the army in Israel.
The number of them of the tribe of Benyamin: thirty and five thousand and four hundred.
And the sons of Dan in their families, for their tribes to the house of their fathers, in the number of the names of a son of twenty years and above, everyone who goes out in the army in Israel.
The number of them of the tribe of Dan: sixty and two thousand and seven hundred.
And the sons of Ashir in their families, for their tribes for the house of their fathers, in the number of the names of a son of twenty years and above, everyone who goes out in the army in Israel.
The number of them of the tribe of Ashayr: forty and one thousand and five hundred.
And the sons of Naphtali in their families, for their tribes for the house of their fathers, in the number of the names of a son of twenty years and above, every who goes out in the army in Israel.
The number of them of the tribe of Naphtali: fifty and three thousand and four hundred.
These are the numbers that Moshe and Ahron numbered and the Princes of Israel, twelve men, one man from the house of his father.
And all of them of the number of the sons of Israel for the house of their fathers, of a son of twenty years and above, everyone who goes out in the army in Israel.
And all of them the number of the sons of Israel were six hundred and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.
And the Levites and the tribe of their fathers were not numbered among them.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
The tribe of Levi you shall not number and their computation you shall not take among the sons of Israel.

But authorize Levi over the Tabernacle of the Testimony and over all of its implements and over everything that is in it, those shall carry his tabernacle and all its implements and those shall serve it who shall be camping around the Tabernacle.
And whenever the Tabernacle picks up, the Levites shall take it down, and whenever the Tabernacle sets up, the Levites shall erect it and a stranger who approaches it shall be killed.
And the sons of Israel shall pitch, each man in his camp and each man in his troop in their armies.

And Levites shall be setting up around the Tabernacle of the Testimony and there shall not be wrath on the assembly of the children of Israel and the Levites shall keep watch of the Tabernacle of the Testimony."
And the children of Israel did as LORD JEHOVAH commanded Moshe; in this way they did.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and with Ahron and said to them:
"A man shall camp in his troop in the places of the house of their fathers; the children of Israel shall dwell. opposite and around the Time Tabernacle.
And they who camp first from the East are the troop of the camp of Yehuda, their army and the Ruler of the sons of Yehuda: Nakhshon son of Aminadab.
And the number of his army: seventy and four thousand and six hundred.
And they who camp with the tribe of Issakar and the Ruler of the sons of Issakar: Nathaniel son of Tsuar.
And the number of his army: fifty and four thousand and four hundred.
And the rod of Zebulon and the Ruler of the sons of Zebulon: Eliab son of Khelon.
And the number of the army: fifty and seven thousand and four hundred.
All the number of the camp of Yehuda: a hundred and eighty and six thousand and four hundred for their armies; those shall be picking up first.
And the troop and the camp of Rubil from the south for their armies, and the Ruler of the sons of Rubil: Elitsur son of Shedur.
And the number of his army: forty and six thousand and five hundred.
And they who camp with the tribe of Shemon and the Ruler of the sons of Shemon: Shelemuil son of Tsurishadi.
And the number of his army: fifty and nine thousand and three hundred.
And the rod of Gad and the Ruler of the sons of Gad: Eliasaph son of Rawayel.
And the number of his army: forty and five thousand and six hundred and fifty.
All the number of the camp of Rubil: a hundred and fifty and one thousand and four hundred and fifty to their army; they were picking up after the first.
And the camp of Levites picked up the Time Tabernacle inside the camp; they were picking up just as they camped: each man with his troop for their armies.
And the troop of the camp of Aphreim for their armies from the West and the Leader for their armies of the children of Aphreim: Elishama son of Amihud.
And the number of the army: forty thousand and five hundred.
And they who camp with him are the tribe of Menasheh and the Ruler of the sons of Menasheh: Gamaliel son of Pertsur.
And the number of his army: thirty and two thousand and two hundred.
And the rod of Benyamin and the Leader of the sons of Benyamin: Abidan son of Gideoni.
And the number of his army: thirty and five thousand and four hundred.
All the number of the camp of Aphreim: a hundred and eighty thousand and one hundred for their army; they shall pick up after the second.
The troop of the camp of Dan from the north for his army and the Head of the sons of Dan: Akhiazar son of Amishdai.
And the number of his army: sixty and two thousand and seven hundred.
And they who camp with it are the tribe of Ashayr, and the Ruler of the sons of Ashayr: Pagayel son of Akran.
And the number of his army: forty and one thousand and five hundred.
And the rod of Naphtali and the Ruler of the sons of Naphtali: Akhida son of Ainan.
And the number of his army: fifty and three thousand and four hundred.
All the number of the camp of Dan: a hundred and fifty and seven thousand and six hundred; in the last troop they shall be picking up.
These are the numbers of the children of Israel for the house of their fathers; all of the numbers of the camp for their army: six hundred and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.
And the Levites and the rod of their fathers shall not be counted among the children of Israel as LORD JEHOVAH commanded Moshe."

And the children of Israel did everything that LORD JEHOVAH commanded Moshe, in this way they did, and thus they were camping in their troops, and thus they were camping, each man in his camp by the house of his father.
And these are the families of Ahron and of Moshe in the day when LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe in the mountain of Sinai.
And these are the names of the sons of Ahron the firstborn: Nadab and Abihu and Eliazar and Ithamar.
These are the names of the sons of Ahron the Priest who were anointed and they consecrated their hands to the Priesthood.

And Nadab and Abihu died before LORD JEHOVAH when they offered foreign fire before LORD JEHOVAH in the wilderness of Sinai and they had no sons, and Eliazer and Ithamar served as Priest in the life of Ahron their father.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
“Bring the tribe of Levi and present it before Ahron the Priest and it shall serve him.
And they shall keep his charge and the charge of all the assembly before LORD JEHOVAH before the Time Tabernacle to do service in the Tabernacle.
And they shall keep all the implements of the Time Tent and the guarding of the children of Israel to do the service of the Tabernacle.
And you shall give the Levites to Ahron and to his sons; they are a gift given to him from within the sons of Israel.
And command Ahron and his sons and they shall keep their Priesthood, and the foreigner who comes near to them will be killed.”
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
“Behold, I have taken the Levites from within the children of Israel in place of every firstborn that opens every womb from the children of Israel, that the Levites will be mine.

Because every firstborn is mine; in the day that I killed every firstborn that was in the land of Egypt, I hallowed every firstborn of the children of Israel to me from man and unto animal; they shall be mine; I AM LORD JEHOVAH.”
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe in the wilderness of Sinai and said to him,
“Count the sons of Levi for the house of their fathers by their families; you shall count them, every male from a son of a month and above.”
And Moshe numbered them by the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH as he was commanded.
And these were the sons of Levi by their names: Gershun and Qahath and Merari.
And these are the names of the sons of Gershun for their tribes: Lebni and Shimei.
And the sons of Qahath for their tribes: Amram and Yatzhar and Khebron and Uziel.
And the sons of Merari for their tribes: Makhli and Mushi; these are the families of Levi according to the house of their fathers.
For Gershun: the family of the Libnites and the family of the Shimites, these are the families of Gershun.
And their number in the number of every male from a son of a month and above: seven thousand and five hundred.
The family of Gershun shall be camping behind the Tabernacle from the West.
And the Ruler of the house of the fathers of Gershun: Eliasaph son of Eliab.
And the keeping of the sons of Gershun in the Time Tabernacle, the Tabernacle and the curtain, the door and its veil and the veil of the door of the courtyard that is next to the Time Tabernacle.
And the curtain rods of the courtyard and the veil of the door of the courtyard that is next to the Tabernacle and next to the altar as a circle, and its cords and all its works.
And Qahath’s family of Amram and the family of the Yatzharites and the family of Khebrun and the family of Uziel; these are the families of Qahath.
And their number in the number of every male from a son of a month and above: eight thousand and six hundred keeping the watch of Holiness.
And the family of the house of Qahath will dwell on the borders of the Tabernacle from the South.
And the Ruler of the house of the fathers of the family of Qahath: Elitsaphan son of Uziel.
And their charge: the Ark and the table and the menorahs and the altar and the implements of holiness with which they serve, and the door curtain and all its service.
And the Head of the Rulers of Levi: Eliazer the son of Ahron the Priest, who was authorized over the keepers of the keeping of Holiness.
And Marari’s family of Makhli and the family of Mushi: these are the families of Marari.
And their number in the number of every male from a son of a month and above: six thousand and two hundred.
And the Ruler of the house of the fathers of the families of Merari: Tsuriel son of Abikhel; they shall be camping on the side of the Tabernacle from the North.
And authority of the keeping of the sons of Merari: the boards of the Tabernacle and its bars and its posts and its sockets and all its implements and all its service.
And the pillars of the courtyard surrounding and their cords and their sockets and their curtain rods.

And they who camp before the Tabernacle from the East before the Time Tent from the East: Moshe and Ahron and his sons who keep the guard of Holiness by the guard of the children of Israel, and a stranger approaching will be killed.
All of the number of Levi whom Moshe numbered by the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH, every male from a son of a month and above: twenty and two thousand.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him: “Number every firstborn male of the children of Israel from a son of a month and above and take the number of their names.

And you shall bring the Levites to me (I AM LORD JEHOVAH), in the place of all of the firstborn ones of the children of Israel, and the domestic animals of Levi in the place of all firstborn ones of the domestic animals of the children of Israel.”
And Moshe numbered all of the firstborn of the children of Israel according to what LORD JEHOVAH commanded him.
And all firstborn males by the number of the names of a son of a month and above, the number of them was twenty and two thousand and two hundred and seventy and three.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
“Bring the Levites in the place of every firstborn of the children of Israel, and the cattle of the Levites in the place of their cattle, and the Levites shall be mine; I AM LORD JEHOVAH.
And the redemption of the two hundred and seventy and three more than the Levites, the firstborn of the children Israel:
Take five shekels for a head in the shekel of Holiness; take twenty mayn to a shekel.
And take the money of redemption of those who exceed them to Ahron and to his sons.”
And Moshe took the money of redemption from those who exceeded for the redemption of the Levites.
From the firstborn of the children of Israel, and he took the money: one thousand three hundred and sixty and five shekels in the shekel of Holiness.
And Moshe gave the money of redemption to Ahron and his sons according to the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH, as LORD JEHOVAH commanded Moshe.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and with Ahron and said to them:
“Take an accounting of the sons of Qahath from within the sons of Levi for their families for the house of their fathers.
From a son of thirty years and above, and unto outside fifty years, all who come into the host to do work in the Time Tent.
This is the service of the sons of Qahath in the Time tent, The Holy of Holies.
And Ahron will enter and his sons whenever the camp pick up and they shall take down the veil of the door of the curtain and they shall cover the Ark of the Testimony with it.
And they shall cover a membrane of leather of vermillionsky blue over it and they shall cover with a perfect robe of dark bluepurple from above and they shall set its poles.
And over the table of faces they shall cover with a robe of dark blue and they will set up flasks and spoons and bowls and ladles of drink offerings on it and the bread shall be constantly upon it.
And they shall cover over it with a robe of scarlet and they shall cover it with a membrane of leather of vermillionsky blue that its poles may be set out.
And they shall take a robe of dark blue and they shall cover the lamp stand that gives light and its lamps and its snuffers and its spoons and all vessels of oil with which they serve it.
And they shall put it and all its implements in a cover of leather of sky blue and they will set it on its poles.
And upon the altar of gold they shall cover with a perfect robe of dark blue and they shall cover it with a covering of leather of sky blue and they will set its poles.

And they shall take all of the implements of ministry with which they minister in holiness and they will put them in a robe of dark blue and they shall cover them in a cover of leather of sky blue and they will put them on poles.
And they shall remove the altar and they shall cover over it with a robe of purple.

And they will put with it all of its implements with which they minister upon it, the censers and the forks and the shovels and the fire pans and all of the implements of the altar, and they shall cover over it a membrane of leather of sky blue and they will set its poles.

And when Ahron has finished and his sons covering the holy place and all the garments of Holiness when he picked up camp, then the sons of Qahath shall enter to pick up, that they shall not touch Holiness and die; these are the removals of the sons of Qahath that are in the Time Tent.

And the authority of Eliazar the son of Ahron the Priest of the oil of the lamps and incense of sweet spices and constant meal offering and oil of anointing; he is authorized over all the Tabernacle and over everything that is in it and in the Holy Place and with its implements.”
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and with Ahron and said to them:
“You shall not destroy the tribe of the family of Qahath from within the Levites.

But do this to them and they will live, lest they will die when they enter the Holy Holies; Ahron and his sons shall enter and they shall authorize them, each man over his service and over its removal.
And they shall not enter to see when the holy things are covered lest they die.”
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
“Take an accounting of the sons of Gershun, also of the house of their fathers for their tribes.
From a son of thirty years and above and unto a son of fifty years you shall number them, everyone who comes to be enabled in power and to perform the service in the Time Tent.
This is the service of the family of Gershun to minister and to remove:
They shall take up the curtains of the Tabernacle and the Time Tabernacle.

And the curtain rods of the courtyard and the curtain of the entrance of the door of the courtyard that is next to the Tabernacle, and next to the altar as a circle, and their cords and all implements of their ministry, everything that they were performing and they were ministering.

All the service of the sons of Gershun shall be by the word of the mouth of Ahron and of his sons for all their removal and all their ministry and you shall authorize upon them the keeping over all their removal.
This is the service of the family of the sons of Gershun that is in the Time Tent and their guarding by the hand of Ithamar, the son of Ahron the Priest.
And you shall number the sons of Marari for their families for the house of their fathers.
From a son of thirty years and above and unto a son of fifty years, you shall number them, everyone who comes to be able in power and to do service in the Time Tent.
And this is the guard of their cargo and of all their service in the Time Tabernacle, the boards of the Tabernacle, its bars and its pillars and its sockets.

And the pillars of the courtyard as a circle, and their sockets and their pegs and their cords and all their implements and all their service; count by name the implements of the keeping of their cargo.
This is the service of families of the sons of Marari and all their service in the Time Tent by the hand of Ithamar, the son of Ahron the Priest."
And Moshe and Ahron and the Leaders of the assembly numbered the sons of Qahath by their families for the house of their fathers.
From a son of thirty years and above and unto a son of fifty years, everyone who comes into the host to the service of the Time Tent.
And their number by their families was two thousand and seven hundred and fifty.
These were the numbers of the family of Qahath, everyone who serves in the Time Tent whom Moshe and Ahron numbered by the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH by the agency of Moshe.
And the number of the sons of Gershun by their families for the house of their fathers.
From a son of thirty years and above and unto a son of fifty years, everyone who comes into the host for the service of the Time Tent.
And this is their number by their families by the house of their fathers: two thousand and six hundred and thirty.
These are the numbers of the family of the sons of Gershun, everyone who served in the Time Tent whom Moshe and Ahron numbered by the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH.
And the number of the family of the sons of Marari for their families for the house of their fathers.
From a son of three years and above and unto a son of five years, everyone who comes into the host to the service of the Time Tabernacle.
And their number for their families for the house of their fathers was three thousand and two hundred.
These are the numbers of genealogies of the sons of Merari that Moshe numbered and Ahron by the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH by the agency of Moshe.
Every number that Moshe and Ahron and the Rulers of Israel numbered for the Levites, for their tribes, for the house of their fathers.
From a son of thirty years and above and unto a son of fifty years, everyone who comes into the host to do work in the service and the service of the removal in the Time Tabernacle:
Eight thousand and five hundred and eighty.
By the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH they numbered them by the agency of Moshe, each man for his service and for his removing, and he numbered them as LORD JEHOVAH commanded Moshe.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
“Command the children of Israel that they put everyone who is a leper and everyone who has a discharge and everyone who is defiled in himself outside of the camp.
From male and unto female you shall put out them outside of the camp and they shall not defile your camps, for I dwell among you.”
And the children of Israel did in this way and they took them outside of the camp, and as LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, so the children of Israel did.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
“Say to the children of Israel: ‘If a man or a woman will commit any sin of the children of men and they will do evil before LORD JEHOVAH, that soul shall be condemned.
And they shall confess their sins that they have done and he shall return his sin on his head, and he shall add one fifth to it and return it to him whose the sin offering is.

And if the man does not have one who is near to him, he may bring a sin offering for the sake of sin for his person that they bring for his sake before LORD JEHOVAH to the Priest; it shall be apart from the ram of the atonement by which he is atoned.
And every offering of all holy things that the children of Israel bring to the Priest shall be his.
And a man’s holy thing shall be his, and a man who gives to the Priest, it shall be his.'"
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
“Speak with the children of Israel and say to them: ‘When a man’s wife will go wrong and she will do evil to him.

And a man will have sexual intercourse with her, copulating with seed, and it is hidden from the eyes of her husband, and it is secret, and she is defiled, and there is no witness against her and she is not caught.

And a spirit of jealousy shall come on her husband and he becomes jealous against his wife when she was defiled, or a spirit of jealousy will come upon him and he becomes jealous against his wife when she was not defiled.

The man shall bring his offering to the Priest: one tenth ephah of barley flour, and he shall not pour oil upon it and he shall not lay frankincense upon it, because it is a meal offering, because it is a jealousy meal offering of a memorial that recalls sin.
The Priest shall bring her and she shall stand before LORD JEHOVAH.
And the Priest shall take holy water in a vessel of a potter and the Priest shall take some dust that is in the base of the altar and throw it in the water.

And the Priest shall stand the woman before LORD JEHOVAH and he shall shave the head of the woman and he shall put the meal offering of remembrance, the meal offering of jealousy in her hands, for bitter testing waters shall be in the hands of the Priest.

And the Priest shall put the woman under oath and shall say to her: "If a man has not had sexual intercourse with you and you have not done wrong and you have not been defiled outside of your husband, be cleared from these bitter waters of testing.
But if you have done wrong outside of your husband and you are defiled and a man other than your husband had sexual intercourse with you.”

The Priest shall put the woman under oath in the oath of a curse and the Priest shall say to the Woman: "LORD JEHOVAH shall give you a curse and an oath within your people; LORD JEHOVAH shall rot your thigh; your belly shall swell up.
And these testing waters shall enter your belly and they shall swell your fountains and they shall rot your thighs”, and that woman shall say, "Amen, Amen.”
And the Priest shall write these curses in a scroll and he shall blot them out with the water of testing.
And the woman shall drink the bitter testing water and the testing waters shall enter into her to test her.
And the Priest shall take the meal offering of jealousy from the hands of the woman and he shall offer the meal offering before LORD JEHOVAH and he shall offer it up on the altar.
And the Priest shall offer the memorial of the meal offering and he shall offer it on the altar and then he shall make the woman drink the water.

And if she has been defiled and she has done evil to her husband, and the testing waters entered into her and they tested her and her belly swelled up and her thigh has rotted, that woman will be for a curse from within her people.
But if that woman is not defiled and she is pure, she shall be spared and she shall bear a male.
This is the law of jealousy when a woman does wrong apart from her husband and she shall be defiled.
Or a man upon whom will enter a spirit of jealousy and he will be jealous against his wife and he shall stand the woman before LORD JEHOVAH and the Priest will do to her this whole law.
And that man will be absolved from sin and that woman shall carry her sin.’”
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
“Speak with the children of Israel and say to them: ‘A man or a woman who will separate and will vow the vow of a Nazarite and will be separated to LORD JEHOVAH.
Shall separate from wine and from strong drink; such shall not drink vinegar of wine or vinegar of strong liquor nor shall drink any juice of grapes nor shall eat grapes or raisins.
All of the days of his Nazaritehood, everything that is made from the vine of grape juice, from the skins and unto the grape stones, he shall not eat.

All of the days of the vow of his Nazaritehood; and a razor not will pass on his head until the days when he is separated to LORD JEHOVAH may be fulfilled; he shall be holy and shall grow the hairs of his head.
All of the days that he is separated to LORD JEHOVAH; and he shall not enter upon the person of the dead.
And he shall not be defiled by them, with his father or with his mother or with his brother or with his sister, in their death, because the crown of his God is on his head.
All of the days of his Nazaritehood; he is holy to LORD JEHOVAH.
And if the dead should die upon him suddenly, the head of his Nazaritehood shall be defiled and he shall shave his head in the day of purifying and the seventh day he shall shave it.
And the eighth day he shall bring two turtledoves or two young doves to the Priest, to the door of the Time Tabernacle.
One in exchange for sin and one for a burnt peace offering, and he shall atone for him for the sin that he sinned by the person, and he shall hallow his head in that day.

And he shall be separated before LORD JEHOVAH the days of his Nazaritehood and he shall bring a lamb, a son of a year, for an offering, and the former days shall be nullified because his Nazaritehood was defiled.
This is the law of the Nazarite in the days when the days of his Nazaritehood are fulfilled; he shall be brought to the door of the Time Tabernacle.

And he shall offer his offering to LORD JEHOVAH: one lamb, a son, a year old that has no defect, for a burnt offering, and one ewe lamb, a daughter of a year that has no flaw in her, in the place of sin, and one ram that has no flaw for a peace sacrifice.
And a basket of unleavened bread, cakes of fine flour that is mixed with oil and unleavened wafers that are anointed with oil and their meal offerings and their drink offerings.
And the Priest shall bring it before LORD JEHOVAH and he shall offer his sin offering and his burnt peace offering.
And he shall make a ram a peace sacrifice to LORD JEHOVAH on a basket of unleavened bread, and the Priest shall make his meal offering and his drink offering.

And the Nazarite shall shave the head of his separation in the gate of the Time Tabernacle and he shall take the hair of the head of his separation and he shall put it in the fire that is under the peace sacrifice.

And the Priest shall take the boiled shoulder of the ram and one unleavened cake from the basket and one unleavened wafer, and he shall put it in the hands of the Nazarite after he shaves his Nazaritehood.

And the Priest shall set them apart as a dedication before LORD JEHOVAH; it is holy to the Priest with the breast of dedication and with the shank of dedication, and then the Nazarite will drink wine.

This is the law of the Nazarite who vows to LORD JEHOVAH an offering for his separation apart from whatever he is capable of, according to what is enough; the vow that he vows, in this way he shall do, by the law of his Nazaritehood.’”
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him.
“Speak with Ahron and with his sons and say to them: ‘In this way you shall bless the children of Israel: say to them.
“LORD JEHOVAH shall bless you and shall preserve you.
LORD JEHOVAH shall shine his faces upon you and he shall give you life.
LORD JEHOVAH shall lift his faces upon you and make peace for you.”
And they shall put My Name on the children of Israel and I shall bless them.’”

And it was in the day that Moshe finished his establishment of the Tabernacle, he anointed it and he hallowed it for him and all its implements, and the altar and all its implements; he anointed them and hallowed them.
And the Rulers of the children of Israel approached, the Heads of the house of their fathers, those Princes of the tribes, those who preside over the census.
And they brought their offerings before LORD JEHOVAH: six carts set in order, and twelve oxen, a cart for two Princes and an ox to each, and they brought them before the Tabernacle.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe,
“Take of them and they shall be for performing the service in the Time Tent among them, and give them to the Levites, each man, enough for his service.”
And Moshe took the oxen and the wagons and delivered them to the Levites.
Two wagons and four oxen he gave to the sons of Gershun, enough for their service.
And four wagons and eight oxen he gave to the sons of Merari, enough for their service into the hand of Ithamar, son of Ahron the Priest.
And he did not give to the sons of Qahath, because of the labor of the sanctuary delivered to them, and they carry upon their shoulders.
And the Princes brought gifts of the dedication of the altar in the day when they anointed it, and the Princes brought their offerings before LORD JEHOVAH.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “One Ruler per day shall be bringing their offerings for the dedication of the altar.”
And Nahshon son of Aminadab, the Chief of the tribe of the house Yehuda, was bringing his offering on the first day.

His offering: one plate of silver of a hundred and thirty shekels and one saucer of silver of seventy shekels, by the shekel of Holiness, both being filled with fine flour sprinkled with oil for an offering.
And one spoon, ten shekels of gold, and filled with incense.
And one ox, son of a herd, and one ram and one lamb, son of a year, for a burnt offering.
And one kid of goats for the sake of sins.
And for a peace sacrifice, two bulls, five rams and five goats, and five lambs, offspring of a year old, this is the offering of Nakhshon son of Aminadab.
And in the second day, the Head of the tribe of Issakar, Nathaniel son of Tsuar, came.

His offering: one plate of silver of a hundred and thirty shekels and one saucer of silver of seventy shekels, by the shekel of Holiness, both being filled with fine flour mixed with oil for an offering.
And one spoon, ten shekels of gold, and filled with incense.
And one ox, son of a herd, and one ram and one lamb, son of a year, for a burnt offering.
And one kid of goats for the sake of sin.
And the peace sacrifice: two bulls and five rams and five goats, and five lambs, male offspring of a year; this is the offering of Nathaniel son of Tsuar.
And in the third day, the Head of the tribe of Zebulon, Elyab son of Khalun, came.

His offering: one plate of silver of a hundred and thirty shekels and one saucer of silver of seventy shekels by the shekel of Holiness, both being filled with fine flour mixed with oil for the offering.
And one spoon, ten shekels of gold, and filled with incense.
And one ox, a son of a herd, and one ram and one lamb, a son of a year for a burnt offering.
And one kid of goats for a sin offering.
And for the peace sacrifice: two bulls and five rams and five goats and five lambs, male offspring of a year; this is the offering of Elyab son of Khalun.
And in the fourth day, the Head of the tribe of Rubil, Elitsur son of Shedayur, came.

His offering: one plate of silver of a hundred and thirty shekels and one saucer of silver of seventy shekels by the shekel of Holiness, both being filled with fine flour mixed with oil for the offering.
And one spoon, ten shekels of gold, and filled with incense.
And one ox, a son of a herd, and one ram and one lamb, a son of a year for a burnt offering.
And one kid of goats for a sin offering.
And for the peace sacrifice: two bulls and five rams and five goats and five lambs, male offspring of a year; this is the offering of Elitsur son of Shedayur.
And in the fifth day, the Head of the tribe of Shemon, Shlamayel son of Tsurishaday, came.

His offering: one plate of silver of a hundred and thirty shekels and one saucer of silver of seventy shekels, by the shekel of Holiness, both being filled with fine flour mixed with oil for the offering.
And one spoon, ten shekels of gold, and filled with incense.
And one ox, a son of a herd, and one ram and one lamb, a son of a year for a burnt offering.
And one kid of goats for a sin offering.
And for the peace sacrifice: two bulls and five rams and five goats and five lambs, male offspring of a year; this is the offering of Shlamayel son of Tsurishaday.
And in the sixth day the Head of the tribe of Gad, Elyasaph son of Reuil, came.

His offering: one plate of silver of a hundred and thirty shekels and one saucer of silver of seventy shekels, by the shekel of Holiness, both being filled with fine flour mixed with oil for the offering.
And one spoon, ten shekels of gold, and filled with incense.
And one ox, a son of a herd, and one ram and one lamb, a son of a year for a burnt offering.
And one kid of goats for a sin offering.
And for the peace sacrifice: two bulls and five rams and five goats and five lambs, male offspring of a year; this is the offering of Eliasaph son of Reuil.
And in the seventh day, the Head of the tribe of Aphreim, Elishama son of Amihud, came.

His offering: one plate of silver of a hundred and thirty shekels and one saucer of silver of seventy shekels, by the shekel of Holiness, both being filled with fine flour mixed with oil for the offering.
And one spoon, ten shekels of gold, and filled with incense.
And one ox, a son of a herd, and one ram and one lamb, a son of a year for a burnt offering.
And one kid of goats for a sin offering.
And for the peace sacrifice: two bulls and five rams and five goats and five lambs, male offspring of a year; this is the offering of Elishama son of Amihud.
And in the eighth day, the Head of the tribe of Menasheh, Gamaliel son of Partsur, came.

His offering: one plate of silver of a hundred and thirty shekels and one saucer of silver of seventy shekels, by the shekel of Holiness, both being filled with fine flour mixed with oil for the offering.
And one spoon, ten shekels of gold, and filled with incense.
And one ox, a son of a herd, and one ram and one lamb, a son of a year for a burnt offering.
And one kid of goats for a sin offering.
And for the peace sacrifice: two bulls and five rams and five goats and five lambs, male offspring of a year; this is the offering of Gamaliel son of Partsur.
And in the ninth day, the Head of the tribe of Benyamin, Abidan son of Geduni, came.

His offering: one plate of silver of a hundred and thirty shekels and one saucer of silver of seventy shekels, by the shekel of Holiness, both being filled with fine flour mixed with oil for the offering.
And one spoon, ten shekels of gold, and filled with incense.
And one ox, a son of a herd, and one ram and one lamb, a son of a year for a burnt offering.
And one kid of goats for a sin offering.
And for the peace sacrifice: two bulls and five rams and five goats and five lambs, male offspring of a year; this is the offering of Elishama son of Amihud.
And in the tenth day, the Head of the tribe of Dan, Akhiazar son of Amishdaya, came.

His offering: one plate of silver of a hundred and thirty shekels and one saucer of silver of seventy shekels, by the shekel of Holiness, both being filled with fine flour mixed with oil for the offering.
And one spoon, ten shekels of gold, and filled with incense.
And one ox, a son of a herd, and one ram and one lamb, a son of a year for a burnt offering.
And one kid of goats for a sin offering.
And for the peace sacrifice: two bulls and five rams and five goats and five lambs, male offspring of a year; this is the offering of Akhiazar son of Amishdaya.
And in the eleventh day, the Head of the tribe of Ashayr, Pagayel son of Akran, came.

His offering: one plate of silver of a hundred and thirty shekels and one saucer of silver of seventy shekels, by the shekel of Holiness, both being filled with fine flour mixed with oil for the offering.
And one spoon, ten shekels of gold, and filled with incense.
And one ox, a son of a herd, and one ram and one lamb, a son of a year for a burnt offering.
And one kid of goats for a sin offering.
And for the peace sacrifice: two bulls and five rams and five goats and five lambs, male offspring of a year; this is the offering of Akhiazar son of Amishdaya.
And in the twelfth day, the Head of the tribe of Naphtali, Akhida son of Ainan, came.

His offering: one plate of silver of a hundred and thirty shekels and one saucer of silver of seventy shekels, by the shekel of Holiness, both being filled with fine flour mixed with oil for the offering.
And one spoon, ten shekels of gold, and filled with incense.
And one ox, a son of a herd, and one ram and one lamb, a son of a year for a burnt offering.
And one kid of goats for a sin offering.
And for the peace sacrifice: two bulls and five rams and five goats and five lambs, male offspring of a year; this is the offering of Akhida son of Ainan.

This was the offering of dedication of the altar in the day that it was anointed from the presence of the Princes of Israel: twelve plates of silver and twelve saucers of silver and twelve spoons of gold.
A hundred and thirty shekels of silver was of one plate and seventy of one saucer; all silver implements were two thousand and four hundred by the shekel of Holiness.
And twelve spoons of gold and being full of incense, being of ten shekels, the spoons were by the shekel of Holiness; all the gold of the spoons was a hundred and twenty shekels.
And all oxen for the burnt offering: twelve; twelve rams and twelve lambs, offspring of a year and their fine flour, and twelve kids of goats for sin.
And all oxen for the peace sacrifice: twenty and four; sixty rams and sixty goats, and sixty lambs, offspring of a year; this is the dedication of the altar after it was anointed.
And when Moshe entered the Time Tabernacle, he heard a voice speaking with him from the Mercy Seat above the Ark of the Testimony; between the two Cherubim, it spoke with him.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
“Speak with Ahron and say to him: ‘Whenever you shall light the lamp opposite the face of the lamp stand, seven lamps shall be giving light.’”
And Ahron so made seven lamps opposite the face of the lamp stand and he lit its lamps as LORD JEHOVAH commanded Moshe.

And this was the work of the menorah: it is of the forging of gold from its base and unto its top; it is of the forging according to the appearance that LORD JEHOVAH had shown Moshe; thus was the work for the menorah.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
“Take the Levites from within the children of Israel and purify them.
In this way do to them and purify them: sprinkle upon them the waters of sins and they shall pass a razor on all their flesh and they shall wash their garments and they shall be purified.
And they shall take one bull, son of a herd, and its offering: one plate full of meal mixed with oil, and offer another bull, a son of a herd for sin.
And bring the Levites before the Time Tabernacle and gather all the assembly of the children of Israel.
And bring the Levites before LORD JEHOVAH and the children of Israel shall put their hands on the Levites.
And Ahron shall dedicate the Levites as a dedication before LORD JEHOVAH from the presence of the children of Israel and they shall be ministering the ministry of LORD JEHOVAH.
And the Levites shall put their hands on the head of the bull and make one for sin and one for a burnt offering to LORD JEHOVAH that he would be atoned for the Levites.
And stand the Levites before Ahron and before his sons and dedicate them as a dedication to LORD JEHOVAH.
And dedicate the Levites from within the children of Israel and the Levites shall be mine.
And after this the Levites shall enter and they shall serve in the Time Tabernacle; and purify them and consecrate them as a consecration before LORD JEHOVAH.
Because they are a gift given to me from within the children of Israel, in the place of every firstborn one opening the womb of all of the children of Israel; I have brought them to me.

Because all the firstborn of the children of Israel, from the children of men and unto an animal are mine; in the day when I killed every firstborn of things that are in land of Egypt, I hallowed them to myself.
And I have brought the Levites in the place of the firstborn of the children Israel.

And I have given the Levites as a gift to Ahron and his children from within the children of Israel to do the service of the children of Israel in the Time Tabernacle and to make atonement for the children of Israel; and death shall not suddenly be among the children of Israel when the children of Israel come near to Holiness."
And Moshe and Ahron and all of the assembly of the children of Israel did to the Levites as LORD JEHOVAH commanded Moshe about the Levites, so the children of Israel did to them.
And the Levites were cleansed and they washed their garments and Ahron consecrated the Levites as a consecration before LORD JEHOVAH and Ahron atoned for them and purified them.

And after the Levites entered to minister their ministry in the Time Tent before Ahron and before his sons, and as LORD JEHOVAH commanded Moshe about the Levites, so the children of Israel did to them.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
“This shall be the law of the Levites from a son of twenty and five years and above: he will come to be strengthened in power in the service of the Time Tabernacle.
And from fifty years old he will return from power of service and he shall serve no more.
And he shall serve his brothers in the Time Tabernacle and the work to keep guard; he shall not work; so do to the Levites in their charge."
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe in the wilderness of Sinai in the second year since the children of Israel came out from Egypt, in the first month, and he said to him:
“The children of Israel shall perform Passover in its time.
On the fourteenth in this month at sunset they shall perform it in its time; they shall perform it according to all its orders and according to all its judgments.”
And Moshe said to the children of Israel that they shall observe Passover.
And they observed Passover on the fourteenth of the first month, at sunset, in the wilderness of Sinai, according to what LORD JEHOVAH commanded Moshe; so the children of Israel did.
And men who were defiled by the persons of men were not able to observe Passover in that day, and they approached before Moshe and before Ahron on that day.
And they were saying to those men, “We are defiled by the person of a man; why are we forbidden and we do not bring the offering of LORD JEHOVAH in its time among the children of Israel?”
And Moshe said to them, “Stand on your place and I shall make heard what LORD JEHOVAH commands concerning you.”
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
“Speak with the children of Israel and say to them, ‘When a man shall be defiled by a person or on a distant road or by your posterity, he shall keep Passover to LORD JEHOVAH.
In the fourteenth in the second month at sunset they shall keep it; they shall eat it with unleavened bread and bitters.
And not they shall leave any of it for the morning, and they shall not break a bone in it; they shall do it according to every commandment of Passover.

And a man that is pure and was not in the way and did not observe Passover in its time, that soul shall be cut off from his people, because he did not bring the offering of LORD JEHOVAH in its time; that man shall bear sin.

And when one who is converted to me will settle with you and he will observe Passover to LORD JEHOVAH according to the commandment of Passover and according to its judgments, thus he shall keep it; one law shall be for you and for the one who has turned to me and for the settler of the land.”

And in the day when the Tabernacle was raised up, a cloud covered the Time Tabernacle and at the door of the Testimony, and in the evening there was on the Tabernacle as the appearance of fire until the morning.
So it was constantly, and a cloud was covering it by day and the appearance of fire in the night.
And when the cloud was ascending from the Tabernacle, after this the children of Israel were picking up and in the place where the cloud abode on it, there the children of Israel pitched camp.

By the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH the children of Israel picked up and by the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH all of them were pitching camp; they stayed for the days when the cloud abode on the Tabernacle.
And when the cloud continued on the Tabernacle for many days, Israel kept the charge of LORD JEHOVAH and did not pick up.
And there was time when the cloud was fewer days on the Tabernacle; by the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH they stayed and by the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH they picked up.

And there was a time when the cloud was on the Tabernacle from evening and unto the morning, and the cloud went up at dawn, and they picked up; whether the cloud went up day or night, they also were picking up.
Or days or months or time to time, however long the cloud was remaining on the Tabernacle, so the children of Israel remained and they did not pick up, and whenever the cloud went up, they picked up.

By the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH they remained and by the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH they picked up, and they kept the charge of LORD JEHOVAH according to the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH by the agency of Moshe.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
“Make for yourselves two horns of silver; make them of forged work and they shall be for you to call the assembly and to pick up camp.
And they shall sound with them and all of the assembly shall be gathered to you to the door of the Time Tabernacle.
And if they sound once, the Princes and the Leaders of the thousands of Israel shall gather to you.
And they shall blow reveille with a trumpet and they shall pick up the camps that dwell at the east.
And they shall blow reveille with a trumpet twice and they shall pick up the camps that dwell at the south; they shall sound with the trumpet for their removal.
And when you assemble, be sounding and not blowing reveille.
And the sons of Ahron the Priest shall sound with the trumpets and they shall be the law to you for eternity with your generations.

And if war will come to your land against the oppressor who oppresses you, blow reveille with the trumpets and you shall be remembered before LORD JEHOVAH your God and you shall be saved from your enemies.

And in the day of your joy and of your meetings and of the beginnings of your months, sound with the trumpets for your burnt offerings and for your peace offerings and they shall be to you for a memorial before your God; I AM LORD JEHOVAH your God.”
And it was in the year two, in the tenth in the second month, the cloud went up from the Tabernacle of the Testimony.
And the children of Israel picked up in their moving from the wilderness of Sinai, and the cloud dwelt in the wilderness of Paran.
And they picked up first at the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH by the agency of Moshe.
And they picked up first the troop of the camp of the sons of Yehuda by their armies and the Prince of the army: Nahshon son of Aminadab.
And Prince of the army of the tribe of the sons of Issakar: Nathaniel son of Tsuar.
And Prince of the army of the tribe of the sons of Zebulon: Eliab son of Khelon.
And they took down the Tabernacle and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari, the bearers of the Tabernacle, picked up.
And the troop of the camp of Rubil and their army picked up; and Prince of the army: Elitsur son of Shedur.
And Prince of the army of the tribe of the sons of Shemon: Shlamayel son of Tsurishaday.
And Prince of the army of the tribe of the sons of Gad: Eliasaph son of Reuil.
And the sons of Qahath the bearers of the Tabernacle were picking up and erecting the Tabernacle until they came.
And the troop of the camp of Aphreim by their army picked up and the Prince of the host: Elishama son of Amihud.
And Prince of the army of the tribe of Menasheh: Gamaliel son of Partsur.
And Prince of the army of the tribe of Benyamin: Abidan son of Geduni.
And the troop of the camp of the sons of Dan picked up and all the camp and their army assembled, and the Prince of the army of the tribe of the sons of Dan: Akhiazar son of Amishaday.
And the Prince of the host of the tribe of Ashayr: Pagayl son of Akran.
And the Prince of the host of the tribe of Naphtali: Akhida son of Ainan.
These were the travels of the children of Israel for their hosts.

And Moshe said to Khubab, son of Rueil the Midianite, father in law of Moshe: “We are moving to the region of which LORD JEHOVAH said, ‘I shall give it to you’; come with us and we will do good to you because LORD JEHOVAH spoke good for Israel.’”
And he said to him “I shall not go, but I shall go to my land where I was born.”
Moshe said to him: “Do not leave us, because you know the place where we dwell that is in the wilderness and you have been eyes for us.
And if you go with us, what good it is that LORD JEHOVAH will do good for us, he will do good for you.”

And they moved from the mountain of LORD JEHOVAH a journey of three days, and the Ark of the Covenant of LORD JEHOVAH moved from before them a journey one day that it would prepare for them a dwelling place.
And the cloud of LORD JEHOVAH was over them by day whenever they moved from the camp.
And it was that when the Ark moved, Moshe said: “Arise LORD JEHOVAH, and your haters shall be defeated and we shall chase your enemies from before you.”
And when it rested he said, “Return, LORD JEHOVAH, unto the ten thousands and unto the thousands of Israel.”

And when the people were complaining, it was evil before LORD JEHOVAH, and LORD JEHOVAH heard and his wrath was provoked and kindled the fire of LORD JEHOVAH among them and consumed in the surroundings of the camp.
And the people cried out to Moshe and Moshe prayed before LORD JEHOVAH and the fire ceased.
And he called the name of that region “The Burning”, because the fire of LORD JEHOVAH burned among them.
And a mixed multitude that were among them lusted lust and returned; they caused weeping also for the children of Israel and they were saying, “Who will feed us meat?
We recall the fish that we were eating in Egypt without charge, and cucumbers and melons and leeks and onions and garlic.
And now, behold, our soul has wasted away and there is nothing but this Manna before our eyes.”
And manna was like the seed of coriander and its color like the color of pearl.

And the people were walking around and they gathered it and were grinding it at the mill or were beating it in a mortar and were boiling it in pots and they made cake from it and its taste was like the taste of dough with oil.
And whenever the dew descended on the camp in the night, Manna came down upon it.
And Moshe heard the people weeping among their families, each man in the door of his tent, and the wrath of LORD JEHOVAH was provoked, and it seemed evil also in the eyes of Moshe.
And Moshe said to LORD JEHOVAH: “My Lord, why have you done evil to your Servant and why have I not found mercy in your eyes, that you have laid upon me all the weight of this people?
Have I conceived all this people or have I given birth to it, that you say to me, ‘Take it up into your bosom like a nurse taking up an infant, to the land that I swore to their fathers?.’
From where do I have meat that I shall give to all this people that weep upon me and they say, ‘Give us flesh to eat?
I cannot endure all this people alone, because it is too hard for me.
If you do so to me, kill me please, if I have found mercy in your eyes, and I shall not see my misery.”

And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “Gather to me seventy men from the Elders of Israel, those whom you know, those who are the Rulers of the people and its Scribes, and take them to the Time Tabernacle, and they will be prepared there with you.

And I shall come down and I shall speak with you there and I shall take away from the Spirit that is upon you and I shall put it upon them and they shall bear with you in the burden of this people and you shall not bear it alone.”

And Moshe said to the people: “Be hallowed tomorrow, for you shall eat meat, because you have wept before LORD JEHOVAH and you have said, ‘But Who will feed us meat, because that was good to us in Egypt’, and LORD JEHOVAH will give you meat that you may eat.
You shall not eat it one day nor two days, nor five days, neither ten days, neither twenty days.

For a month of days you shall eat it until it goes out of your noses and you shall have nausea, because you rejected LORD JEHOVAH Who is among you and you wept before LORD JEHOVAH and you said, ‘Why have we come out from Egypt?.’”
And Moshe said before LORD JEHOVAH: “The people among whom I am are six hundred thousand footmen, and you have said: ‘I shall give flesh to them and they shall eat a month of days.’
If sheep and oxen are slaughtered for them, what do they do for them? And if all of the fish of the sea are caught for them, how are they filled with them?”
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “The hand of LORD JEHOVAH is full; now you shall see if My word comes to pass or not.”
And Moshe went out and told the people the answer of LORD JEHOVAH and he gathered seventy men from the Elders of the people and he stood them around the Tabernacle.

And LORD JEHOVAH came down in the cloud and he spoke with him and took from the Spirit that was upon him and he gave to the seventy men, the Elders, and when the Spirit abode upon them they prophesied and they did not repeat.

And two men were left in the camp; the name of one, Eldad, and the name of the other, Midad, and the Spirit dwelt upon them, also those were written down and they did not come to the Tabernacle and they prophesied in the camp.
And a young man ran and he showed Moshe and said to him: “Eldad and Midad, behold they prophesy in the camp.”
And Husha Bar Nun the Servant of Moshe from his childhood answered and said to him: “My Lord Moshe, forbid them.”
Moshe said to him: “Do not be jealous for me; for who makes all the people of LORD JEHOVAH prophets? It is because LORD JEHOVAH puts his Spirit upon them.”
And Moshe entered the encampment, he and the Elders of Israel.

And the Spirit moved on from before LORD JEHOVAH and brought up quails from the sea and poured them on the camp a day’s journey here and a day’s journey there around the camp, and there were about two cubits on the face of the ground.

And the people arose all that day and all night and all day after it and they gathered the quails; the one that gathered ten cors had little, and they spread them out like a spread all around the camp.
The flesh was yet between their teeth and before it had passed through, the wrath of LORD JEHOVAH prevailed over the people and LORD JEHOVAH struck the people with a plague that was very great.
And he called the name of that country “The Tombs of Lust”, because there they performed a burial for the people who lusted lust.
And from Qabrey D'Regta “The Tombs of Lust”, the people moved to Khatseroth and they were in Khatseroth.
And Maryam and Ahron spoke with Moshe on the matter of an Ethiopian woman whom he took because he had taken an Ethiopian woman.
And they were saying, “Has LORD JEHOVAH spoken only with Moshe? Behold, he has spoken also with us”; and LORD JEHOVAH heard.
And the man Moshe was very humble, more so than all of the children of men upon Earth.
And LORD JEHOVAH said suddenly to Moshe and to Ahron and to Maryam: ‘Go out, you three, to the Time Tabernacle.’
And LORD JEHOVAH descended in a pillar of cloud and stood in the door of the Time Tabernacle, and he called Ahron and Maryam and they both went out.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to them: “Hear now my words; if your Prophecy will come, I LORD JEHOVAH am revealed in a vision upon you, and in a dream I speak with you.
My Servant Moshe has not been so, but he is faithful in all my house.
From mouth to mouth I am speaking with him in a vision and not in a likeness, and he saw the glory of LORD JEHOVAH; why were you not afraid to speak against my Servant Moshe?”
And the wrath of LORD JEHOVAH was provoked against them and he went out.
And the cloud left from the Tabernacle, and behold, Maryam was leprous like snow, and Ahron turned to Maryam and he saw that she was leprous.
And Ahron said to Moshe, “I beg of you, my Lord, do not account the sin against us for that foolishness we have done and for that sin we have done.
And do not let us be as a dead person who goes out from the belly of her mother and the body of her flesh is consumed!”
And Moshe cried out to LORD JEHOVAH and he said, “Help, O God! Heal her!”

And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “If her father had spit in her face, it would have been fitting for her that she would be ashamed seven days; she shall dwell seven days outside of the camp and then she shall enter.”
And Maryam dwelt outside of the camp seven days, and the people did not pick up until Maryam entered.
And then the people picked up from Khatseroth and they camped in the wilderness of Paran.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
“Send men; they shall explore the land of Canaan that I give to the children of Israel; every Leader that is among them, send one man from the tribe of his fathers.”
And Moshe sent them from the wilderness of Paran by the word of the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH; all of the men were Leaders of Israel.
And these are the names from the tribe of Rubil: Shamua son of Zakor.
From the tribe of Shemon: Shaphat son of Khadi.
From the tribe of Yehuda: Kalab son of Yuphana.
From the tribe of Issakar: Negayel son of Yoseph.
From the tribe of Aphreim: Husha son of Nun.
From the tribe of Benyamin: Palti son of Daphu.
From the tribe of Zebulon: Gaddiel son of Suri.
From the tribe of Yoseph of the tribe of Menasheh: Gaddi son of Susi.
From the tribe of Dan: Gamaliel son of Gamli.
From the tribe of Ashayr: Sethur son of Malkiel.
From the tribe of Naphtali: Nakhbi son of Waphsi.
From the tribe of Gad: Gwayel son of Makir.
These are the names of the men whom Moshe sent to spy the land, and Moshe called Husha, son of Nun, Yeshua.
And Moshe sent them to spy the land of Canaan and said to them: “Come up in this way to the south and go up the mountain:
And see the land, what it is, and the people dwelling in it, if it is strong or weak, or it is few or many.
And what the land is which they inhabit.
If it is fertile or trees are in it or not.” And they were strengthened and carried some fruit of the land, and the days were the days of first fruits of grapes.
And they went up and explored the land from the wilderness of Tsin and unto to Rekhob, which is in the entrance of Khamath.
And they came up to the south and they came unto Khebron and Akhiman; and Shishay and Tulmay, sons of the giants, were there, and Khebron was built seven years before Tsaan that is in Egypt.
And they came unto the valley of SegulaClusters and they cut from there a vine shoot and one cluster of grapes and they took it away on a pole between two and some pomegranates and some figs.
And they called that place the valley of SegulaClusters because of the clusters that sons of Israel had picked from there.
And they returned from the exploring of the land after forty days.

And they came to Moshe and to Ahron and to all of the assembly of the children of Israel to the wilderness of Paran, to Raqem, and they returned a report to him and to all of the assembly, and they showed them the fruit of the land.
And they related to him and they said: “We went on to the land where you sent us, and it flows milk and honey and these are its fruit.
However the people dwelling in the land is strong and the towns are fortified and very great, also we saw the sons of the giants there.
Amelik dwells in the land of the south and the Hittites and Yebusites and Amorites dwell in the mountains and Canaanites dwelt on the side of the sea and upon the side of the Jordan.”
And Kalab silenced the people in the name of Moshe and said: “Let us surely go up and possess it because we can prevail.”
And the men who went up with him were saying, “We cannot go up against the people because it is stronger than we.”

And those who spied out sent out the report of the land to the children of Israel and they said to them: “The land in which we passed through and spied out, the land consumes its inhabitants, and all the people whom we saw within it are men of stature.
And there we saw giants, sons of giants who are from the giants, and we were in their eyes like grasshoppers, thus we were in their eyes.”
And all of the assembly was in an uproar and lifted up their voice with weeping and the people wept that night.

And all of the children of Israel complained against Moshe and against Ahron, and the whole assembly said to them: “Oh, that we had died in the land of Egypt or in this wilderness! Oh that we were dead!
Why does LORD JEHOVAH bring us into this land that we shall fall by a sword, and our women and our little ones shall be prey? It was better for us when we were dwelling in Egypt!”
And they said, each man to his brother, “Come, we shall make a Leader for us and we shall return to Egypt!”
And Moshe and Ahron fell on their faces before all the crowds of the assembly of the children of Israel.
And Yeshua, son of Nun, and Kalab, son of Yuphana, of the spies of the land, tore their garments.
And they said to all of the assembly of the children Israel: “The land in which we went through to explore is very good land.
Truly, LORD JEHOVAH is pleased with us and brings us to this land and he gives to us this land that flows milk and honey.

Only do not rebel against LORD JEHOVAH and do not be afraid of the people of the land, because they are our bread, for their strength itself has passed away from them and LORD JEHOVAH is with us; you shall not be afraid of them.”
And all of the assembly said to stone them with stones, and the glory of LORD JEHOVAH was revealed in the cloud in the Time Tabernacle to the eyes of all the children of Israel.
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “How long do this people anger me, and how long do they not trust me in all the signs that I have done among them?
I shall strike them with a plague and I shall destroy them and I shall make you a people greater and mightier than they.”
And Moshe said to LORD JEHOVAH: “The Egyptians will hear that you brought up this people by your power from among them.

And the inhabitants of this land who heard that you are LORD JEHOVAH within this people will say that you LORD JEHOVAH are seen, eye to eye, and your cloud stands above them, and in a pillar of cloud you have gone out before them by day and in a pillar of fire by night.
And whenever you have killed this people as one man, the nations that have heard your name will say:
‘Because LORD JEHOVAH was not able to bring in the people to the land that He swore to them, because of this, He slaughtered them in the wilderness.’
And now let your power be great LORD JEHOVAH, as you have spoken and you have said.

LORD JEHOVAH, your Spirit is patient and your kindness abundant, and you forgive evil and sin and you do not justify to justify; you order the lovedebts of the fathers on the children and upon the children of the children, upon three and upon four generations.
Forgive now the evil of this people according to the multitude of your favors, and as you have forgiven them from Egypt and unto now.”
And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “I have forgiven them, according to your word.
However, I live, and all the earth is filled with the glory of LORD JEHOVAH.
And all the men who have seen my glory and the signs that I have done in Egypt and in the wilderness and have tested me, behold, ten times, and they have not listened to my voice.
They shall not see the land about which I have sworn to their fathers: ‘No one provoking me shall see it.’
And my Servant Kalab, for the sake of my Spirit Who has been with Him, and he has been fully after me, I shall bring him into the land that he entered there and his seed shall inherit it.
And the Amalekites and the Canaanites dwell in the mountains; tomorrow turn and move you to the wilderness to the way of the Sea of Reeds.”
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and with Ahron and said to them:
“How long is this evil assembly complaining before me? I have heard the complaining that the children of Israel were complaining before me.
Say to them: ‘I am living, says LORD JEHOVAH, but as you have said before me, so shall I do to you.
In this wilderness your corpses will fall and all your number and all your numbering from a son of twenty years and above, because you were complaining against me.
You shall not enter the land for which I raised my hands for you to dwell in, except only Kalab son of Yuphanah and Yeshua son of Nun.
