And in year fourteen of King Hezekiah, Sennakherib, King of Assyria, went up against all the fortress cities of Yehuda and he seized them.

And Hezekiah King of Yehuda sent to the King of Assyria at Lakish and said to him: “I have sinned! Return from me, and anything that you lay on me, I am bearing.” And the King of Assyria laid on Hezekiah, King of Yehuda, three hundred talents of silver and thirty talents of gold.
And Hezekiah gave all the silver that was found in the house of LORD JEHOVAH and in the treasury of the house of the King.
In that time Hezekiah stripped the doors of the temple of LORD JEHOVAH and the door posts which Hezekiah, King of Yehuda, had overlaid, and he gave them to the King of Assyria.

And returning, the King of Assyria sent Tartan and Rab Siseq and Rab Shakeh from Lakish to Hezekiah the King with a powerful army to Jerusalem, and they came up to Jerusalem and they stood at the ascent of the Upper Lake that is on the road of the Field of the Fortress.
And they called to the King, and Eliakim, son of Khelqia, the Steward, and Shebna the Scribe, and Yuakh, son of Asaph, the Chronicler went out to them.
And Rab Shaqeh said to them: “Say to Hezekiah, ‘Thus says the great King, the King of Assyria: what is this trust that you have trusted?
And you have said that speech of the lips is in you, and counsel and might for battle! Now, on whom is it you have trusted, that you have rebelled against me?

Behold, you have trusted on a support of a broken reed, on an Egyptian, upon which a man having leaned, it went into his hand and pierced him. So is Pharaoh, King of Egypt, to all whoever trust upon him.

And if you say to me: “Upon LORD JEHOVAH our God we trust”, was it not Hezekiah who removed the high places and the altars, and he said to Yehuda and to Jerusalem: “You shall worship before one altar in Jerusalem!”
And now the Assyrians are mixed with my Lord the King, and I shall give you two thousand horses, if you have horsemen whom you will set upon them!
And how do you turn the face of one of the little Nobles, the Servants of my Lord, and are confident yourself upon an Egyptian to give you chariots and horsemen?
And now, lest you think that I went up against this land for its destruction apart from LORD JEHOVAH, LORD JEHOVAH himself said to me: ‘Go up against this land, and I shall destroy it!’”

And Eliakim, son of Khelqia, and Shebna and Yuakh said to Rab Shakeh .”Speak with your Servants in Aramaic, because we hear, and do not speak with us in Judean before the people who stand on the wall.”

And Rab Shakeh said to them.”It was not to you and to your Lord that my Lord has sent me to say these words, but to the men sitting on the wall, that they would not eat their own dung and would not drink their urine with you.”
And Rab Shakeh stood and he called in a loud voice in Judean, and he said: ”Hear the statement of the great King, the King of Assyria.
Thus says the King: let not Hezekiah deceive you, for he cannot save you from my hands!
And do not let Hezekiah cause you to trust on LORD JEHOVAH, and he will say: ’Surely LORD JEHOVAH will deliver us and this city is not delivered into the hands of the King of Assyria!’

You will not hear Hezekiah, for thus says the King of Assyria: “Make a blessing with me and go out with me, and a man will eat his grapes and a man his figs, and a man will drink the waters of his well.

Until I come and I bring you to a land like your land, a land of varieties and produce, a land of grain and of vineyards, a land of olives and of fertility and of honey, and live, and you shall not die; and you should not hear Hezekiah, and do not let Hezekiah deceive you and say to you:”LORD JEHOVAH delivers us!”
Are the gods of the nations able to save the God of his land from the hands of the King of Assyria?
Where are the gods of Khamath and of Raphad, and where are the gods of Sapharvim and Dana and of Iva? Have they delivered Samaria from my hands?
Who of all the gods of these lands has delivered his land from my hands, that LORD JEHOVAH will deliver Jerusalem from my hands?”
And the people kept silent and they did not give him an answer, because the King commanded and said: “Do not give him an answer!”
And Eliakim son of Khelqia the Steward came, and Shebna the Scribe and Yuakh son of Asaph the Chronicler to Hezekiah while their clothes were torn, and they told him the words of Rab Shakeh.
And when Hezekiah the King heard, he ripped his clothes, and there was hidden sackcloth, and he entered into the house of LORD JEHOVAH.
And Eliakim Master of the house and Shebna the Scribe and the Elders of the Priests, while covered in sackcloth, sent to Isaiah the Prophet, son of Amuts
And they said to him: “Thus says Hezekiah, ‘It is a day of trouble and of rebuke and of passion today, because labor pains of the children have arrived, and there is no power in the laboring mother!

Perhaps LORD JEHOVAH your God will hear the words of Rab Shakeh whom his Master the King of Assyria sent to reproach the living God, and he shall rebuke him for the words that LORD JEHOVAH your God heard, and you will seek and you will pray for the rest who are left.”
And the Servants of King Hezekiah came to Isaiah the Prophet.

And Isaiah said to them, “Thus you will say to your Master: ‘Thus says LORD JEHOVAH, you shall not be afraid of the words that you heard by which the Messengers of the King of Assyria blasphemed before me.
Behold, I have put a spirit in him, and he will hear a report, and he shall return to his land, and I shall cast him down by the sword in his land.’”
And Rab Shakeh returned and he found the King of Assyria when he fought with Lebnah, because he heard that he had picked up from Lakish.
And he heard concerning Tarkhaq, King of Kush, that he went out to fight with him, and he returned and he sent Messengers to Hezekiah:
“Thus you will say to Hezekiah, King of Yehuda: your God upon whom you trust should not deceive you and you would say that Jerusalem is not delivered into the hands of the King of Assyria.
Behold, you have heard all that the King of Assyria has done to all lands, and he destroyed them, and are you delivered?
How have the gods of the nations delivered Gauzan and Kharan and Ratsaph and the children of Eden who were in Dalaser, whom my fathers killed with the sword?
Where is the King of Khamath and the King of Raphad and the King of the city Sepharvaim, and of Ana and of Ava?”
And Hezekiah took the letters from the hand of the Messengers, and he called them and he went up to the house of LORD JEHOVAH, and Hezekiah spread the letters before LORD JEHOVAH.

And Hezekiah prayed before LORD JEHOVAH, and said: “LORD JEHOVAH of Hosts, God of Israel, sitting above the Cherubim, you are God, you alone, over all the kingdoms of Earth. You have made Heaven and Earth.
Incline, LORD JEHOVAH, your ear, and hear; open, LORD JEHOVAH, your eyes and see, and hear all the words of Sennakherib who sent to reproach the living God!
Truly, LORD JEHOVAH, the Kings of Assyria have ravaged the nations and their lands
And their gods they have burned in fire, because they were not gods, but the work of the hands of the children of men, of wood and of stone, and they burned them.
And now, LORD JEHOVAH, our God, save us from his hands, that all the kingdoms of Earth may know that you are LORD JEHOVAH God, you alone!”
And Isaiah son of Amuts sent to Hezekiah, and he said: “Thus says LORD JEHOVAH, God of Israel, ‘Everything that you have prayed before me concerning Sennakherib, King of Assyria, I have heard.
This is the word that LORD JEHOVAH has spoken concerning him: “The virgin daughter of Zion despises you and mocks you, and the daughter of Jerusalem wags her head at you.
Whom have you rebuked, and in front of whom have you blasphemed, and against whom have you lifted your voice? You have lifted your eyes on high against The Holy One of Israel!

And by the agency of your Messengers you have rebuked LORD JEHOVAH, and you have said: “With the multitude of my chariots I shall ascend to the height of the mountains and to the borders of Lebanon, and I shall cut off the heights of the cedars, and the best of its cypresses, also I shall enter to the height of the limit of the forest of Karmela.
I shall dig and I shall drink the waters and I shall dry up with the hooves of my horses all the mighty rivers.”
Have you not heard that from a distance I have made it, and from the first days I have prepared it, and now I have brought it, that you may be for a wilderness and a desolation as a fortress city?

Because their power has become feeble, they are broken down, and they are ashamed, and they have become as grass of the field and like green sprouts of spring, and like the grass of a rooftop, and like a tender sprout before the stalk.
Your dwelling and your entering and your going out I know, and that you were presumptuous against me.

Because you have been presumptuous and you were arrogant against me, and your blasphemy went up in my ear, I shall put a ring into your nose and a bridle into your lips, and I shall turn you back into the way by which you came.

And this will be a sign for you: eat this year an uncultivated crop, and the second year that which grows of the uncultivated crop, and the third year sow and reap and plant vineyards and eat their fruit.
And the rest of the house of Yehuda who were left shall continue; they cast a root beneath and they make fruit above.
Because from Jerusalem will come forth a remnant, and an escape from the Mountain of Zion. The indignation of LORD JEHOVAH of Hosts shall do this.

Because of this, thus says LORD JEHOVAH about the King of Assyria: “He shall not enter this city, and he shall not shoot an arrow there, and he shall not come before it with shields and he shall not lay an ambush in wait against it.
But by the way that he came in he shall return, and he shall not enter this city, says LORD JEHOVAH.
And I shall dwell upon this city and I shall save it for my sake and for the sake of David my Servant.”

And it happened that night and the Angel of LORD JEHOVAH went out and he killed a hundred and eighty five thousand in the camp of the Assyrians, and they arose at dawn, and they saw, and behold, all the dead carcases.
And they picked up and they went, and Sennakherib the King of Assyria returned and he dwelt in Nineva.

And while he was worshiping in the house of Nesrak his god, Adramelek and Sharetsar his sons murdered him with a sword, and they escaped to the land of Aradat, and Serakhdum his son was made king after him.

In days those Hezekiah became ill to die, and Isaiah the Prophet, son of Amuts, came to him, and said to him: “Thus says LORD JEHOVAH: ‘Command concerning your house, because you are dying and you are not going to live.’”
And Hezekiah turned his face to the wall and he prayed before LORD JEHOVAH and said:
“Oh, LORD JEHOVAH, remember that I walked before you in the truth and with a whole heart, and I have done what is beautiful before you!” And Hezekiah wept with great weeping.
And Isaiah had not gone out to the middle courtyard, and the word of LORD JEHOVAH was upon him saying:

“Return, and say to Hezekiah, the Ruler of my people: “Thus says LORD JEHOVAH, the God of David your father: ‘I have heard your prayer and I have seen your tears. Behold, I am healing you, and on the third day you shall go up to the house of LORD JEHOVAH.

And I shall add to your days fifteen years, and from the hands of the King of Assyria I shall save you and this city, and I shall rest on this city and I shall save it for my sake and the sake of David my Servant.”
And Isaiah said: “They shall take a mass of figs and they shall put it on the absess and it is healed.”
And Hezekiah said to Isaiah: “What is the sign that LORD JEHOVAH heals me, and I come up on the third day to the house of LORD JEHOVAH?”

And Isaiah said: “This is the sign for you from before LORD JEHOVAH, that LORD JEHOVAH will perform for you the statement that he spoke: will the shadow walk ten degrees, or will it return ten degrees?”
And Hezekiah said: “This is little that the shadow would walk ten degrees; not so, but let the shadow return behind itself ten degrees!”
And Isaiah the Prophet called to LORD JEHOVAH, and the shadow returned by the degrees that the sun had gone down in the degrees of Akhaz, ten degrees backward.
At that time Merudak Baladan, son of Baladan, King of Babel, sent a letter and an offering to Hezekiah when he heard that Hezekiah was ill and he survived.

And Hezekiah rejoiced with them and he showed them the house of his treasury, silver and gold and fragrances and fine ointment and all the houses of his armory, and all that was found in the house of his treasury, and Hezekiah left nothing that he did not show them in his house and in all the place of his dominion.

And Isaiah the Prophet came to Hezekiah the King and said to him: “What did these men say to you, and from where did they come to you?” And Hezekiah said: “From the distant land of Babel they came to me.”
And he said: “What did they see in your house?” And Hezekiah said, “All that is in my house they have seen, and I left out nothing in my house that I have not shown them in my treasury.”
And Isaiah said to Hezekiah: “Hear the answer of LORD JEHOVAH:

Behold, the days are coming and everyone who is in your house shall be taken, and the treasure that your fathers kept until today shall go to Babel, and you shall have nothing left, says LORD JEHOVAH.
And some of your children whom you beget who will come out of you, they shall be taken away and they shall be Eunuchs in the temple of the King of Babel.”
And Hezekiah said to Isaiah: “The answer of LORD JEHOVAH which you have spoken is good! But oh that there would be peace and truth in my days!”

And the rest of the words of Hezekiah and all his heroism, and that he made a lake and a canal and he brought water to the city, behold, they are written in the book of The Chronicles of the Kings of Yehuda.
And Hezekiah fell asleep with his parents, and Manassheh his son became king after him.
Manassheh was a son of twelve years when he became king, and he reigned fiftyfive years in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Khebtsibah.
And he did evil before LORD JEHOVAH, like the defilement of the Gentiles whom LORD JEHOVAH destroyed from before the children of Israel.

And he turned and built the high places that Hezekiah his father had torn down, and he raised up altars to Baal and he made dreaded things, as Akhab King of Israel had done, and he bowed to all the host of Heaven and he worked for them.
And he built altars in the house of LORD JEHOVAH, who had said, “In Jerusalem I shall put my name.”
And he built altars to all the host of Heaven in the two courtyards of the house of LORD JEHOVAH.
And he made his son to pass into fire, and he consulted an oracle and practiced sorcery, and made Necromancers and Soothsayers, and he increased doing evil before LORD JEHOVAH to anger him.

And he placed the image of the dreaded thing that he had made in the house of LORD JEHOVAH, in the house of which LORD JEHOVAH had said to David and to Solomon his son: “In this his house and in Jerusalem which I have chosen from all the tribes of Israel, to put my name there for eternity.

And I shall not continue to make the feet of Israel roam from the land that I have given to their fathers, if only they will keep and they will do everything that I have commanded them and every law that my Servant Moshe commanded them.”
And they did not listen, and Manassheh led them astray, and he did what is evil, more than the nations that LORD JEHOVAH destroyed from before the sons of Israel.
And LORD JEHOVAH spoke by the agency of his Servants the Prophets
“Because Manassheh son of Hezekiah King of Yehuda did these works and did evil more than all that the Amorites had done before him, and he caused Yehuda to sin with his idols
Because of this, thus says LORD JEHOVAH, God of Israel: ‘Behold, I bring evil on Yehuda and upon Jerusalem, so that everyone who will hear it, his two ears will tingle.

And I shall cast upon Jerusalem the cord of Samaria and the plum line of the house of Akhab, and I shall strike Jerusalem and I shall destroy it, because of all the evil that Manassheh practiced in Yehuda
And I shall forsake the rest of my inheritance and I shall deliver them into the hand of their enemies, and they shall be for prey and for being downtrodden by all of their enemies
Because they have done evil before me, and they were angering me from the day that their fathers had gone out from Egypt and until today.”
Also Manassheh shed very much innocent blood until he filled Jerusalem, end to end, apart from his sins which he caused Yehuda to sin, and he did evil before LORD JEHOVAH.
And the rest of the words of Manassheh and everything that he did, and the sins that he sinned, behold, they are written in the book of The Chronicles of the Kings of Yehuda.
And Manassheh fell asleep with his parents, and he was buried in the garden of his house in the garden of the treasury, and Amon his son became king after him.
Amon was a son of twenty two years when he became king, and he reigned two years in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Meshelemeth, daughter of Kheduts, from Yatbath.
And he did evil before LORD JEHOVAH, like what Manassheh his father did
And he walked in all the ways where his father had walked, and he served the idols which his father had served, and he bowed to them
And he abandoned LORD JEHOVAH, the God of his fathers, and he did not walk in the way of LORD JEHOVAH.
And the Servants of Amon rebelled against him, and they killed him in his house.
And the people of the land killed all who rebelled against King Amon, and the people of the land made Yoshiah his son king after him.
And the rest of the words of Amon, and everything that he did, behold they are written in the book of The Chronicles of the Kings of Yehuda.
And they buried him in his tomb in the garden of the treasury, and Yoshiah his son became king after him.
Yoshiah was a son of eight years when he became king, and thirtyone years he reigned in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother, Yadida, daughter of Azarayah, was from Bazqeth.
And he did what is beautiful before LORD JEHOVAH, and he walked in all the way that David his father had walked, and he did not turn aside to the right or to the left.
And in year eighteen of King Yoshiah, the King sent to Shaphan, son of Alatsia, son of Mashlam, the Scribe of the house of LORD JEHOVAH, saying:
“Go up to Khelqia the great Priest, and he shall hand over the silver that comes into the house of LORD JEHOVAH which the keepers of the door have collected from the people.

And they shall deliver it to my Servants of the work that was appointed in the house of LORD JEHOVAH, and they shall deliver it to the Servants of the work that is in the house of LORD JEHOVAH, to make the repairs of the house of LORD JEHOVAH
To the Carpenters and to the Stone Masons and to the Master Builders, to buy wood and cut stones to restore the house of LORD JEHOVAH.”
However, the money that was given into their hands was not accounted with them, because they were working by faith.

And Khelqia the Priest High said to Shaphan the Scribe: “I have found the Scroll of the Law in the house of LORD JEHOVAH!” And Khelqia the Priest gave the Scroll of the Law to Shaphan the Scribe, and he read it.

And Shaphan the Scribe came to the King, that he would return to the King an account, and he said: “Your Servants have delivered the silver that was found in the house of LORD JEHOVAH and have delivered it to the Servants of the work who were put in charge in the house of LORD JEHOVAH.”
And Shaphan the Scribe showed the King and said: “Khelqia the Priest gave me the Scroll of the Law!” And Shaphan read it before the King.
And when the King heard the words of the Scroll of the Torah, he ripped his clothes.
And the King commanded Khelqia and Akhiqam, son of Shaphan, and Abakur, son of Mikah, and Shaphan the Scribe, and Ashia the Servant of the King, and said:

“Go ask of LORD JEHOVAH for me and for all the people and for all Yehuda concerning the words of this scroll that was found, because the anger of LORD JEHOVAH is great with which he is angered against us, because our fathers have not listened to the words of this scroll to do according to what is written in it.”

And Khelqia the Priest went on, and Akhiqam, and Abakur, and Shaphan, and Ashaya to Khuldi the Prophetess, the wife of Shallum, son of Teqwa, son of Khadkhas, Keeper of Armor, and she was dwelling in Jerusalem in the second rank, and they spoke with her.
And she said to them: “Thus says LORD JEHOVAH, God of Israel: ‘Say to the man who sent you to me:
‘Thus says LORD JEHOVAH, God of Israel: behold, I bring evil on this place and upon its inhabitants all the words of the scroll that the King of Yehuda read

Because they have forsaken me and they have placed incense to other gods, and they angered me by the work of their hands, my anger will increase in this place, says LORD JEHOVAH, and I shall destroy you.’
And to the King of Yehuda who sent you to inquire of LORD JEHOVAH, thus say: ‘Thus says LORD JEHOVAH, God of Israel:

The words which you have heard, because your heart was sad and you trembled from before LORD JEHOVAH when you heard the thing that I said against this place and against its inhabitants, that it will be a horror and for a curse, and you have torn your garments and you wept before me, also I have heard, says LORD JEHOVAH.

Because of this, behold, I gather you to your fathers and you shall be gathered to a tomb in peace, and your eyes shall not look on all the evil that I bring on this place.’” And they returned the answer to the King.
And the King sent and assembled to himself all the Elders of Yehuda and of Jerusalem.

And the King went up to the house of LORD JEHOVAH, and all of the house of Yehuda, and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem with him, and the Priests and the Prophets and all the people, from the little ones and unto the great, and he read before them all the words of the Scroll of the Covenant that was found in the house of LORD JEHOVAH.

And the King stood by a pillar and he covenanted a covenant before LORD JEHOVAH to walk after LORD JEHOVAH to keep his commandments and his testimonies and his covenants with all their heart, and with all their soul, that they shall establish the words of this covenant that are written in this scroll. And all the people agreed to the covenant.

And the King commanded Khelqia the High Priest and the Priests who were behind him and those keeping the door, to cast out from the house of LORD JEHOVAH all the garments that were made for Baal and for the idols and for all the host of Heaven, and they burned them outside of Jerusalem in the valley of Qedrun, and he took their ashes to Bayth Eil.

And he killed those Priests whom the Kings of Yehuda appointed to lay incense on the high places in the villages of Yehuda and in the environs of Jerusalem, and who were setting incense for Baal, and for the sun, and for the moon, and for the stars and all the host of Heaven.

And he brought out the dreaded thing from the house of LORD JEHOVAH, outside of Jerusalem to the valley of Qedrown, and he burned it in the valley of Qedrown, and he made it as the dust, and he cast its dust on the tombs of the children of the people.
And he tore down the houses of prostitution that were in the house of LORD JEHOVAH, and of the women who were weaving garments for the dreaded idols there.

And he brought all the Priests from the cities of Yehuda and defiled the high places where the Priests set incense in them, from Dan and unto Beersheba, and he threw down the high place that was in the entrance of the Gate of Redemption that is in the city, that was upon the left of the man who is at the gate of the city.
However, the Priests of the high places were not going up to the altar of LORD JEHOVAH in Jerusalem, but only ate unleavened bread with their brethren.

And he tore down the high places that the Kings of Yehuda had made in Taphat that is in the Valley in BarHennum, because the Kings of Yehuda had made a man to pass his son or his daughter into fire for Amlek.

And he killed the horses that the Kings of Yehuda gave to the sun in the entrance of the house of LORD JEHOVAH, in the arched chamber of Nathan the Eunuch of the King that was in Paruda, and the chariots of the sun he burned in fire.

And the altar that was upon the roof of the high place of Akhaz that the Kings of Yehuda had made and the altar that Manassheh made for the two courtyards of the house of LORD JEHOVAH, the King tore down, and he rode quickly from there and he cast their dust into the torrent of Qedrown.

And the high places that were before Jerusalem that were at the right hand of the Destroying Mountain that Solomon King of Israel had built for Astruth, godess of the Tsidonians, and for Kemush the dreaded one of Moab, and for Malcum the dreaded one of the children of Amon, King Yoshiah broke down.
And he shattered the monuments and cut off the idols and filled their place with the bones of the sons of men.

Also the altar that is in Bayth Eil and the high places that Yorbaam, son of Nebat, made who caused Israel to sin, also he broke down the altar and the high place, and he burned the high place and he pulverized it like dust, and he burned the feared things.

And Yoshiah returned and he saw the tombs there on the mountain, and he sent and he took the bones from the tombs, and he burned on the altar and defiled it according to the word of LORD JEHOVAH that the Prophet of God spoke who had cried out these words.

And the King said: “What is this monument that I see?” The men of the city were saying to him, “It is the tomb of the Prophet of God who came from Yehuda, and he cried these words that you have done against the altar of Bayth Eil.”
And he said: “Leave him! Do not let a man come to the tomb, and no man shall move his bones!” And they saved his bones, the bones of the Prophet who came from Samaria.

Also all of the houses of the idols that are in the towns of Samaria that the Kings of Israel had made to anger LORD JEHOVAH, Yoshiah removed, and he made them like the works that he had done in Bayth Eil.
And he slaughtered all the Priests of the high places who had put incense on the altars, and he burned the bones of the men upon them, and he returned to Jerusalem.
And the King commanded all the people, and said: “Perform the Passover to LORD JEHOVAH your God, as it is written in the Book of this Covenant
Because a Passover like this had not been made from the day of the Judges who had judged Israel, until all the days of the Kings of Israel and the Kings of Yehuda
Except only in the eighteenth year of King Yoshiah, when this Passover was made to LORD JEHOVAH in Jerusalem!

Also Necromancers and Soothsayers and idols and dreaded things and every abomination that appeared in the land of Yehuda and in the streets of Jerusalem, Yoshiah rooted out so as to confirm the words of the Book of the Torah that are written in the Scroll that Khelqia the Priest found in the house of LORD JEHOVAH.

And there was not a King like him before him, for he was turned to LORD JEHOVAH with all his heart and with all his soul and with all his power, as it is written in the Torah of Moshe, and after him none arose like him.
However, the great anger of LORD JEHOVAH did not turn away, because he was angry against Yehuda, because of the anger with which Manassheh had angered him.

And LORD JEHOVAH said: also I remove Yehuda from before me as when I removed Israel, and I shall reject this city which I have chosen, Jerusalem, and the house of which I said: “My name shall be there.”
And the rest of the words of Yoshia and everything that he did, behold, they are written in the book of The Chronicles of the Kings of Yehuda.

In his days Pharaoh, the Cripple King of Egypt, went up by Mabug that is by the River Euphrates, and King Yoshiah went to meet him to fight with him, and Pharaoh said to him: “It is not against you that I come; turn aside from me.” And Yoshiah did not listen to him, and Pharaoh struck and killed him in Magdu when he saw him there.

And his Servants took him away from Magdu when he died, and they brought him to Jerusalem, and they buried him there in his tomb, and the people of the land took Yehukhaz, son of Yoshiah, and they anointed him and they made him king in place of his father.
Yahuakhaz was a son of twenty three years when he became king, and he reigned three months in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Khamatul, the daughter of Jeremiah, from Lebna.
And he did evil before LORD JEHOVAH as Manassheh had done.

And Pharaoh, the Cripple, the King of Egypt bound him in Deblath, in the land of Khamath, when he became king in Jerusalem, and he imposed taxes on the land, a hundred talents of silver and ten talents of gold.
And Pharaoh the Cripple made Eliakim, son of Yoshiah, king in the place of Yoshiah his father, and he named his name Yoiqim, and he led Yehuakhaz away, and he entered Egypt and died there.

And the silver and the gold Yoiqim gave to Pharaoh, however, the silver and gold he raised over the land because of the word of the mouth of Pharaoh. Each man as he thought right was bringing silver and gold from the people of the land, because of the word of the mouth of Pharaoh the Cripple.
Yoiqim was a son of twenty and five years when he became king, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Zebida, daughter of Paria from Ramtha.
And he did evil before LORD JEHOVAH as his fathers had done.
In his days Nebukadnetsar King of Babel went up against Jerusalem, and Yoiqim was a Servant to him for three years, and he turned and he rebelled against him.

And LORD JEHOVAH provoked bands of robbers of the Chaldeans against him, and robbers of Edom, and robbers of Moab, and robbers of the children of Amon, and he provoked them against Yehuda to destroy them, according to the word of LORD JEHOVAH that he spoke by the agency of his Servants the Prophets from the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH.
And there was great wrath against Yehuda, that he would remove them from before him, because of the sins of Menashe and everything that he did,
Also the innocent blood that he shed; and innocent blood filled Jerusalem, and LORD JEHOVAH was unwilling to forgive.
And the rest of the words of Yoiqim and everything that he did, behold, they are written in the book of The Chronicles of the Kings of Yehuda.
And Yoiqim fell asleep with his parents, and Yuyaqin his son became king after him.
And the King of Egypt did not go out again from his land, because the King of Babel had taken everything that the King of Egypt had, from the River of Egypt and unto the River Euphrates.
Yuyaqin was a son of eighteen years when he became king, and he reigned three months in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Nekheshta, daughter of Elithan, from Jerusalem.
And he did evil before LORD JEHOVAH as his father did.
At that time Nebukadnetsar King of Babel went up against Jerusalem, and the city was besieged in the siege.
And Nebukadnetsar King of Babel came upon the land, and his Servants were encamping against the city.

And Yuyaqin King of Yehuda went out to the King of Babel, he and his mother, and his Servants, and his Princes, and his Eunuchs, and the King of Babel took him with him in the eighth year of his kingdom.

And he brought out from there all the treasure of the house of LORD JEHOVAH, and the treasure of the house of the King, and he cut apart all the vessels of gold that Solomon King of Israel had made for the house of LORD JEHOVAH, as LORD JEHOVAH had said.

And he led all Jerusalem captive, and all the Princes, and all ten thousand mighty men of the army into captivity, and all the Guards, and all the Runners, and he left none except the poor of the people of the land.
And he led Yuyaqin captive to Babel, and the mother of the King, and the wives of the King and his Eunuchs, and the Princes of the land, and he took them into captivity from Jerusalem to Babel.
And all seven thousand mighty men, and the Guards and the one thousand Runners and all men who were workers of war, the King of Babel brought them into the captivity to Babel.
And the King of Babel made Metanya his paternal uncle king in his place, and he named his name Tsedeqia.
Tsedeqia was a son of twenty and one years when he became king, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Khamatul, daughter of Jeremiah, from Lebna.
And he did evil before LORD JEHOVAH as did Yoiqim.
And the anger of LORD JEHOVAH was upon Yehuda and upon Jerusalem until he drove them out from before him, and Tsedeqia rebelled against the King of Babel.

And in the ninth year of his kingdom, on the tenth of the tenth month, Nebukadnetsar King of Babel came, he and all his army, against Jerusalem, and he encamped against it and built against it bulwarks in a circle.
And the city was besieged in the siege until the eleventh year of King Tsedeqia.
And in year eleven of King Tsedeqia, in the fifth month, on the ninth of the month, a famine prevailed in the city, and there was no bread for the people of the land.

And the city was breached and all the men warriors fled, and they went out from the city in the night on the road of the gate that was between the walls that were at the side of the garden of the King, and the Chaldeans surrounded the city, and they went on the road of the plain.
And the army of the Chaldeans pursued after the King, and they overtook the King in the plain of Yerikho, and all the army were scattered from him.
And they seized the King and they brought him to the King of Babel to Deblath, and he spoke judgment with him.
And the King of Babel slaughtered the sons of Tsedeqiah before his eyes, and he put out the eyes of Tsedeqiah, and he bound him in chains and led him to Babel.
In the fifth month, on the ninth of the month of the nineteenth year for Nebukdanetsar the King of Babel, Nebuzaradan the High Guard, the Servant of the King of Babel came to Jerusalem.
And he burned his house of LORD JEHOVAH and the house of the King and all the houses of Jerusalem, and every house of the Princes he burned in fire.
And all the army of the Chaldeans that was with the General of the Guards pulled down the surrounding wall of Jerusalem.

And the rest of the people who were left in the city, and the deserters who deserted with the King of Babel and the rest of the army, Nebuzaradan General of the Guards took captive and he took them to Babel.
And some poor of the land Nebuzaradan General of the Guards left as Vinedressers and Farmers.

And the pillars of brass in the house of LORD JEHOVAH and the basin and the sea of brass in the house of LORD JEHOVAH the Chaldeans broke down, and they picked up all their brass and they brought it to Babel.
The metal pots and the cauldrons and large hanging pots and spoons and all implements of brass with which they served, they took.
And the General of the Guards took away the censers and the incense burners of gold and of silver and the cups
The two pillars and one sea and the basin that King Solomon made for the house of LORD JEHOVAH, and there was no weight measure for the brass of these vessels and those pillars.

Eighteen cubits was the height of one of the pillars, and the capital atop it of brass, and the height of the capital was three cubits, and carved ornaments and pomegranates on the capital in a circle and all of brass, and thus also on the other pillar and all of brass.
And the General of the Guards took Sheriah the Great One of the Priests and Tsephenia the Priest who was after him, and three Keepers of the Gate

And he led one Eunuch from the city, he who was charged over the men Warriors, and five men who had appeared before the King who were found in the city, and the Scribe and the General of the army who had strengthened the people of the land, and sixty men of the people of the land who were left in the city.
And Nebuzaradan, General of the Guards, took them and he led them to the King of Babel at Deblath.
And the King of Babel struck them and he killed them in Deblath, in the land of Khamath, and he led Yehuda captive from his land.
And the people who were left in the land of Yehuda, those whom Nebukadnetsar King of Babel left, he appointed over them Gedaliah, son of Akhiqam, son of Shaphan.

And all the Commanders of the army heard, those and their mighty men, that the King of Babel authorized Gedaliah, and Ishmayl, son of Nethaniah, and Yokhanan, son of Qorakh, and Sharia, son of Tanakhmath, and Tubia and Yazeniah, son of Makath, came to Gedaliah to Metspaya, they and their mighty men.
And Gedaliah swore to them and to his men and said to them: “You shall not be afraid of the Chaldeans. Dwell in the land and serve the King of Babel, and it shall be well with you!”

And in the seventh month Ishmayl, son of Nethaniah, son of Ishmayl, came from the seed of the kingdom, and ten men with him, and they struck Gedaliah and he died, also the Judeans and the Chaldeans who were with him in Mezpaya.
And all the people of the land arose, from little one and unto the great, and all the Commanders of the army, and they entered into Egypt because they were afraid of the Chaldeans.

And in year thirty seven for the captivity of Yoiakin, King of Yehuda, in the twelfth month on the twenty seventh of the month, Ewal Merudak King of Babel in the first year that he reigned, lifted up the head of Yoiakin, the King of Yehuda, and brought him out from the prison.
And he spoke good things with him and he raised his throne higher than the thrones of the Kings who were with him in Babel.
And he changed his prison garments and he ate bread in his presence constantly, all the days of his life.
And with his banqueting a meal was given to him constantly before the King of Babel, every day, all the days of his life.

name: 1 Chronicles
chapters: Adam, Shayth, Enosh.
Qainan, Mehalaleil, Yared.
Henok, Mathuselah, Lamek.
Noah, Shaym, Kham and Yaphet.
The sons of Yaphet: Gamar and Magug and Maday and Yavan and Tubeil and Mashak and Tiras.
The sons of Gamar: Ashknez and Diphar and Tugarma.
And the sons of Yavan: Elisha and Tarshish and Katheem and Dudaneem.
And the sons of Kham: Cush and Metsraim and Phut and Canaan.
And the sons of Cush: Shebah and Khavila and Sabta and Rama and Sebaktha and the sons of Rama: Shebah and Daran.
And Cush begot Nemruud; he began to be a mighty man in the earth.
And Metsrim begot Ludeem and Yabeem, Lahbeem and Yaphtukheem.
And Pathruseem and Kaslukheem of whom had gone out from there the Philistines and Qapduqians.
And Canaan begot Tsidon his firstborn and the Khethites.
And the Yebusites and the Amorites and the Gergusites.
And the Kharites and the Arqites and the Sinites.
And the Arudites and the Tsamrites and the Khamathites.
The sons of Shaym: Ilam and Ashud and Arphakshar and Lud and Aram and Uz and Khul and Gathar and Mash.
And Arphakshar begot Shalakh and Shalakh begot Abar.
And to Abar were born two sons his name of one Palag because in his days was divided the Earth and the name of his brother Yaqtan.
And Yaqtan begot Elmudad and Shalaph and Khetsarmuth and Yarakh.
And Haduram and Uzel and Deqla.
And Ubal and Abimel and Shaba.
And Auphir and Akhwila and Yubab. All these are the sons of Yaqtan:
Shaym, Arphekshar, Shalakh.
Eber, Palag, Aru.
Serug, Nakhor, Tarakh.
Abram who is Abraham.
And these are sons of Abraham: Iskhaaq and Ishmaeyl.
And these are their generations: the firstborn of Ishmael: Nebiuth, also Qadar and Arbal and Mabsem.
And Mashmah and Ruma, Massa and Hadad, Taymna.
And Natur and Naphesh and Aqdama; these are the sons of Ishmaeyl.
And the sons of Qetura, the concubine of Abraham: Zamran and Yaqshan and Maran and Medyan Ashbaq and Ashwakh and the sons of Yaqshan: Shebah and Daran.
And the sons of Medyan: Epha and Aphar and ENoah and Abida and Elda; these all are sons of Qetura.
And Abraham begot Isaaq; the sons of Isaaq: Esau and Israel.
The sons of Esau: Eliphaz and Ruyel and Yaush and Ilaam and Qurakh.
And the sons of Eliphaz: Tayman and Umar and Tsuph and Gatham and Qanez and Tamna and Ameliq.
The sons of Ruil: Nakhath and Zarakh and Shama and Maza.
And the sons of Seir: Lutan and Shubal and Tsebun and Ana and Dooshan and Atsar and Dayshan.
And the sons of Lutan: Khurar and Humam and the sister of Lutan, Tamna.
And the sons of Shubal: Anwan and Mankhath and Ubal and Shaphar and Uyam and the sons of Tsabown: Ana and Anah.
And the sons of Anah: Dishun and the sons of Dishun: Khamran and Ashkan and Yathran and Karan.
The sons of Atsar: Kalhan and Zamun and Yaqan and the sons of Dishun: Uz and Aram.
And these are the Kings that reigned in the land of Edom, before a king would reign over the children of Israel: Bala, son of Beor, was made king in Edom, and the name of his city, Daihab.
And Bala died, and after him Yubab, son of Zarakh, from Butsar, became king.
And Yubab died, and Khusham from the land of the south became king after him.
And Khusham died, and Hadad, son of Badad, became king after him, who put the Midianites to the sword in the fields of the Moabites, and the name of his city, Gweeth.
And Hadad died, and after him Samla from Masreqa became king.
And Samla died, and after him Shaul from the River Rekhbuth became king.
And Shaul died, and Belkhnanen, son of Abkur, became king after him.
And Belkhnanen son of Abkur died, and after him Hadad became king, and the name of his city, Pau, and the name of his wife, Mahtwael, daughter of Matreed, son of Mizaheb; and Hadad died.
And there were Princes of Edom: Prince Tamna, Prince Ithith.
Prince Ahlibama, Prince Ilah, Prince Pinun.
Prince Qanez, Prince Tayman, Prince Mabtsar.
Prince Magdiyel, Prince Giram; these were the Princes of Edom.
And these are the sons of Israel: Rubil, Shemon, Levi, Yehuda, Issakar, Zebalaun
Dan, Yoseph, Benjamin, Naphtali, Gad, Ashir.
The sons of Yehuda: Eyr and Unaan and Shila; these three were born to him from Bathshua the Canaanitess, and Eyr was the firstborn of Yehuda; he was evil before LORD JEHOVAH, and he killed him.
And Tamar her daughter in law gave birth to Phares and Zarah; all the sons of Yehuda were five.
The sons of Pharets: Khetsrun and Khamuyel.
And the sons of Zerekh: Zamri and Athan and Haman and Kalcal and Darda, all five of them.
And the sons of Karmi Akar the impediment of Israel because he did evil with an accursed thing.
And the sons of Athan: Azaryah.
And the sons of Khetsrun who were born to him: Rakhmayel and Ram and Salki.
And Ram begot Aminadab and Aminadab begot Nakhshun, Prince of the children of Yehuda.
And Nakhshun begot Salma and Salma begot Baaz.
And Baaz begot Ubar and Ubar begot Iyshay.
And Iyshay begot his firstborn, Eliab, and the second, Abinadab and Shama the third.
And Nathanail the fourth and Dari the fifth.
And Utsum the sixth and Elihu the seventh and David the eighth.
And their two sisters Tsuria and Abigal; and the sons of Tsuria: Abishi and Yuab and Ashail, three.
And Abigal gave birth to Amasa, and the father of Amasa was Yathar.
And Kalab, son of Khetsron, begot from Aruba his wife, Idiuth, and these are her sons: Dakar, Ashir and Shubab and Aduun.
And Aruba died and Kalab took to himself Ephrath, and she bore to him Khur.
And Khur begot Uri and Uri begot Betsaliel.
And after these things, Khetsron entered into the daughter of Makir, the father of Gelad, and he took her and he was a son of sixty years, and she bore to him Sagub.
And Sagub begot Yair, and he had twentythree towns in the land of Gelad.
[Verse text missing in source]
And after these things, Khetsron died in the land of Kalab with Ephrath, and she had given sons birth to him, Ashtwair the father of Taqu.
And the sons of Yarkhmel were: the firstborn of Khetsrun, Aram and Banna, and Aran and Atsam their sister.
And Yarkhmel had another wife, and her name was Etra; she was the mother of Eunim.
And the sons of Aram, the firstborn of Yarkhmel, were Maz and Nabin and Utar.
And the sons of Eunim were Sabi and Yuda, and the sons of Sabi: Nadab and Abishur.
And the name of the wife of Abishur was Abikhel, and she bore to him Akhban and Mulid.
And the sons of Nadab: Salad, Palarim, and Salad died without sons.
And the sons of Palarim: Eshaia and the sons of Eshaiah: Shushan, and the sons of Shushan, Akhli.
And the sons of Yuda, the brother of Sabi: Yathar and Yonathan, and Yathar died without sons.
And the sons of Yonathan: Lapath and Uza; these were the sons of Yarkhmel.
And Shushan had no male children, but only daughters, and Shushan had one man, one son in law from Matsra, and his name was Yardakha.
And had given him Shushan his daughter for a wife, and she bore to him, Atti.
And Atti begot Nathan, and Nathan begot Debir.
And Debir begot Aphil, and Aphil begot Yubab.
And Yubab begot Azraya, and Azraya begot Yahu, and Yahu begot Azraya.
And Azraya begot Khalaz, and Khalaz begot Elasa.
And Elasa begot Samsi, and Samsi begot Shalum.
And Shalum begot Elqamyah, and Elqamyah begot Elishma.
And the sons of Kalab, the brother of Irakhmel: Elishmi, the firstborn is the father of Ziph, and the sons of Marasha, the father of Khebroun:
Qurakh and Tappukh and Raqem and Shema.
And Shema begot Rakham, the father of Yarqam, and Yarqam begot Sammi.
And Uphna of the concubine of Kalab gave birth to Khuran, and Khuran begot Guzan.
These were the sons of Kalab, son of Khur, the firstborn of Aphrath: Shabal was born in Quryath Narin.
[Verse text missing in source]
[Verse text missing in source]
[Verse text missing in source]
[Verse text missing in source]
Samla was born in Bayth Lekhem; Abi was born in Gadar.
And the sons to Shabal in Quryath were Narin, Atruth, Yubal, Khatsri.
[Verse text missing in source]
Sepharim, Netuphath, Samla, Shemuthim, Shekab, Khama.
All these were sons to Shabal in Quryath: Narin.
And these were the sons to David in Khebruun: the name of his firstborn son, Khamnun of Akhinam who was from Yizreil, the second, Kalab, from Abigel the wife of Nabal who was from Mount Karmela.
The third, Abishlum, son of Maaka, daughter of Talmay, King of Geshur, the fourth, Aduniah son of Khagith.
The fifth, Shephatiah, son of Aphietel, the sixth, Ituram, who was from Egla, the wife of David.
These six sons were born to David in Khebruun, and he reigned there seven years and six months, and thirty and three years he reigned in Jerusalem.
And these are the names of the sons who were born to him in Jerusalem: Shemua and Shakub and Nathan and Solomon.
And Yabkhar and Elishema and Elida.
And Eliphlat and Nugah and Nephag.
And Naphig and Elisha.
These are all the sons of David, and Tamar was their sister.
And the sons of Solomon: Rekhabaam his son, Abia the son of Asa, the son of Yushaphat his son.
Yuram his son, Ekhazyah his son, Yoash his son.
Amutsia his son, Uzzia his son, Yotham his son.
Akhaz his son, Hezekiah his son, Manassheh his son.
Amon his son, Yoshiah his son.
And the sons of Yoshia: the firstborn Yokhanan, the second Yoiqim, the third Tsedeqia, the fourth Shallum.
And the sons of Yoiqim: Yokania his son, Tsedeqia his son.
And the son of Yokania, Ashrashtil his son.
And Malcum his son and Peraia his son and Shaatsar his son and Neqamia his son and Shemua his son and Nedabia his son.
And the sons of Nedabia: Zurbabeil and Shami, and the sons of Zurbabeil: Meshlam and KhananYah and Selqath their sister.
And Kheshiba and Yahuyel and Bria and Khesadya.
The sons of KhananYah: Pelatyah and Ushaya, Arphaya his son, Arnon his son, Oubedaya the son of Ushaya.
And the son of Shekania: Shemaiah; and the son of Shemaiah, Khatush; and Negayel and Azarya and Khezaqaya.
The Peshitta omits this verse.
And Hudiah and Alshib and Pelayah and Yaqub and Yokhanan and Daliah and Anen.
And these are the sons of Yehuda: Pharez and Khetsrun and Karmi and Yanur and Shubal.
And Laiah, son of Shubal, begot Nakhath, and Nakhath begot Akhimi and Alyad; these are the generations of Rekhabam.
And these the sons of Aminadab: Akhizarel and Neshma and Dibash.
And Peguyel and Khushiya, and these are the sons of Khur, the firstborn of Aphrath, whose father was from Bayth Lekhem.
And Akhishur, the father of Tequ, had two wives.
And one of them bore Akhiram and Taphar and Tayman and Kharshtar; these were the children of one of them.
The Peshitta omits this verse.
The Peshitta omits this verse.
And one of them was precious to his mother and to his father, and he called his name Aynay.

And he said to him: “Blessing, LORD JEHOVAH will bless you, and he shall multiply your border, and his hand shall be with you, and he shall save you from the Evil One that he will not have power over you, and he will give you whatever you ask him.”
And Kalab the brother of Akhia begot Mekhir; he was the father of Eshtun.
And Eshtun begot Rupha, and Rupha begot Paskhi, and Paskhi begot Tekhia, and Tekhia begot Yazir.
[Verse text missing in source]
[Verse text missing in source]

These were the sons of Kalab, son of Yuphna: the name of his firstborn son, Ala, and the name of the second, Naam, and the name of the third, Qanz, and the name of the fourth, Ashiph, and the name of the fifth, Yamuyel, and the name of the sixth, Yahrub, these were sons of Kalab, son of Yuphna.
[Verse text missing in source]
[Verse text missing in source]
[Verse text missing in source]
And the sons of the wife of Uriah, the sister of Nakhum, the father of Qayla: Zemri and Eshtmu and Makith and Eshtma and Ashimun.
And the sons of Ashimun: Amon and Dumia and Zarakh and Shela.
[Verse text missing in source]
[Verse text missing in source]
[Verse text missing in source]
These are the sons of Yehuda: Yamueil and Yamin and Ahar and Yakeen and Tsakhar, and Reeb, and Zarakh, and Shaul, the brother of Nakhum, the father of Qayla.
And the sons of Shela, the son of Yehuda.
And the sons of Shemon: Mebsam his son, Mashma his son, Shemathi his son, Khemel his son, Zakki his son, Shami his son.
And Shami had sixteen sons and six daughters, and his brother did not have many children, and all their families did not increase until the children of Yehuda came to him.
And they dwelt with them in the towns of Beer Sheba, and in Mulada, and in Darath Shua
[Verse text missing in source]
[Verse text missing in source]
[Verse text missing in source]
And in Alha and in Etsrem and in Elathlad.
And in Ansel and in Kherma and in Khatsragada and in Kheshmun and in Khethplet and in Tsnaqlag
And in Marmarana and Samsala, these towns until King David, and their villages.
And Aqim and Aqin and Remun and Athken and Ashen, five towns.

And all the hamlets that were around these towns of the Arqites, these were the towns of their habitation, and they had the great name of their beautiful habitation, and tranquility and peace was around them.
[Verse text missing in source]
And these are the names of the Princes who were there with their families, and in the house of their fathers.
And they came to the entrance of Gadar unto the east of the valley to seek pasture for their sheep.
And they found pasture that was good and lush, and land great and good, and tranquility and peace was there because of those dwelling in it from the first.

And these men who were written in the years of Hezekiah, King of the house of Yehuda, came and they destroyed their tents, and they polluted every spring of waters that was there until today, and they dwelt in their places, because the pasture of their sheep was very good.

And some of the sons of Shemon went to the Mount of Gabel, five hundred men, and these are the names of the men who went to their front: Pelatiah and Matitha and Rephaia and Uziel; these four men, the sons of Ishi, went to their front.
And they attacked with the sword all the rest of the Amalekites who were left, and they have dwelt in their place until today.

And the sons of Rubil, the firstborn of Israel, because he was the firstborn of his father and he despised the bed of his father, his birthright was given to Yoseph his brother, son of Israel, and upon these two came a blessing from all the tribes of Israel.
From Yehuda will come forth The King Messiah , and the right of the firstborn shall be given to Yoseph. .
The sons of Rubil, the firstborn of Israel: Khenuk and Palu and Khetsrun and Karmi.
And the sons of Karmi: Shemaiah his son, Duag his son, Shemei his son
Mikah his son, Uriah his son, Bela his son
Abdiah his son, whom TeglathPalasar, King of Assyria, took captive. He was the Prince of the tribe of the house of Rubil,
And his brothers with their families, and when they were written by genealogy in their lineage, their Chief Azrayl, the second, Zechariah
And Bela, son of Uzi, son of Shemei, son of Yoel, was dwelling in Aduir, and unto Nebu and to the Plain of Maun.
And to its East unto the borders of the wilderness that is from the river Euphrates, because they and their possessions increased in the land of Gelad.
And in the days of Shaul they made war with Arabians dwellers of Saka, and they were given into their hands, and they dwelt in their tents on all the face of the east of the land of Gelad.
And the children of Gad dwelt opposite them in the land of Mathnin, unto the border of Siba and of Salka.
And Yoel went out at their front, and he was judging them and he taught them beautiful scriptures.
[Verse text missing in source]
These are the sons of Abaykhel, son of Khur, son of Zarakh, son of Gelad, son of Makir
Son of Abdeil, son of Eli, Chiefs of their fathers.
And they dwelt in Mathnin and in its villages.
All of them were written by genealogy in the days of Yotham, King of the house of Yehuda, and in the days of Yorbaam, King of Israel.

And the sons of Rubil and Gad and half the tribe of Menashe, men holding a sword and shield, and shooters of the bow and acquainted with war, forty and four thousand and six hundred and sixty and six, these all went out into war.
And they made war with the dwellers of Saka.
And they were delivered into their hands, because they had prayed to LORD JEHOVAH in battle, and he paid attention to their voice, because they trusted upon him.
And they plundered many riches and property and fifty thousand camels and two hundred and fifty thousand sheep and two thousand male donkeys, and a hundred thousand souls of men.
Because many fell slain from their tents in battle, and they remained in their places by their tents.

And the children of half of the tribe of Menashe dwelt in the land of Mathnin unto the valley of Mount Hermon and unto Sair, the great Mountain of Hermon, they increased unto HarTeman, and they became great.

And these were the heads of the house of their fathers: Apha and Shub and Eldaa and Azrayel and Adumya and Uriyah and Khezayel, mighty men of the army, men of fame, for they were the heads of the house of their fathers.
And they denied the God of their fathers, and they went astray after gods of the nations of the Earth whom LORD JEHOVAH had destroyed from before them.

And LORD JEHOVAH God of Israel aroused the spirit of TeglathPalasar King of Assyria against them, and he led the tribe of the house of Rubil captive, and the tribe of the house of Gad, and the half tribe of Menashe, and he brought them to Khalakh and to Khabar the River, Guzan, cities of Media, and they dwell there until today.
The sons of Levi: Gershun and Qahath and Merari.
And the children of Qahath: Amram and Itshar and Khebron and Uzeil.
And the children of Amram: Moshe and Aaron and Maryam, and the children of Aaron: Nadab and Abihu and Eliazar and Ithamar.
And Eliazar begot Phinkhes, and Phinkhes begot Abishu.
And Abishu begot Abidar, and Abidar begot Azi.
And Azi begot Zerakhyah, and Zerakhyah begot Maru.
And Maru begot Amaryah, and Amaryah begot Akhitub.
And Akhitub begot Tsaduq, and Tsaduq begot Akhimets.
And Akhimets begot Azrayah, and Azrayah begot Yukhanan.
And Yukhanan begot Azaryah, and Azaryah ministered in the house that Solomon built in Jerusalem.
And Azaryah begot Amaryah, and Amaryah begot Akhitub.
And Akhitub begot Tsadoq, and Tsaduq begot Shalum.
And Shalum begot Khelaqyah, and Khelaqyah begot Azaryah.
And Azaryah begot Serayah, and Serayah begot Tsaduq.
And Tsaduq was taken captive when LORD JEHOVAH led the men of Yehuda and Jerusalem captive to Babel, to Nebukadnetsar.
The sons of Levi: Gershun and Qahath and Merari.
And these are names of the sons of Gershun: Lebni and Shemi.
And the sons of Qahath: Amram and Yatshar and Khebrun and Uziel.
And the sons of Marari: Makhli and Mushi; these are the generations of Levi for their fathers:
Gershun, his son Nakhath, his son Zema his son
Yuwakh his son, Adu his son, Yatur his son
The sons of Qahath: Aminadab his son, Qurakh his son, Asir his son
Halqanah his son, Akensaph his son
Takhuth his son, Uriel his son, Uzziah his son, Shaul his son.
And the sons of Halqana: Amshi and Akhimuth
Halqanah his son, Tsuph his son, Nakhath his son, Zarakh his son, Shemala his son, Mari his son
Eliab his son, Gadkhiel his son, Halqanah his son.
And the sons of Shamuyel: his firstborn son, Yuyel, and the name of his second son, Abia.
And the sons of Merari: Makhli his son, Lebni his son
Shemi his son, Aza his son, Shemaiah his son, Eshaia his son.
And these are all those whom David appointed that they would minister in the house of LORD JEHOVAH in the place where the Ark is set

And they would be serving before the Tabernacle in the Tent of Time, with hymns of great praise, until Solomon builds the house of LORD JEHOVAH which is in Jerusalem, and he appointed them to their occupations, according to their law.
And these and their sons of the sons of Qahath from the tribe of the Levites ministered: Haman and Yoel, son of Shemueil
Son of Halqanah, son of Yarkhum, son of Eliel, son of Takhu
The son of Tsuph, son of Halqanah, son of Khamath, son of Mushi
Son of Halqanah, son of Yuel, son of Azaryah, son of Zephnaya
Son of Takhath, son of Asir, son of Aksiph, son of Qurakh
Son of Yatshar, son of Qahath, son of Levi, son of Israel
And his brother Asaph who stood at the right of Asaph, son of Brakyah, son of Shemaiah.
Son of Mikayel, son of Masya, son of Melakya
Son of Athni, son of Zarakh, son of Araya
Son of Athan, son of Zema, son of Shemi
Son of Yukha, son of Gershun, son of Levi
The sons of Merari their brother who stood at the left side: Athan, son of Qishi, son of Amar, son of Maluk
Son of Kheshabya, son of Amutsaya, son of Khelaqya
Son of Natur
Son of Makhli, son of Mushi, son of Merari, son of Levi.
And their brothers the Levites who were giving to all the works of the house of the Tabernacle of the house of LORD JEHOVAH.

And Aaron and his sons offered sacrifices on the altar, and upon the altar of incense burners of sweet spices, all the work of the Holy of Holies, to make atonement for Israel as Moshe the Servant of LORD JEHOVAH commanded.
And these are the sons of Aaron: Eliazar his son, Phinkhes his son, Abishu his son
Baqi his son, Azi his son, Zerakhyah his son
Maru his son, Amaryah his son, Akhitub his son
Tsaduq his son, Akhimats his son.
And these are the names of the cities that came to the families in their borders: to the children of Aaron, the family of Qahath, because to them the first lot arrived.
And they gave them Khebruun in the land of Yehuda and all the hamlets that are around it that are in the land of Yehuda.
And all the fields of the cities that were around it they gave to Kalab, son of Yuphna.

And to the children of Aaron they gave all the cities of salvation. They gave to the Priests Khebron and its hamlets, and Lebna and its hamlets, and Yathar and its hamlets, and Lekhem and its hamlets, and Eshtemu and its hamlets
And Debir and its hamlets
And Ashan and its hamlets, and Etra and its hamlets, and Bayth Shemesh and its hamlets.
And from the tribe of Benyamin they gave Gebah and its hamlets, Almuth and its hamlets, Nathuth and its hamlets, all the cities were thirteen for their families.
And to the children of Qahath who inherited from the tribe of the family of Menashe, ten cities in number.

And for the children of Gershun for their families, from the tribe of Issakar, and from the tribe of the house of Ashir, and from the tribe of Menashe, and from the tribe of the house of Naphtali, thirteen cities in number.

To the children of Marari for their families, from the tribe of the house of Rubil, and from the tribe of the children of Gad, and from the tribe of the children of Zabalaun, thirteen cities in number.
And the children of Israel gave the Levites these cities and their hamlets.

And they gave by lots from the tribe of the house of Yehuda, and from the tribe of the children of Shemon, and from the tribe of Benyamin these cities that were themselves called by the name of the families of the children of Qahath.
And the cities and their borders were from the tribe of the house of Aphreim.
And they gave them the cities of salvation: Shecheem and its villages in the mountain of the tribe of the house of Aphreim, and Yazir and its hamlets
And Bayth Khuran and its hamlets
And Alilun and its hamlets, and Gethremun and its hamlets.
And from half of the tribe of Menashe they gave Anath and its hamlets, and Yablin and its hamlets, and to the tribe of the children of Qahath that they inherited.
To the children of Gershun from half of the tribe of Menashe: Gulan that is in Mathnin and its hamlets and Ashtur and its hamlets.
And from the tribe of Issakar: Raqem and its hamlets, Dabruth and its hamlets
Ilam and its hamlets.
And from the tribe of the house of Ashir: Mashal and its hamlets, Ebrun and its hamlets
Qiq and its hamlets, Dahab and its hamlets.
And from the tribe of the house of Naphtali: Reqem that is in Galila and its hamlets, Hamun and its hamlets, Quryathim and its hamlets.
And from the tribe of the house of Merari that they inherited from the tribe of the house of Zabalaun: Armuni and its hamlets, Tabor and its hamlets.

And at the crossing of Jordan of Yerikho, from the east of the Jordan, from the tribe of the house of Rubil: Butsar in the wilderness and its hamlets, Mepuphath and its hamlets, Yahats and its hamlets
Qadmuth and its hamlets, Muphath and its hamlets
Ramuth and its hamlets, Makhlam and its hamlets
Yezir and its hamlets, Kheshbon and its hamlets.
The sons of Issakar: Tula and Phua and Shuub and Shemroon, these his four sons.

The sons of Tula: Azi, Arphaya, Yuyel, Lakhmi and Yabsam and Shamuyel, the Chiefs among their fathers. These are the sons of Tula, mighty men of power for their families in their generations by their number, and in the days of David, twenty and two thousand and six hundred.
And the sons of Uuz: Izarkhan; and the sons of Izarkhan: Malkayel and Oubdaya and Yuel and Ishwa, four Chiefs, all of them.
Their families for the house of their fathers, mighty men of the army and makers of war, thirty and six thousand, because they increased, and their wives and their children.
And their brothers, all the families of the house of Issakar, mighty men of the army: eighty and seven thousand.
And these are the names of the sons of Benjamin: Bela and Akbar and Ashkuul and Gaara and Naaman and Akhi and Aruush and Maphi and Khapheem and Adar.

And the sons of Baala: Atsbuun and Azi and Zayel and Yarmuth and Azri five Chiefs of the house of their father, mighty men of the army, and the number of them was twenty and two thousand and thirty and four.
And the sons of Akbar: Zemura and Yawush and Eliazar and Elani and Amri and Yarmuth and Abia and Anathuth and Almuth; these all are the sons of Akbar.
And their number was for their families, for the Chiefs among their fathers to their generations, mighty men of the army, twenty and two thousand and two hundred.
And these are the sons of Ashkul: Balhan; and the sons of Balhan: Yawush and Benjamin and Ihur and the Canaanites, and Zaytuun and Tarshish and Akheshrakh.
All these are the sons of Ashkul, to the heads of their fathers for their families, mighty men of the army, seventeen thousand and two hundred who go out in the army of war.
And Shaphan and Khaphis, the sons of Gur, the sons of Khusha.
The sons of Naphtali: Yatshayel and Guni and Yatsar and Shalom, these are the sons of Balha.
The sons of Menashe whom his concubine gave birth to him: Isarkel and Aduniah, and she bore Mekir the father of Gelad.
And Makir took to him a wife, one daughter of a Prince, and the name of his sister was Maaka, and the name of his elder brother was Tsalphkhad, and Tsalphkhad had no sons, only daughters.
Also Maaka mother of Makir bore a son, and she called his name Peresh, and the name of his brother Sheresh, and the sons of Alum: Reqem.
And the sons of Reqem: Baran. These are the sons of Gelad, son of Makir, son of Manassheh.
And his sister Maaka gave birth to Ashkhur and Abiazar
And Shemira, and Alinun, and Shim and Etham.
And the sons of Aphreim: Shuthelakh, and Bakar his son, and Takhath his son, and Eldaa his son and Akhath his son
And Zabur his son, and Shultakh his son, and Lazar his son, the men, the sons of Gath who were born in the land. They had gone down to take their wealth.
And Aphreim their father was mourning many days, and his brothers came to comfort him and to put consolation in his heart.
And he came to unity with his wife, and she conceived and she bore a son, and she called his name Bria, because there was evil with him in his house
And his daughter who remained in Bayth Khuran, lower and upper.
And his daughter healed all these who remained, because she was a Nurse and she healed their sick.
And she healed Adan the son of Amihud.
Of the sons of Anun the father of Yehushba.
And their property and their beds in Bayth Eil and its hamlets, and Shekeem and its hamlets, and Anath and its hamlets.

And upon the borders of the sons of Manassheh: Bayth Shekeem and its hamlets, Tanak and its hamlets, Magdu and its hamlets, Dur and its hamlets, as these towns dwelt the children of Yoseph son of Israel.
The sons of Ashayr: Yamna and Shua and Shwi and Braya and Sarakh their sister.
And the sons of Bria: Khabar and Malkil, he who is the father of Barzith.
And Khaphar begot Phelet and Shamir and Khatum and Shu their sister.
And the sons of Phelet: Pasak and Bemhal and Ashut, and these are the sons of Palet.
The Peshitta lacks this verse.
The Peshitta lacks this verse.
The Peshitta lacks this verse.
The Peshitta lacks this verse.
The Peshitta lacks this verse.
Arakh and Khananayel.

These all are the sons of Ashir, Heads of the house of their fathers in their generations, mighty men of the army, Chiefs of the Princes, for their accounting in the army of war was by their number, twenty and six thousand men.
And Benjamin begot Baala his firstborn, Akbar the second, and Ashkul the third
And Gera the fourth, and Naman the fifth, and Akhi the sixth, and Aruush the seventh, and Mathim the eighth, and Khasim the ninth and Adar the tenth.
And sons to Baala were Abdu and Gera and Abihur
And Abishu and Naman and Akhia
And Shaphim and Khupham and Akhiram.
These are the sons of Abihur, and these were the Chiefs of their fathers for their families, and they were taken captive to the plain of Naaman.
The Peshitta omits this verse.
The Peshitta omits this verse.
And he begot from Kharash his wife, Yubab and Tsabya and Malkum.
And Menasheh and Sariyah and Yarmana and Tsabats, these are his sons, Chiefs of the fathers.
Makhshim begot Ikhubat and Eliphleg.
And the sons of Eliphleg: Ubir and Mashlam and Shamir, and he built Iyu and Lud and its villages.
And he was the head of the fathers over the dwellers of Gath.
Shishaq and Yarmuth.
And Zebadya and Azur and Adi.
And Mankel and Ansheephi and Ebrun and Zabdi.
