For they looked to the roads of the south and they waited for the paths of Sheba
They were ashamed in the thing that they trusted; they came to it and were ashamed
Also you have turned against me, because before you saw the earthquake you were afraid
For if had I said to you: “Give to me”, or “From your wealth offer a bribe for my sake
And deliver me from the hands of my oppressors”, or “Save me from the hand of a mighty man”
Teach me, and I shall be silent, and make me know what I have done wrong
Why do you reject the Word of truth, and who is it of you that humbles and rebukes?
Behold you will plan to correct words and you will consider my word against my spirit
Behold, you are magnified against orphans and you grieve your friend
And now, be instructed and pay attention, and I shall speak in front of you, and I shall not lie
Repent, I beg you now, and do not be as the evil. Repent therefore and be justified
Is evil in my tongue, or is my mouth not speaking truth?
Now there is time for a person on the Earth, and like the days of a hired man are his days
And like a Servant who waits for the shade, and like a hired man who waits to finish his work
In this way I have inherited for myself worthless months, and I had countless nights of pain
If I lie down, also I say, ‘when shall I arise?’ And I stretch out the evening and lie down, and I tremble until dawn
My flesh wears worms and my body, the dust; my skin is shriveled and rotted
My days are swifter than a spider’s web and they are consumed without hope
I will remember that The Spirit is life, and you shall turn my eyes back to see the goodness
The eye that sees me will not rejoice for me, and your eyes are on me, and I am not
As a cloud disappears and departs, in this way he who has descended to Sheol does not go up
And he does not return again to his house and he is not known again to his place
Also I shall not restrain my mouth, I shall speak in the grief of my spirit and I shall narrate in the bitterness of my soul
Am I the sea or a dragon that you post a watch over me?
Because I said, ‘you will comfort me and I shall take heart from the agony of my lying down’
And behold, you have disturbed me with dreams, and with visions you have terrified me
And you picked my soul out of destruction, and my bones from death
I have lost hope for myself and I am not living to eternity. Depart from me, for my days are empty!
What is man that you would destroy him and you would set your heart upon him?
And would you visit at dawn, and at that time would you test him?
And how long will you not leave me? Will you not leave me until I swallow my spittle?
If I have sinned, what shall I do to you, The Maker of Man? Why have you appointed me that I would meet with you, and you have been a burden to me?
And how long will you not forgive my debt and remove my sins? For now I shall lie down on the dust, and you will seek me, and I am not!”
And Bildad the Shukhite answered and said
How long will you speak these things and a proud spirit fill your mouth?
Will God practice evil judgment, or the Almighty One pervert the right?
If your children sinned against him, he sent them away in their sins
And if you seek God and you will beg The Almighty
If you are innocent and upright, now he will be attentive to you, and he shall restore the dwelling of your righteousness
And your beginning will be small and your end will be very great
Ask now about the first generations and discern the search of their fathers
For we are of yesterday; we know nothing, and our days on the Earth are like a shadow
And behold, those will teach you and they will say to you, and words will go forth from their heart:
“Does a reed grow in a thirsty place or papyrus reed spring up where there is no water?
While it is in its growth it is not picked, and before all grass it dries up
So are the ways of everyone who forgets God, and the hope of the pagan will be destroyed
And his trust will be cut down, and his house is the house of the spider
He will put trust on his house and it will not stand, and he will hold firmly and it will not be established
It is as green fruit before the sun
And the waves will dash against his foundation, and he will see a house of stone
If they uproot him from his place, and he will lie to him and shall say to him: “I did not see you”
Behold, he examines all their ways, and from the dust another will sprout
God does not despise the perfect and does not hold evil doers by the hand
Until your mouth will be filled with laughter and your lips with a song
Your haters will be clothed in shame and the tent of the wicked is not
And Job answered and said:
“Truly I know that it is so, and no man overcomes God
If he will desire to judge his people, not one of a thousand answers him
He is wise of heart and mighty in power; who is hardened against him and has peace?
He who removes mountains, does he not know? And he overturns them in his wrath!
He who shakes the Earth from its foundations and its inhabitants quake
He who spoke to the sun and it did not rise, and seals the surfaces of the stars
He who stretched out Heaven alone and treads on the power of the sea
He who made Pleiades and the star Aldebaran and the Giant Orion and the circuit to the south
He who made great things without limit and mighty things without number
Behold, if He passes by me, I do not see him, and if he surrounds me, I do not discern him
Behold, if he destroys, who would command with his hand and who will say to him: ‘what are you doing?’
God does not turn back his anger, and under him the helpers of many will be humbled
Also I shall answer him and I shall set my words in order before him
If I am not justified, I will not be heard, and to my Judge I shall make supplication
If I called and he answered me, still I would not believe that he heard my voice
For with power he spares me in every hair of my head, and he increases my wounds without charge
And he has not left me that my spirit may rest, because bitterness filled me, and wormwood made me drunk
If one is strong in power, he is, and if he is for judgment, who is going to appease him?
If I shall claim innocence, my mouth will condemn me. I am perfect and I am perverted
I am perfect and I do not know my soul. I have despised my life.
It is one, because of this I have said: ‘He is destroying the righteous and the wicked.’
If his rod kills suddenly, also he is mocking the senselessness of the innocent
The Earth he has given into the hand of The Wicked One and the faces of Judges are covered, but who endures his anger?
My days are faster than a runner; they flee away and they see no good
They have passed by with the many ships of an enemy like an eagle that flies upon its food
With my bitterness I have forgotten my story; if I shall leave my meditation or my words, I am grieved
And if I rest, I am afraid of every torment, because I know that you do not declare me innocent
Behold, if I will be condemned, why do you destroy me as if it were nothing?
And if I am washed in water of snow and I am cleansed in the purity of my hands
Then you will sink me into a ditch, and my garments will stay away from me
Because he is not a man like me that I would give him an answer, and we would enter as one to judgment
But, oh that there would be a reprover between us that he would lay his hand on the mouth of us both!
And he would remove his rod from me, and his terror would not shake me
I would speak and I would not be afraid of him, because I have not been against him
My soul is grieved with my life. I reasoned in my reasoning and I shall speak in the bitterness of my soul
And I shall say to God: “Do not condemn me; teach me about what it is for which you judge me
Is it not enough to you that you treat unjustly and you despise the work of your hands, and you look upon the mind of the wicked?
Are your eyes as the eyes of flesh, or are you seeing like a man sees?
Or are your days as the days of man, or your years as the days of a man
That you inquire about my debts and about my sins?
And you know that I am innocent, and there is none who is delivered from your hands
Your hands labored in me and made me, and after this you choose to condemn me and to sink me!
Remember that you have made me like clay and you return me to the dust
And you churned me like milk and you curdled me like cheese
In skin and flesh you have clothed me, and with muscles and with bones you have strengthened me
And life and peace you have made with me, and my spirit has kept your commandments
And these things you have hidden in your heart; I have known that this was in your mind
For if I have sinned, you keep watch over me, and from my sins you do not declare me innocent
If I have sinned, woe to me! And if I am justified, I shall not lift my head. I am filled with disgrace and I have seen affliction!
And if I will rise up as a lion, you will hunt me and turn like a giant against me
And you oppose your weapon against me and multiply your wrath toward me, and you spring armies against me
And why have you brought me out from the womb? I might have died and an eye would not have seen me
And I had been as if I had not been, and from the womb to the tomb they would have carried me
Few are the days of my life. Depart from me and I shall be quiet and I shall be comforted a little
Before I shall go and I shall not return again, to the land of darkness and of the shadows of death
Of wasting away as deep darkness and shadows of death, and there are no ranks of generations in it, and there is languishing as deep darkness
And Tsuphar the Namthite answered and said:
“Do not answer with a multitude of words, also no man speaking is justified with his answer
Behold, at your words the dead may be silent; you speak and there is none who restrains you, and if you mock, there is none who rebukes you
And you said that you have been led righteously and you have been pure in the eyes of your soul
However the Maker, the Giver, is God the Word, and his lips are lifted up with you.
And he was showing you the secrets of wisdom, because there are inner chambers to wisdom, and you have known that God has forgiven you your sins
Can you know the declaration of God, or can you stand at the uttermost limit of The Almighty?
Do you know the high place of Heaven or the depth of Sheol? From where do you know?
Its measure is longer than the Earth and it is broader than the sea
If he will pass by, he shall seize and he shall gather; who turns him?
For he knows when time came to be, and he sees evil and he considers
And a man who is pure is encouraged, and he who is mighty helps a man
If you have prepared your heart and you have stretched forth your hands to him
If there was evil in your hands, he was removing it far away, and evil did not camp in your tent
And then you will lift your hands and you will not be afraid of sorrow
And then you shall forget your misery, and like running water you shall be led away
And at noon the grave shall rise up, and thick darkness will be like the dawn
And you will trust that there is hope and you will sleep and you will be rested
And there is none who rouses you up. Many will seek your face
And the eyes of the wicked will be darkened and their strength will be destroyed from them, and the hope of their souls
And Job answered and said:
“In truth, you are the people, and wisdom will die with you!
I also have a heart like you, and I have not been less than you who have such things as these
And there was laughter with his neighbors, and he called to God and he answered him, because he is pleased with righteous men without blemish
To remove madness and evil and to restore a disjointed foot
The tents of robbers and the trust of those who are angry with God will be removed, because there is no God in their heart
Or else ask the beast and it will teach you, and the bird of the sky and it will show you
Or tell the earth and it will teach you and the fish of the sea will tell you
Who is it that does not know all these things, that the hand of LORD JEHOVAH has made these things?
Because in his hand are the souls of everything that lives and the spirit of all flesh and man
The ear hears words and the palate tastes food
And in the elders is wisdom and in length of days is discernment
With him is wisdom and heroism; his is intelligence and understanding
Behold if He casts down, who builds up, and if he shuts up the face of a man, who opens?
If he rebukes the waters, they dry up, and if he sends them out, they overturn the land
His is power and wisdom; his is strength and salvation
He governs Kings with awe and Judges he drives mad
He brings down Kings to the gates and binds a belt on their waist
He governs the Priests with awe and he brings down mighty men
He removes speech from Eunuchs and the discretion of old men he takes away
He pours madness on Rulers and the courage of the mighty he weakens
He reveals deep things from darkness and brings out the light from the shadows of death
He deceives the nations and scatters them. He strikes down the nations and destroys them. He spreads out the peoples and he leaves them
He intoxicates the heart of the Leaders of the people of the Earth and causes them to wander in a trackless waste without a road
They will search in darkness and not in the light, and they will grope in the darkness and not the light, and he inebriates them like drunkards
Behold, my eyes saw all these things, and my ear heard and I considered
I know according to your knowledge also, and I have not been inferior to you
However, I am speaking to The Almighty One, and I shall speak my reproof to God
However, you are speaking falsehood, and you are healers of nothing
He, The Maker, is silent; you should be silent, and let this be for your wisdom
Hear, therefore, my reproof, and obey the judgment of my lips
You speak evil about God and do you speak deceit about him?
Do you accept his person, or do you judge with God?
It is good for you that he investigates you, if as a man is judged you are judged by him in all things
He shall surely reprove you if you will accept his person!
His dread is your punishment and his terror will fall on you
Remember that your power is from the dust, and your lofty dwelling is upon a bank of clay
Be silent before me and I shall speak also, and I will tell everything that has passed against me
Why do I take up my flesh into my teeth, and my soul is put into my hands?
If he will kill me, it is for him alone I look, because my ways are before him
Also he shall be to me The Savior, because a phony does not come into his presence
Listen to hear my words and I shall tell my story before you
Behold, also I bring my judgment and I know that I am innocent
Who is he that contends with me? For now I shall be silent and be at rest
However, two things do not turn away from me, and then I shall not turn aside from before you
Do not remove your hand from me, and do not let your awesomeness terrify me
Call me and I shall answer, or I shall speak and bring me an answer
How many debts and sins do I have? My debts and my sins teach me
Why do you turn your face from me and you consider me as an enemy to you?
Do you trample a dry leaf that falls, or do you chase a dry blade of grass?
For you have written instruction against me, and you remind me of the sin debts of my youth
And you set my feet in the stocks and have set a guard on all my ways, and shall you look upon the footprints of my feet?
And he is as a skin which wears out, and like clothing which a moth eats.
Thus is the man born of woman, and he is few of days and suffers terror
And like a flower he comes forth and dries up and fades and flees as a shadow and does not remain
Also on this one you opened your eyes, and will you bring him into judgment with you?
Who will render the pure from the defiled? Not even one!
If his days are cut off and the number of his months are circumcised, you have made the Law that he will not pass from it
And he will breathe until his days run out as a hired man
Because there is hope for a tree that if it is cut, it is renewed again; its offshoots are not lacking
And if its root grows old in the earth and its vegetation may die in the dust
From the scent of waters it will flourish and it will make leaves as a plant
And a man dies and wastes away and a son of man is finished and he is not
Waters disappear from the sea, and a river dwindles and dries up
And a man who falls asleep does not rise until the Heavens wear out; they will not be awakened and they do not break out from their sleep
But The Maker who is in Sheol has hidden me and concealed me until your wrath had passed, but you set a resurrection for me and you remembered me
If a man dies does he live? And all the days of his youth he waits until his old age comes
If you call me I shall answer you, and upon the work of your hands I shall think
Because now, do not number my goings and do not hold my sins against me
Seal my debts in a bundle and remove my sins from me
Truly a mighty mountain falls or a precipice is being shaken from its place
The waters wear away stone or a clod of the dust of the Earth. You have destroyed the hope of man
You will prevail over him to eternity and he will walk in the shame of his face; you will cast him away
If his sons will be multiplied, he does not know, and if they diminish, it is not known to them
However his flesh upon him will sorrow, and his soul will mourn for him”
And Eliphaz the Taymanite answered and said:
“Will the wise of spirit answer in knowledge and anger fill his belly
To conceal words that are not useful and with words without profit in them?
Also you bring reverence to nothing and you multiply speaking before God
Because sin will teach your mouth and you delight in a tongue of deceit
Your mouth will condemn you and not I, and your lips will testify against you before me
Were you born before man or were you formed in the womb before the hills?
Have you heard the secret of LORD JEHOVAH, and is wisdom revealed to you?
What do you know that we do not know, or what do you discern that was not with us?
Behold, there are with us those who are old, also are with us those who are old and more ancient also than your father in days
Restrain from yourself the threatening of God and speak in comfort to your soul
Why is your heart lifted up and why do your eyes beckon us?
For you are lifting up your spirit to God and you bring out words from your mouth
Who is the man who is innocent, or who born of woman is going to be justified?
Behold, in his holy ones he does not trust, and the Heavens are not pure before him
Even if, rejected and afflicted, a man will drink evil as water
I shall show you, hear me, and what I have seen I shall repeat
What the wise have shown and have not hidden from their fathers
To them by themselves the Earth was given and no stranger passed among them
All the days of the wicked he magnifies himself and the number of his years will be kept by The Almighty One
The sound of terror is in his ears and in peace the Destroyer will come upon him
He does not believe that he is returning from darkness. He looks for the sword
He flees for the threat of judgment and wherever he is, he knows that the day of darkness is prepared at his hand
Suffering will terrify him and adversity will overtake him as a King who is prepared for battle
Because he lifted up his hand against God and was valiant against The Almighty
And he runs upon him with a high neck, and on the back of his neck with a multitude of rows of shields
And he covered his person with his fat and made Pleiades higher than the star Aldebaran
And he settled desolate cities that are uninhabited and is prepared for battle
He will not continue and his power shall not stand, and the words not will be exalted on the Earth
And they shall not depart from darkness and the flames will dry up his branches; he will be made insane by the spirit of his mouth
And he should not believe the lie of the Deceiver, because his growth was in vain
And because he does not dry up in his days and his hands are not found
He will uproot his body like a vine and will cast off his growth like an olive tree
Because the assembly of the heathen will be for the wilderness and for desolation, and fire will consume the tabernacle of the evil
They conceived evil and gave birth to depravity and their bellies are filled with deceit”
And Job answered and said:
“I have heard of many things like these, and you are comforters in evil, all of you!
Do not grieve my spirit with words. Even though you will speak to me, I am not answering
I also might speak like you, but, oh that your souls were treated in the place of my soul, and I had tested you with words and I would shake my head at you!
But I would have examined you in your words, and the speaking of my lips I would not have kept from you!
For if I shall speak, my sorrow is not removed, and I am if silent, who is relieving me?
Because now he troubles me and he keeps all my testimony
And he appointed me and I have become a witness and my lie stood against me, and I have spoken before him
His anger has broken me and torn me to pieces and his teeth gnashed on me. My enemies fixed their eyes on me
And they have opened their mouths against me with insult and they struck my cheeks together, and they have been filled with fury against me
He handed me over to the Evil Angel, and he handed me over to the hand of the Wicked One
I was silent and he struck me. He seized on my neck and shook me. He has set me up for himself as a target
And his arrows have increased against me, and he shot at my kidneys and showed no pity. He poured my bile on the ground!
He broke me breach within breach and he ran against me as upon a giant!
Sackcloth I put on my skin and I sprinkled my head with dust
My face is troubled from weeping and my eyes from the shadow of death
Because there is no evil in my hands and my prayer is pure
The Earth will not cover my blood and there shall be no place for my cry
And now, behold, in Heaven are my witnesses, and my acquaintances in the High Place
My brothers and my friends, my eyes overflow to God!
But, oh, that a son of man might blame God, as a man his neighbor!
For the number of his years are coming, and he goes the way without return!
My spirit is exhausted and my days have become extinct! The tomb is ready for me!
Because there is no falsehood with me, and in their bitterness my spirit spends the night
Set now my tent with you! Why is this entrusted into my hands?
Because you have hidden their heart from understanding, therefore they shall be exalted in their deception
And a friend will be lifted up over his friend, also the eyes of their sons will be darkened
He raised up the Ruler of the nations, and he created a veil over their faces
My eye aches from passion and all my senses are as a shadow
The upright will be amazed for this, and the innocent one will be emptied out for the guilty
The righteous shall hold his way and will grasp firmly, and he whose hands are clean will add to power
However, behold, all of you returned and you turned and you came to me, and I did not find among you one who is wise
Passers of days! Aborters of ideas! Destroyers of the hearts of people!
You put the night for day, and you sacrifice the light to the darkness
If I shall lie in ambush, my house is in Sheol, and I deserve my bed in darkness
To destruction I cried to the worm: ‘you are my father!’, and ‘my mother and my sister!’
‘Where is my hope and my trust from now on? And my hope, who shall find it?
Into the bottom to Sheol you will go down!’ And together to the dust they shall descend!”
And Beldad the Shukhite answered and said:
“How long will you put up opposition to words? Discern, and in the end we shall speak!
Why are we considered as a beast and we are defiled in your eyes?
He kills his soul in his wrath. Behold, because of you shall the Earth be forsaken, and a mountain move from its place?
Also the lamp of the wicked will be extinguished; also the flame of its fire will be put out.
The light will darken in their tents and its lamp will be extinguished over him
His steps will be laid waste in his sickness and his thoughts will sink him
Because he stretched his feet into a snare and he has walked upon a net
And snares grasped on his heel; droughts will prevail over him
His destruction is hidden in the earth and his snare shall wrap around on the path
Rows of terror surrounded him and they scattered themselves at his feet
And his affliction will be a famine, and ruin is prepared for his posterity
His possessions will be devoured by violence, and his first born dies by violence
His trust will be cut off from his tent and the terrors of Kings will terrify him
And they shall dwell in his tent, that which no man has; brimstone will be cast on his place
His roots will dry up from beneath and his vintage will fall off from above
His memorial will be destroyed from the Earth, and he has no name on the face of creation
He will be thrown out from light into darkness and from the world they will reject him
He has no name and no memory among the people and he has no survivor in his dwelling
Let the latter ones be stupefied at his days, and the hair of the first ones stands on end
However these are the tents of the evil, and this is the place of him who does not know God
And Job answered and said:
“How long will you grieve my soul and afflict me with words?
Behold, ten times you have rebuked me and you are unashamed that you sadden me
And if truly I have gone astray, my error will be with me
And if, from truth, you have magnified yourselves against me, and you have rebuked me and reproached me
Know therefore that God is my creditor, and he has spread his net over me
And if I wail, no man answers me, and if I shall make an appeal, there is none who redeems me
My ways he has fenced in that I may not pass, and laid darkness on my path
My glory he stripped from me, and has taken away the crown of my head
He has uprooted me from all my sides and I am gone, and he picked up my hope like wood
And his anger had grown strong against me, and he considers me as an enemy to him
And his Apostles together came against me and they disputed their ways against me and they camped around my tent
My brothers have gone far from me and my acquaintances have left
My neighbors are cut off from my presence and my acquaintances have forgotten me
Dwellers in my house and my maids consider me as a foreigner, and I am a stranger in their eyes
I called my Servant and he did not answer me, and I entreated him with the words of my mouth and I begged him
I have been a stranger to my wife and I have begged the son of my bowels
Also the evil abhorred me; I stood up and they spoke against me
All the counselors of my counsel have despised me, also my friends turned against me
My skin and my flesh is stuck on my bones and I have escaped by the skin of my teeth
Have pity on me! Have pity on me, you my friends, because it is the hand of God that has touched me!
Why will you persecute me, even you, like God, and with my flesh you will not be satisfied?
But, oh Creator, that my words were written and inscribed in a scroll
And with a pen of iron and with a pen of lead for eternity, and upon stone they would be inscribed!
And I know that my Redeemer is living, and in the end on the Earth he shall be revealed
And over my skin it is I will be surrounded with these things and over my flesh
If my eyes will see God, my kidneys see the light. It is ending, for I am ended by my resurrection
For you say: ‘why do we persecute him?’ For The Good Word has found occasion against me
Save yourselves from before the sword, because the wrath of sins is a sword, so that you will know there is a judgment!”
And Tsuphar the Namthite answered and said:
“Because my goings were discerned, and for my sake they persisted for me
The instruction of my reproof I shall hear, and the spirit of my understanding will answer me
This I have known from the world from when Adam was created on the Earth, from The Maker of man on the Earth
Because the boasting of the wicked from within them, and the rejoicing of the godless one until now is for a little time
If his height ascends to Heaven and his head will arrive to the clouds
As a hurricane he will be destroyed for eternity; those who saw him will say: ‘Where is he?’
And like a dream he will fly and will not be found, and he will flee as a vision of the night
The eye that saw him will not see him again, and they will consider his place no more
His sons will be broken in poverty and his hands he will reach out to his child
His bones are filled with marrow and will lie down with him on the Earth
If his evil will be sweet in his mouth and he will hide deceit under his tongue
He will show sympathy for it and he will not forsake it or restrain it within his darkness
His bread in his stomach will be turned, and the venom of The Dragon is within him
And the food that he swallowed he will vomit from his belly and God will destroy him
The venom of the asp he will pour out and the tongue of an asp will kill him
He will not see the tributaries of rivers, and of torrents of honey and of butter
He will return to labor and he will not swallow with strength; his barter he shall not enjoy
For he thought to forsake the poor, and a house of violence will not be built up
Because he did not know his judgment in his gut, and by his lusts he will not escape
There is none who escapes from his generations, therefore his good will not remain
In the measure that he measured he shall be paid, and every hand of the evil will come upon him
And his conception will be for destruction, and the heat of his anger will be prolonged in him and it will rain upon him with its warlike strength
For he fled from breastplates of iron, a bow of brass will shoot through him
It is drawn and it brings out his insides and the skin of his gall will be pierced; truly power is against him
And all darkness is hidden for his generations; fire will consume him that he will not breathe, and a survivor will do evil in his tabernacle
Heaven will reveal his sins and Earth will be avenged on him
The foundations of his house will be exposed, and he will be scourged in the day of rage
This is the portion of a wicked son of man from before God, and the inheritance that will fill him from The Highest”
And Job answered and said:
“Listen to hear my words and it will be understanding among you
Be patient with me and I shall speak, and after my words, mock on
I shall speak my words to a son of man: ‘why is my spirit distressed?
Return to me and marvel, and put your hands on your mouth!
For if I remembered I was disturbed, and terror seized my flesh
Because, why are the wicked alive and mighty men of power growing old?
Their seed are established before them, and their children in their sight
Their households are quieted of fear and there is no rod of God upon them
His ox breeds and does not bellow, and his cow brings forth and it is not destroyed
Their sons are firm as a flock, and their children dance
They picked up tambourines and harp and rejoiced to the voice of singing
They spend their days in prosperity, and quickly to Sheol they descend
And they were saying to God: ‘Depart from us’, and ‘We do not want to know your ways!’
And they were saying: ‘Who is God that we would serve him, or what do we gain that we should seek him?’
Behold, their benefit was not by their hands, and the thinking of the wicked is far from me
How often the lamps of the wicked will be extinguished, and ruin will come upon them! He will distribute corruption in his anger!
And they shall be as straw before the wind and like chaff that a tornado picks up!
God keeps his evil for his children and he will pay him and he will know
His eyes will see his destruction, and he will drink from the anger of God
What is the pleasure in his house after him, and the number of his months kept for his children?
Do you teach God knowledge? For he will judge the proud!
For if this one should die in the strength of full age, he is trusting and quiet
And his sides are filled with fat, and his bones are filled with marrow
That one will die in a soul of bitterness and not eat in prosperity
They lie down together on the Earth, and worms will cover them
Behold, I know your thoughts and the counsels that you are counseling about me
Because you have said: ‘Where is the house of the righteous one, and what is the dwelling of the tent of the wicked?’
For have you not asked passersby of the road, and have you not perceived their signs?
Because the evil is kept for the day of destruction and he is remembered for the day of rage
Who is showing him the way, and who is paying him what he did?
But he goes to the tomb and is kept for an accident
Deep caves of a torrent swallow him, and every person is dragged after him and before him without number
And how will you comfort me with futility, and have the answers of the reprobate given profit before me?”
And Eliphaz the Taymanite answered, and he said:
“Do you, a man, speak with God , that you are equal with him in wisdom?
And what is the gain that you perfect your ways from your fear?
He will rebuke you and will enter into judgment with you
Behold, your evil is great and there is no end to your sin
Because you took the tent of your brothers for nothing and you stripped the garments of the naked
You gave no water for the weary to drink and from the hungry you have withheld bread
There is a man who sows the land for himself , and a lord will take it from him by force
A widow you have sent away with nothing and the arm of the orphan was brought low
Because of this, snares surround you and fear shakes you suddenly
From where the darkness is, you have not seen, and pouring forth of waters will cover you
Behold, God lifts up Heaven and he sees the top stars that are exalted
And you said: ‘What does God know? Will he judge within the thick darkness?’
Clouds are for his shelter and they do not see, and upon the orb of Heaven he walks
Have you kept the way of the world that the people of lying trod that was not in their time?
And at crossing of the river they were stopped and they did not remember him who laid their foundations
And they were saying to God: ‘depart from us, and we do not want to know your ways, and what does God do for us?’
And he fills their households with blessing and the scheming of the evil was kept far from them
The righteous shall see and they will rejoice and the innocent ones will mock at them
But they will be humbled from their hardness and fire will consume their remnant
But make an agreement with him and hand over some of your good crops
Accept the law from his mouth and he shall put his words in your heart
If you are converted to God, you will be built up, and if you will put evil far from your dwelling
And you will accumulate silver as the dust, and the gold of Ophir like the sand of the valley
And God will be for your help and you shall have accounting money
And you will lift your face to God
And you will pray before him and he will hear you and you will hand over your vows
And you will speak a word and it will happen to you, and upon your ways the light will dawn
Because he who is humbled, he says that he will be lifted up, and he whose his eyes are meek shall be redeemed
The innocent will escape where he is, and he will escape by the purity of his hands”
And Job answered and said:
“Also today my tale is bitter and his hand has been heavy on my groaning
But who makes it possible for me to arrive unto his seat?
And I shall set judgment in order before him and I shall fill my mouth with rebuke
And I had known what he answered me and I had understood what he said to me
In the abundance of power he judged with me, and otherwise there was awe laid upon me
There I would have judged justly with him, and I would have been justified, for I would have been allowed to escape in judgment
If he goes before me I do not know, or from behind him I do not perceive him
I looked at my left and he did not appear to me, and I turned to my right and I did not see him
He has known my way and my existence and he has tested me as gold, and I came forth
And my feet were established in his steps; his ways I have kept
And I have not despised the commands of his lips, and I have not departed from his pleasure. I have kept the words of his mouth
And in what one of these will he return to me, for his soul delights and he does it?
However, he completed his covenant, and there are many things like these with him
Because of this, I am afraid before him, I consider and I am shaken by him
Because God has shaken my heart and my head is shaken
Because I was not silenced by the presence of the darkness and by the presence of the veil of gloom
Why do the evil not hide themselves from the presence of God, and they are famous and they have not seen their days?
And the border they have removed and the flock they have taken
And male donkeys of orphans they have taken and they pawn the ox of a widow
The evil have hidden themselves by the road, and the meek of the Earth hide themselves together
As wild donkeys in the wilderness that rise early for food, for bread for their young
And in the field hay that is not theirs they will reap, and the vineyard of the wicked they will gather
Spending the night naked for lack of clothing and without a cloak in the cold
From the downpour of the mountains they will be wet and from lack of refuge, they will embrace stones
They seize some pillage of orphans, and on the poor they set desolations
Naked they walked for lack of clothing and the hungry picked up bread
And in the house of banquets they stoop and are hungry while carrying a basket and a measure, and in time they will hunger in their feasts; treading they will tread and be thirsty
From within the city the dying will groan, and the soul of the slain will cry out, and God will not receive their prayer
Those have been in lofty dwellings of light and they did not know his ways, and they did not walk in his steps
In the light stands a killer and he kills the poor and the afflicted and is as a thief by night
And eyes of adulterers watch for darkness and they say: “Eyes will not see”, and he conceals his face in darkness
He breaks into houses in darkness and by day marks them so that they will not see the light
Therefore they sought for themselves the shadow of death, and they found it, and the shadows of death terrified them
Quickly and upon the face of the waters will be carried away their portion in the earth, in the road of the vine dressers
Drought and heat will seize the waters of the snow, into Sheol they have sinned
And they were forgotten from the womb; the worm sucked them and the dead will not be remembered again, and the evil one will be broken like wood
The barren evil woman shall not bear; they have not done good to the widow
And the property of a mighty one shall stand by his power, and he will not believe in a vision
And he will dwell in quietness and his eyes will be confident also on their ways
They will be lifted up a little and they are not, and they will be humbled and all the provokers will be destroyed, and like the head of blades of wheat they dry out and will be crumbled
And if it was not his anger, he will convict me of lying, and my word shall be considered before God”
And Beldad the Shukhite answered and said:
“Authority and awe are with him. He made peace in his high places
And there is no computation to his power, and upon whom does his light not dawn?
And how can a son of man be justified by God, or how is one born of a woman righteous?
Behold, the moon cannot be pure and the stars are not pure before him
But truly man is a worm and a son of man is a maggot”
And Job answered and said:
“Why are you a helper without power, and have you saved the arm without strength?
How have you counseled him who is without wisdom, and how much instruction have you made known?
To whom have you shown words and whose soul has proceeded from you?
Behold the Giants shall be killed and will be quieter than the waters
Sheol is naked before him and there is no covering for destruction
He has stretched out the North from the waste place and has suspended the Earth on nothing
He has bound the waters in his clouds and a cloud does not burst under them
He has seized on the face of the covering and spread a cloud over it
He drew a circle on the face of the waters, unto the ends of the light with the darkness
The pillars of Heaven shake and shall be astonished at his rebuke
By his power he rebukes the sea and in his wisdom he gives life to the multitude
By his Spirit he governs Heaven, and his hand killed the Serpent which fled
Behold these all are from the tip of his ways, and what evil statement has been heard about him, and the immensity of his heroism who comprehends?
And Job added to speak his parable and said:
“Living is God who has twisted up my judgment and The Almighty who spoke to my soul
“All of whom is in me and The Spirit of God is in my nostrils
My lips will not speak evil and my tongue will not speak deceit
God forbid me until I shall die, my integrity will not depart from me!
And I shall hold fast to my righteousness and I shall not let it go, and my heart does not chastise me from my days
And my enemies will be as the wicked and those who hate me, as the evil
What is the hope of the heathen who possesses riches at the time when God takes his soul?
Because God does not hear the voice of his prayer when evil will come upon him
But if he will trust on The Almighty and he will cry out to God at all times, God will answer him and will listen to him
But I deliver you into the hand of God that your works are not hidden from him
Behold, all of you have seen, and why are you magnifying yourselves for nothing?
Behold, this is the portion of a wicked son of man from the presence of God, and they shall receive the inheritance of evildoers that is from The Almighty One
If their children will be multiplied for the sword and their offspring, they will not be filled with bread
And their survivors will be buried in death and their widows will not weep
If they will gather money like dust, and would prepare clothing like clay
Those will prepare and the righteous one will wear and he will divide their money
For he builds his house like spiders, and he made his tabernacle like a booth
Because the rich will lie down and he will not be rising again; he opens his eyes, and he is not
Trouble will overtake him like waters and The Spirit in the night like a flying tempest will take him away
And he will go and it will take him away from his place
And he will cast him out without pity, and by his hand he puts to flight, and he will scatter
His hand will strike upon them and he will hiss at them from his place
Because there is silver coming forth and the place where gold pours out
And iron is melted from the dust, and from the rocks copper is melted
He set an end for darkness and he knows the end to everything, the stone of a deep pit and the shadow of death
A mountain pass they inherited from alien people; they have strayed from the torrents and are cut off from man
They lay bare the Earth from which food came forth, and under it is turned up as fire
And the place of sapphire, its stone, and the pouring out of gold in its paths
A bird of prey has not known it, and the eye of the vulture has not seen it
And animals have not trodden it, and the lion does not cross over it
His hand reaches on the rock of flints and overturns the mountains from their roots
In his might he divides rivers and his eye has seen every precious thing
And the strength of rivers he had shut in, and from the veil went out light
And where shall his wisdom be found, and what is the place of understanding?
No man knows her treasury, and it is not found except in the place of life
The depths said: ‘It was not in me!”, and the sea said: ‘It is not with me!’
Gold is not given in exchange for it, neither is silver counted for its price
It is not exchanged in gold of Ophir, or in beryl, or in sapphire
Gold does not compare to it, or crystal, or precious pearls, neither do gems or jewels compare to it
Better is wisdom than precious stones; agate does not compare to it, neither with emeralds nor with chalcedony is it exchanged, for wisdom is better than everything, and nothing compares to it
The pearls of KushEthiopia and the stones of the ephod are not comparable to it
From where comes wisdom, and what is the place of understanding?
It is covered to the eyes of everything that lives, and from the bird of the sky it is hidden
Destruction and death said: ‘we have heard its report with our ears’
Because God is discerning its ways and he knows its place
Because he sees the end of everything that is in the Earth, and he sees under all Heaven
Because he made a scale for the wind and has prepared a weight with a measure
He made a law for the rain and a way for the sights of loud noise
Then he saw it and he told it, he weighed it, also he examined it
And he said to a son of man: ‘the awe of God is wisdom, and to depart from evil is understanding’”
And Job continued to tell his parable, and said:
“But who will make me as the first months and like the days when God preserved me?
His awe he made above my head in spreading his lamp over me, and by his light I walked in the dark
As I was in the days of my favor when God was resting over my tent
And when The Almighty was surrounding my youth
[Verse text missing in source]
When I proceeded to the gate and I called, and I sat in the market as the afflicted
Young men saw me and they hid themselves, and the Elders stood and were amazed at me
And Princes were restrained in words, and they placed their hands on their mouths
The voices of Leaders were concealed, and their tongue stuck to the roof of their jaws
Because the ear that heard praised me, and he whom I saw testified to me
Because I saved the poor from suffering, and the orphan who had no helper
The blessing of the perishing would enter upon me, and the heart of the widow was gladdened
I wore truth and it clothed me as a mantle and a crown of judgment
I have been eyes to the blind and feet to the crippled
I was a father to the afflicted, and judgment that I had not known I searched out
I have broken the molars of the evil man and I took the prey from between his teeth
And I said to the poor people: ‘I shall deliver’, and I shall end like a reed stalk, and like the sand of the seas I shall multiply my days
My roots are planted by the waters and the dew will stay, and in harvest I shall be invited
It was held with me, also my bow is renewed in my hands
And if they heard me and they hoped and they heard my advice
And from my words they have not erred and my word was pleasing to them
They waited for me as for the rain and their mouths they opened wide as for spring rain
I shall laugh at them and they will not believe, and in the light of my face they will not walk
I shall search their ways and I shall return and I shall dwell as a King among his army and like a man who comforts mourners
And now, behold, those that are inferior to me have laughed at me
In those days that I despised their fathers and I did not value them with the dogs of my flocks
And the power of their hands, why did it go to me?
For all strength was lost upon them
And with force he will seek for them like a thief with the strength of a torrent
To dwell in caverns of dust and stones
And under cliffs and under thorn bushes
The foolish children will be overthrown with evil children; there they will be leveled from the Earth
And now I have been their meditation, and I have been a horror to them
They despise me and are removed far from me and they did not restrain spitting from my face
Because they started after me and they humiliated me, and they put a bridle in my mouth
On my right they stood up and they tripped up my feet and they confused me in the path of their ways
And they entangled my steps for nothing, and they rejoiced for whatever happened to me, and they will have no helper
As a great ruin will come, and they will be broken under a hurricane
For they turned trouble against me and pursued my path like the wind, and my salvation is passing like a cloud
And now my soul is troubled, and the days of my affliction have surrounded me
In the night my bones weigh upon me, and my body does not rest in an abundance of power
I am clothed with garments and I am girded in my tunic
They likened me to mud and I am compared to dust and ashes
I shall cry to you and you will not answer me; I stood and you noticed me
And you have made me an enemy to you, and with the strength of your hands you have bound me
And you have picked me up and you caused me to lie down on the wind, and you afflicted me and you made me wretched
Therefore I know that you will return me from death to the house and the meeting of All Life
However, he will not reach his hand against me, and whenever I have cried out to him, he will save me
I wept for the poor by day, and my soul was troubled for the afflicted one
I have waited for good and evil came; I have waited for the light, and blackness came
My bowels were alarmed and did not cease, the days of my affliction went before me
I have walked sadly without anger, I stood in the assembly and I wept
I have been a brother to jackals, and a friend to the daughters of ostriches
My skin is shrunken against me, and my bones are warped as if by parching heat
My harp was for grieving, and my song for the voice of weeping
I have established a covenant for my eyes that I shall not take notice of a virgin
And what is the portion of God from above, and the inheritance of God from the high place
But ruin to the evil and alienation upon the Servants of evil?
He sees my ways and he counts all my goings
If I have walked with deceivers and if my feet hasten to run in treachery
He will weigh me in a balance of truth and God shall know my integrity
If I have turned my goings away from the road, and if my heart goes after my eyes, and if anything is stuck in me
But if I have sown then I ate, and if I planted, then I raised and I stored up
And if my heart was enticed by an estranged woman, or if I lay in wait at the door of my neighbor
Unless my wife has ground meal for others and she has baked in another place
Because this is sin, and this is an eye that is of contrivance
And it is fire that consumes unto destruction, and all my crops would be uprooted
If I have despised the justice of my Servants or of my Maidservants when they have been contending with me
But I have said, ‘what shall I do when God stands up and when he commands, what I shall return to him?’
Behold, in the womb The One who made me made him, and in one womb he formed us
If I have withheld from the poor a thing that he desired, or if I have darkened the eyes of a widow
If I ate my bread alone and the orphan did not eat of it
Because sorrows from my youth raised me and groanings from the belly of my mother
If I have seen one lost who was without clothing, and the afflicted without a cloak
If they were not reared on my knees or warmed from the wool of my sheep
If I have lifted my hands against the orphan, (but whenever I saw him at the door I helped him)
My shoulder will fall from its socket and my arm will be broken from its shoulder
Because the awe of God shook me and his ruin came against me, and I could not stand from terror
If I have made gold my trust and if I have said to precious stones: ‘you are my trust’, and if I have said to pure gold, ‘you are my hope’
And if I was rejoicing when my property increased and when my hands found much
If I have seen light when peaceful and the moon when shining
If I go and my heart is enticed in secret and I have kissed my hands with my mouth
Also he sees all my devices, if I have lied before God
And if I am gladdened in the ruin of my hater or if I have celebrated when evil happened to him
And I have not yielded my mouth to sin and my soul has not asked for one of these things
But my friends said: ‘but who gave us of his flesh and we are not satisfied!’
I have not made a stranger lodge in the street and I opened my door to the wayfarer
If I have hidden my sins as a man and if I have hid my debts in a secret place

And if I have trampled the power of many, (but it is an abundance of families that ruined me) and a son of man I have not turned away at the gate, and I have not ruminated on the speech of the lips) the provocations of God have humbled me
But The Giver who hears me, if he is God, he will answer me, and he will write in a scroll his judgments of a man
But on my shoulder, I would take it up and I would have made it a crown for myself
I shall show him the number of my steps, and like a Ruler I would declare it
And the Earth will wail over me and its furrows will weep as one
If I consumed its power without expense, or if I have made the soul of the bitter drunk
Instead of wheat will come forth a dung heap, and instead of barley will come forth thorns.” And the statements of Job are finished
And these three men who had wanted the condemnation of Job were silent, because he was righteous in their eyes
And Elihu son of Barakiel, the Buzite, from the generation of Ramun, his anger was provoked against Job, for he justified himself more than God
And he was provoked against his three fellows because they were not able to give an answer to Job and to condemn him
And Elihu rebuked Job with words, because they were older than he in days
And Elihu saw that these three men were unable to give him an answer, and his anger was provoked
And Elihu, son of Barakiel the Buzite answered, and he said: “Because I am lesser than you in days, and you are Elders, because of this I was afraid and I was shaken to show you my knowledge
For I have said, ‘days are speaking and a multitude of years teach wisdom’
Truly a spirit is in a man and the breath of God is discernment to them
It is not therefore abundance of days that makes wise, neither is it the old ones who discern judgment
Because of this, I say: ‘hear me, and also I shall show you my knowledge’
Behold, I kept silent for you and for your words, and I paid attention until you finished and I examined you with words
And I discerned your testimony, and behold, there is no reprover for Job, or one of you giving him an answer
For you say, ‘we have found wisdom; God is he who has wounded him, and not a son of man’
I will not speak contradictory words, and I do not offer him your counsel”
They stopped and they did not answer him; they abstained from words and they kept silent
Because they were not speaking, therefore they rose up and they did not answer him again
“I shall answer also with my word, and I shall show also my knowledge
Because I am filled with words and The Spirit is poured out into my belly
Behold, my belly is pained, and is not opened, and like Ab – in the month of harvest, it bursts
I shall speak and it will relieve me; I shall open my lips and I shall give an answer
I shall not accept the face of a man, and I shall not be ashamed of the face of a man
Because I do not know shame, or in a little while my Strength would have taken me away
However, hear Job, my words, and give ear to all my words
Behold, I have opened my mouth and in my mouth my tongue speaks
Upright is the speaking of my mouth and chosen is the speech of my lips
The Spirit of God has awakened me and the breath of God has given me life
If can you return an answer, be ready and stand before me
I, like you, am with God, also I was formed from clay
And now my dread will not shake you and my anxiety will not be a burden for you
However, you have spoken in my ear and the voice of your words I have heard
For you have said: I am innocent without debts, and I am righteous, and there is no sin in me, and I am free from evil
Behold, he finds occasions against me and he counts me as an enemy to him
And he laid my feet in the stocks and he watches in all my ways
In this you cannot be justified; I shall give you an answer: ‘God is greater than man’
Why have you sued with him, and in all his statements he has not answered?
Because God speaks with one and does not do more with two, and by the lips he is not understood
In a dream and in a vision of the night, when sleep falls on man in slumber on a couch
Then he opens the ear of a man, and in their revolt he will humble them
To cause a man to pass from his works and to hide the body of a man
He will save his soul from corruption and his life from destruction
And he will hide in sickness on his bed and the multitude of his bones will be sore
His flesh will waste away from his fear, and his soul will not be satisfied with bread, and his soul will lust for food
He will consume his flesh from fear, and the multitude of his bones he will not see
And his soul will come near to destruction and his life to death
If he has an Angel that hears him, one of a thousand, he will show to the man his uprightness
And he will show compassion upon him and will say: ‘Save him, that he will not descend to destruction and he will find salvation!’
His flesh shall be transformed as that in his boyhood, and he shall return to the days of his youth
And he will pray before God and he will answer him, and will be pleased with him and will appear before him in glory, and he will award to a son of man his righteousness
And Uprightness will speak concerning a man, and he will say: ‘I have sinned, and truly I have offended, and my soul has not prospered
Save my soul from the destruction of corruption, and my soul will see light!’
All these things God does three times with a man
He returns his soul from corruption to see the light of life
Give ear, Job, and hear, and be silent and I shall speak
If you have words, bring them to me, because it is desired that you be innocent
And if not, hear me, be still, and I shall teach you wisdom”
And Elihu answered and said:
“Hear my words, wise men, and those who know, pay attention to me
Because the ear tests words and the palate tastes food
We will select a Judge for us and he will know what is beautiful between us
You have said, Job, that you are innocent, for you said: ‘I am justified and God has turned my judgment against me!’
Who is the man who is destroyed without an offense?
Who is a man like Job that would drink mockery like water?
And he was a partner and a friend to workers of evil, walking with men who are sinners
You said that a man is not just when he is a worshiper of God
Because of this, men of heart, hear me; God forbid God that he would do evil and God forbid that he would commit sin!
According to the works of a son of man he pays him, and a man finds according to his ways
Truly God does no evil, neither does God pervert the way
Who commanded the Earth and made all the world?
If he turns his heart against it, his spirit and his soul are gathered to him
All flesh ends as one, and a son of man returns to his dust
And if you understand, hear these things and give ear to the voice of my words
Whoever hates judgment is unjust, and if one is innocent, he is not justly condemned
He who says evil about a King, a King of Rulers, a King of Governors
Who will not accept the faces of Princes and will not accept the faces of the poor, because all of them are the work of his hands
Suddenly they die, and in the middle of the night are consumed and are destroyed and they see The Almighty One, not in power
Because his eyes are on all the ways of a man and he sees all his steps
Without darkness and without shadows of death for the Servants of evil to hide themselves there
Because again, a man was not appointed to walk with God in judgment
For he has brought evil to many without limit, and we will live together in their place
Because of this, he shall know them from their works, and he shall turn them away into the night
And their works will be leveled under evil in the place of power
For they turned away from after him, and they did not understand all his ways
The prayer of the poor approaches before him, and the cry of the afflicted he hears
And if he forgives, who is condemned, and if he turns his face, who is forgiving the people or a son of man together?
Because a changeable man does not reign, or a sinner over the people
Because God said: ‘I have forgiven; I do not destroy without sins’
Teach me if you have done evil
I shall not again give you an answer, because you have sinned, for you are examined and not I; anything that you know, speak
Men of heart will speak to me, and wise man will listen to me
Job was not speaking in knowledge, and his words were not with intelligence
Truly Job has been tested unto the limit, and he is not considered evil by men
And if he adds foolishness to his sins among us, he will be commanded and he will bring his words before God
And Elihu answered and said:
Were you considered within justice in this, for you said ‘I am more righteous than God?’
If you spoke, what did it avail you for your soul? And what use that I would condemn you?
I shall return you words, also to your friends with you
Gaze into Heaven and search in the clouds that are higher than you
If you sin, what will you do to him, and if your evil increases, what will you do to him?
And if you have been righteous, what do you profit him, or what does he receive from you?
Your evil is to your soul, and to your soul is your righteousness
From the abundance of oppression they speak, and they cry out from much evil
And they do not say: ‘where is God who made us, who gives ideas in the night?
More than before the animal of the Earth and more than the bird of the air, he teaches us wisdom
There they cry out and he does not answer them, because of the pride of the evil
God does not hear uselessly, neither does God praise such
Even though you have said: “You shall not praise him; sue before him and beg him”
Now he does not command his anger and he does no evil to the soul
And Job uselessly opened his mouth and multiplies words without knowledge
And Elihu added and said:
“Wait for me a little and I shall show you, because there are more to God words
I shall take my knowledge from a distance, and to my Maker I shall give the right
However, my words truly have been revealed, and my doctrine is without defect with you
Behold, God is mighty; he does not despise him that is pure as milk
The evil has no life, and he gives justice to the afflicted
For the righteous does not fail from before his eyes; he puts Kings on the throne, and they are lifted up for eternities
And if are they bound in chains, they descend by corruption to poverty
And he will show them their works and their crimes because they made themselves Masters
And he will open their ears for instruction and he shall say that they should return from evil
If they will hear and they will do, also their days will be perfected in goodness, and their years in pleasures
And if they will not hear, they shall depart into destruction and they will come to an end without knowledge
And the changeable of heart shall be consumed, and they will not wail in passion; they will wail when he will bind them
And their soul shall die in youth, and they live in famine
The afflicted shall escape in his affliction, and he will open their way in oppression
Also he will deliver you from the mouth of the mocker, and a spacious place is substituted for distress, and he will set a table full of rich food
And he will judge the wicked entirely with justice, and with justice they will be held captive
He will not provoke you in the wrath of war, and he will not compel you with the abundance of salvation
He will be joined to you that he may save you, that you will not be distressed by any mighty in power
And he who removes one in the night also will give nations for your sake and peoples for the sake of your soul
Beware that you not be changed by evil; because of this, you are tested in poverty
God is mighty in power, who teaches like he?
Who commands concerning his ways, and who says to him: ‘You have done evil?’
Remember that his works are great, and all men praised him
And the children of men saw him and they looked from a distance
Behold, God is mighty and is not known, and the numbering of his years has no end
If he will number the pillars of Heaven and will bind the drops of rain by himself
That pour down the sky in time, and clouds sprinkle on the children of men, and they will rejoice greatly
And who will understand and spreads a cloud from the abundance of his tabernacle?
He spreads his light over them and he waters the covering of the sea
Because by them he will judge the nations and he will give food to the many
And with the hands he will cover the light, and he will go forth upon them that they may meet with him
He will show his possessions to his friends, also to the evil
Also, for this, his heart will be moved and will move from its place
Hear surely his voice in passion, the judgment that proceeds from his mouth
Under all Heaven they will praise him, and his light upon the edges of the Earth
In his place he will thunder with a voice of majesty, and he will not search them, because they hear his voice
And God will thunder with his voice; he does wonders, also great things, and he is unknown
Because he says to the snow: ‘Be on the Earth, and the rain and the great spring rain’
By every man he certifies to make his works known to every man
And the wild beast will enter into a lair and shall dwell in its den
From the inner chambers will come the whirlwind, and from a violent rain, the cold
And from the breath of God will be given ice crystal, and a multitude of waters going down
And gently are spread out the clouds, and he scatters the clouds of his light
And it turns around and it returns to perform counsel, everything that he commands them on the face of the world of his Earth
For if for the Leaders or if for the land or if for the holy that are found in it
Give ear to this, Job, and hear, and discern the wonders of God
Behold, do you know the thing that God appointed upon them and he has revealed the light of his clouds?
Do you know the going forth of clouds and the wonders of him who is without defect in knowledge?
Your garments will be warm and the Earth will move from the south
Will you spread out the mighty sky with him to uphold together?
Teach me what I shall say to him, and we shall not be destroyed from before the darkness
And I shall tell him what I shall say, and if a man will speak, he would be swallowed up
And now they do not see the light; it is illuminated in the sky of Heaven, and the Spirit will pass and purify them
From the North gold will come, and from God, light of The Almighty One
Great power and judgment and great innocent ones he will not answer
Because of this, the people will fear and all the wise of heart will be shaken by him
And LORD JEHOVAH answered and said to Job from a hurricane:
“Who is this who devises doctrine in words without knowledge?
Wrap your garment now on your loins as a mighty man. I shall ask you; teach me!
Where were you when I was laying the foundations of the Earth? Show me, if you know understanding
Who set down its measurements, if you know, and who has stretched a cord upon it?
On what have its ends stood, or who laid the stone in its corner?
He created as one the stars of the morning and all the sons of the Angels shouted for joy
And he shut the doors of the sea and it gushed out from the womb and it brought forth
When he set the work of the clouds her clothing, and thick darkness its swaddling band
And he made for her a covenant, and he made doors and lock bolts
And I said: ‘unto here you shall come and not go beyond, and here you will stay in the magnificence of your waves’
From your days have you commanded the dawn? Do you know which is the place of early dawn
To seize on the outer coverings of Earth, and the wicked shall be thrown out of it?
Their bodies shall be changed like clay, and they shall rise as clothing
Their light shall be withheld from sinners, and an high arm will be broken
Have you entered to the depths of the sea, and in the foundations of the Abyss have you walked?
Were the doors of death revealed to you and the doors of the shadows of death?
Have you seen all the breadth of the Earth? Show me if you know
In which path does the light dwell, and what is the place of darkness
If you know her border and the paths of her house
And if you know when you were conceived, and the number of your days, if they are many?
Have you entered the storehouses of the snow and do you see the storehouses of hail
Which are kept for the time of trouble and the day of conflict of war?
Or by which path is the light divided and the wind goes out on the Earth?
And there was an appearance and a sound
To send down rain on the land without a man, and in the wilderness where is no man
To satisfy every tangle of thorns and to make the procession of spring, spring up
Is there a father for the rain, or who begot the drops
And the dew and ice? From whose belly did they go out? And who begot the frost of Heaven?
The waters are hardened like stone, and the surfaces of the depths are frozen
Have you seized on the face of the Pleiades, or have you seen the path of the GiantOrion?
Behold, shall you bring forth the Waggoner in its time, or have you stood at the face of the star Aldebaran?
Do you know the law of Heaven, or do you lay down the law in the earth?
Do you lift up your voice over the clouds, and do you cover them with a multitude of waters?
Are you sending lightnings and they go and say to you: ‘Here we are!’?
Who set wisdom in a hiding place, or who gave vision to discernment?
Who numbered the clouds in his wisdom, and who established the pillars of Heaven?
Who poured the dust on the Earth, and who fashioned the cliffs?
Who gives a lion food and has filled the soul of the lions?
Who multiplies animals in the wilderness
And who gives the raven her food, because her children cry out to God and are failing from lack of food?
Do you know the time that the wild goats on a cliff bear young, and the bearing of the hinds?
Do you keep the numbers of the months and do you know the time of their delivery
And when they kneel and give birth
And they rear their children and they are weaned?
Who sent away the wild donkey a son of the free, and lets him escape from the yoke
That makes the valley his house, and his dwelling in the place of salt?
He laughs at the multitude of the cities and is not afraid of the voice of Rulers
In the multitude of the mountains is his pasture, and he treads upon every green thing
Does a wild bull consent to serve you, or does he spend the night at your stall?
Do you bind a yoke on the neck of the wild ox and he drives a plow in the rugged place?
Do you trust upon him because his power is great, and do you leave your labors upon him?
Do you trust him to thresh your granary and gather your seed?
The glorious wings of the peacock are made great, and she comes and makes a nest
And she leaves her daughters on the ground, and warms them upon the dust
And she has forgotten that she has the foot of a bird of prey, and the animal of the Earth treads on it
She has multiplied children that are not with her, and she has labored uselessly without fear
Because God has increased wisdom and he has not distributed understanding to her
She will be lifted up high as a palm tree; she will laugh at the horse and at his rider
Did you gave the horse manly strength, and did you cloth his neck with armor?
Do you shake him like a grasshopper, or do you terrify him with fear?
He paws in the depths and prances in the valley and goes out in armor for the attack
He laughs at a pit and is not afraid, and he does not turn from the face of the sword
The quiver is glorious against him and the flash of the spear and of the javelin
He runs on the Earth with shaking and with passion and is not afraid of the sound of the trumpet
With a voice he says, ‘Hurrah!’ and from a distance he smells the war and shakes Princes with his whinnying
Does the hawk exist from your wisdom and spread his wings to the south?
Will the eagle be lifted up by the word of your mouth and set up his nest on the cliff?
And he dwells and lodges on a crag of a cliff
And he is sustained from his prey, and his eyes gaze a long distance
And his chicks lick up blood, and where the slain are killed, there he is”
And LORD JEHOVAH answered and said to Job:
“Many are the counselors of God, and he who rebukes God gives an answer!”
And Job answered and said to LORD JEHOVAH:
“I have come short to myself. What shall I return to you? I have put my hands on my mouth
Once I have spoken and I shall not give an answer, and I will not continue to speak a second time!”
And LORD JEHOVAH answered and said to Job from the hurricane:
“Wrap now your loins as a mighty man. I shall ask you; teach me
If it is that you nullify me and you condemn me that you will be justified
Do you have an arm like God, and do you thunder with a voice like his?
Wear magnificence and heroism and put on brightness and excellence
And pour out the heat of your wrath and see everything that is exalted and pull it down
And cast down sinners in their places
And bury them together in the dust and cover their faces with dust
Also I shall praise you when your right hand has saved you
Behold, Behemoth which I have made with you; he eats grass like an ox
And his strength is in his hindquarters
He lifts up his tail like a cedar and the sinews of his thighs stand up
His bones are strong as brass and like iron
He is the chief of all the creation of God, for he made him that he would make war
And the abundance of the mountains carry to him, and all the animals of the wilderness
Under its shade he lies down; in the shelter of the rushes he crouches
The shadows surround him, and the ravens of the valley surround him
If the river rises, he is not shaken and trusts that he may pour the Jordan into his mouth
In his clouds let them take him, and in the net let him be held captive
Will you hold the Dragon in a net by a cord? Will you hold him by his tongue?
Do you cast a bridle in his mouth, and will you pierce his cheek with a thorn?
And if he begs much from you and speaks to you tenderly
Does he establish a covenant with you, or do you account him as a Servant forever?
Do you play with him like a bird and keep him for the days of your youth?
Shall companions be gathered for him and divide him among many?
Do you fill his skin with meat and his head in the shadow of fire
Lay your hand upon him and you shall have no more memory of war
Now your foot is released, also God shall take away your bitterness
He will not go far when he is awakened, and who is going to stand before me?
Who is before me that I would surrender? All under Heaven is mine!
And I shall not be silent at his strength and at the sinews of his power
Who opens the face of his clothing, and when he attacks the net, who comes?
Who opens the door of his mouth? The row of his teeth is awesome!
For in the torrent his mouth is tied up and bound and sealed
They are touching one another, and air does not enter between them
They are joined one to another and shut and are not separated
And his eyes are filled with light, and his eyes are like the bright shining dawn
And lamps go out of his mouth as coals of fire, and are burning like rays of fire
From his nostrils smoke goes out as the flame of a caldron which is seething
His breath kindles burning coals and flames come out from his mouth
On his neck lodges strength, and before him fear will dance for joy
His flesh is glorious and strong and firmly knit and unmoved
His heart is firm as stone and it is hard as quartz
From his awesomeness the mighty will be moved and the strong will be laid low
The cut of the sword cannot remain; he bears the lances of great ones
Iron is considered as straw to him, and brass is as rotten wood with a boring worm in it
He does not flee from the bow, and the stones of a sling are considered by him like chips
He laughs at a spear
[Verse text missing in source]
And the deep is considered like dry land to him
[Verse text missing in source]
And his power that causes destruction walks on the dust
He sees all that is high, and he is King over every creeping thing
And Job answered and said to LORD JEHOVAH:
“I Know that you can do all these things, and no mind is hidden from you
Who is this who devises doctrine by words without knowledge? Because of this you showed me that I did not understand and I have not known things that are greater than I
Hear me and I shall speak. I shall ask; you teach me
It is by the hearing of the ear I have heard you, and now, behold, my eye has seen you!
Because of this I shall be silent and I shall be revived on the dust and on the ashes

And it was after LORD JEHOVAH spoke these answers with Job, LORD JEHOVAH said to Eliphaz the Taymanite: “My anger was enraged against you and against your two companions, because you have not spoken righteousness before me as my Servant Job

Now take to yourself seven oxen and seven rams and go to my Servant Job, and he shall offer up a gift in exchange for yourselves, and my Servant Job shall pray for you, and I shall serve his person so that I will not cause you shame, because you have not spoken righteousness before me as my Servant Job”
And Eliphaz the Taymanite, Beldad the Shukhite and Tsuphar the Namathite went and they did as LORD JEHOVAH said to them, and LORD JEHOVAH served the person of Job
And LORD JEHOVAH turned back the captivity of Job when he was praying for his friends, and LORD JEHOVAH added double to everything that Job had possessed before

And all his brothers and his sisters came to him and all who knew him from former time, and they ate bread with him in his house where they had been mourning for him, and they comforted him from all the evil that LORD JEHOVAH brought upon him, and they gave him, each man, a ewe, and each one a nose ring of gold
And LORD JEHOVAH blessed the end of Job more than his beginning, and he had fourteen thousand sheep and six thousand camels and a thousand yokes of oxen and a thousand she donkeys
And he had seven sons and three daughters
And he called the name of one of them Yemema, and the name of the second, Qetsua, and the name of the third, Qarnphuuk
And there were not found women who were as beautiful the daughters of Job in all the land, and their father gave them an inheritance with their brothers
And Job lived a hundred and forty years after that, and he saw his sons, and his grandsons, unto four generations
Job was old and his days were full, and he died

name: Psalms
chapters: Blessed is the man that walks not in the way of the evil and does not stand in the counsel of sinners and does not sit on the seat of mockers.
But his pleasure is in the Law of LORD JEHOVAH, and in his Law he will meditate day and night.
He will be like a tree planted upon a stream of waters, it gives its fruit in its time, his leaves will not wither; all that he does he accomplishes.
The wicked are not so, but are like the chaff that the wind drives away.
Because of this, the wicked will not stand in the judgment, neither sinners in the assembly of the righteous ones.
Because LORD JEHOVAH knows the way of the righteous ones and the way of the wicked will be destroyed.
Why do the nations rage and the peoples plot futility?
The kings and rulers of the Earth stood up and counseled together against LORD JEHOVAH and against his Messiah:
Let us cut their bands and cast their yoke from us!
He who sits in Heaven will laugh and LORD JEHOVAH will mock them.
But this he will speak against them in his passion, and in his anger he will trouble them:
I have set my King on Zion, on the mountain of my holiness.
