Do not say, "I shall pay evil instead of good”, but wait for LORD JEHOVAH that he may save you.
Discrepant weights are defiled before LORD JEHOVAH and deceitful scales are not good.
The steps of a man are before LORD JEHOVAH, and who is the man that will order his way?
It is a snare unto a man who vows holiness and whose soul is sorry for it after he vows.
The wise King scatters evil ones and turns a wheel over them.
The soul of the children of men is the lamp of LORD JEHOVAH, and it searches all the inner rooms of the belly.
Grace and truth keep the King and his throne is established by grace.
The glory of young men is their strength and the excellence of elders is old age.
Misery and torment fall upon the evil, and affliction into the inner rooms of the belly.
The heart of the King is like a stream of waters in the hands of God, and he turns it wherever he chooses.
All the ways of a man are right in his own eyes, and LORD JEHOVAH sets the heart in order.
Whoever does righteousness and judgment is chosen to LORD JEHOVAH by sacrifice.
The pride of the eyes and the spirit of the heart and the lamp of the evil is sin.
The thoughts of the elect one are trusting, and those of the evil one cause want.
The effect of a treasure by a lying tongue is even unto destruction; those that seek death will be overthrown.
The shipwreck of the evil will come upon them, because they did not choose to do justice.
He that perverts his way is a strange man, and the works of the pure one are right.
It is better to sit on the corner of the roof than to dwell with a contentious woman.
The soul of the evil is not apparent to the eyes of his neighbors.
By the injury of the evil the child is made wise, and by his reflection the wise receives knowledge.
The Righteous One understands the heart of the evil, and he drives out the evil ones unto affliction.
He that shuts his ears that he will not hear the poor, he also will call out to God, and he will not answer him.
A gift in secret removes wrath and he that spares giving arouses anger.
The joy of the righteous works justice, and ruin to evildoers.
A son of man that strays from the way of understanding will descend with the children of the Earth.
A man that is wanting and loves joy and wine and pleasure, will not be rich.
The evil is an exchange for the righteous and a liar for the upright.
Better to stay in the land of the wilderness than to dwell with a contentious and angry woman.
There is coveted treasure and oil in the dwelling of a wise man and the intelligent of the children of men will consume it.
He that seeks righteousness and grace finds life, righteousness and glory.
A wise man went up to the city of a mighty man and conquered its trusted fortress.
He that keeps his mouth and his tongue keeps his soul from trouble.
An insolent one whose name is mighty does evil in his wrath.
The lust of the lazy one kills him, because he does not choose to work with his hands.
He desires lust all day long; the righteous gives and does not spare.
The sacrifice of the evil ones is detested before LORD JEHOVAH because they bring it in evil.
A false witness will be destroyed and an attentive man speaks justly.
The face of an evil man is shameless, and he who is upright establishes the ways of his soul.
There is no wisdom, neither intelligence, nor mind, like that of LORD JEHOVAH.
The horse is prepared for the day of war, but salvation is of LORD JEHOVAH.
The Name is better than great wealth, and compassion is better than gold or silver.
The rich and the poor meet each other and LORD JEHOVAH made both of them.
A prudent one sees an evil man being scourged and is powerfully instructed, but fools pass by him and suffer damage.
The pursuers of humility: the awe of LORD JEHOVAH, wealth, honor and Life.
Nets and snares are in the perverse way, and he that keeps his soul will be far from them.
He that reproves a boy concerning the beginning of his way, even if he becomes old, he will not turn away from it.
A rich man will be authorized by the poor and the Servant will lend to him who was his lender.
He that sows evil will reap fraud, and the scepter of his anger will be destroyed.
Whose ever eye is good will be blessed, because he gives of his bread to the poor.
Destroy the evil one and cast out the contentious, abolish judgment and shame, lest he sit in the assembly and shame all of you.
God loves him that is pure in heart and he shows love upon the lips of the king's friends.
The eyes of LORD JEHOVAH preserve knowledge and they destroy the words of liars.
When the lazy man was sent, he said, "There is a lion in the road, and look, a killer is in the streets!"
The mouth of an estranged woman is a deep ditch, and he with whom LORD JEHOVAH is angry falls there.
Senselessness disturbs the heart of a boy and a rod of discipline puts it far from him.
He that harms the poor increases his own affliction and he that gives to the rich suffers loss to his soul.
Bend your ear and hear the words of the wise and apply your heart to knowledge.
Because they are sweet, keep them in your gut, and they will form together upon your lips.
I have informed you today that your trust may be upon LORD JEHOVAH.
And look, I have written these things to you three times
That I may make known to you counsel, knowledge, stillness and the words of truth, that you may return the word of truth to him that sends you.
Do not oppress the poor because he is poor and do not humiliate the afflicted one in the gate.
Because LORD JEHOVAH decides their judgment and the humiliation of their souls is repaid.
You shall not be a friend to an angry man and do not enter with a man that is filled with passion,
Lest you learn his laws and you find a scandal to your soul.
Do not give yourself as security because you are shamed before people.
If you cannot pay, they will take the bed from under you.
Do not remove the border which your fathers have made from old times.
If you have seen a man skilled in his work, he will stand before kings and he will not stand in front of obscure men.
If you sit to dine with a Ruler, perceive what is set before you
Lest you put a dagger to your mouth, if you are a man that breathes.
And do not lust for his food because his bread is the bread of lies.
Do not come near a rich man, but in wisdom, separate from him;
For if you fix your eye on him, he disappears before you, because he makes wings for himself like an eagle and flies into the sky.
Do not dine with a gluttonous man and do not lust for his food.
For just as a man swallows a bristle hair, so you eat and drink with him and his mind is not with you.
And the bread that you eat, you vomit, and you spoil your sweet words.
Do not speak in front of a fool because he despises your words.
Do not remove the boundary which is of old, and do not enter into the field of orphans
Because their Savior is strong and he shall judge their case with you.
Bring your heart to instruction and your ear to the speech of knowledge.
Do not withhold discipline from a boy, because if you strike him, he does not die.
For you strike him with a rod and you deliver his soul from Sheol.
My son, if your heart is wise, my heart will also rejoice.
And my kidneys will be glad whenever your lips speak uprightness.
Let not your heart envy sinners but be zealous for the awe of LORD JEHOVAH all day long.
For there will be a posterity for you, and your hope will not end.
Hear, my son, and be wise, and set up my doctrine in your heart.
Do not be drunk with wine and do not be a glutton of meat.
Because he that is drunk with wine and a glutton of meat will be destroyed, and he that sleeps will wear horse cloth.
Hear your father who begot you and do not despise the old age of your mother.
Buy truth and do not sell wisdom, instruction or understanding.
The father of the righteous will exult and leap for joy, and he that begets a wise son, and his father will rejoice in him.
And your father and your mother will rejoice in you and she that bore you will rejoice.
Give me your heart, my son, and your eyes will keep my ways.
For a whore is a deep ditch and an estranged woman is a disturbed well.
She destroys suddenly and many evil men multiply.
Who has woe? Who has trouble? Who has judgment and who has afflictions? Who has wounds without cause and who has red eyes?
Is it not those that linger at the wine and track down where there is a tavern? You shall not be drunk with wine, but speak with righteous men and walk and talk with them.
Do not fix your eyes on the wine that is red, its color in the cup, but meditate on righteousness.
Because the end of the wine is like a wound from a serpent and like a flying cockatrice.
When your eyes gaze at an estranged woman, then your heart speaks perverse things.
And you yourself are like he that lies down in the heart of the sea, and like a sailor in a great storm.
And you will say, "They hit me and I did not suffer; they were abusive to me and I did not know it. When I wake up and I go out, I will seek it.”
Do not envy evil men and do not lust to be with them.
Because they devise evil in their heart, and their lips speak evil.
A house is built by wisdom and by understanding it is entirely put in good order.
And the inner chambers are filled by knowledge with every possession, honor and delight.
The wise is better than the strong, and a man of knowledge than a mighty man of power.
War is made in provocation, and salvation in the abundance of counsel.
Wisdom casts out the fool and he does not open his mouth in the gate.
He that devises to do evil is called an evil man.
The counsel of the fool is sin; the defilement of a son of man is evil.
Affliction will drive the evil in the day of distress.
Deliver those that are being led to death and do not spare to save those that are being held to kill.
And if you say, "I did not know it”, know that God examines the thought of the heart, and he who keeps your soul knows, and rewards a son of man according to his works.
Eat honey, my son, because it is good, and honeycomb is sweet to your palate.
So also you will find wisdom to your soul, and it will be the end to you, and your hope will not be cut off.
Do not plot evil in the dwelling of the righteous and do not plunder the place of his dwelling.
Because the righteous falls seven times, and he rises, and the wicked is overthrown by evil.
When your enemy has fallen, do not rejoice, and when he is overthrown, let not your heart leap for joy.
Lest LORD JEHOVAH see, and it is evil in his eyes, and he turn his wrath from him.
Do not emulate evil ones and do not envy the evil.
Because there is no posterity to the evil ones and the lamp of the evil will be extinguished.
Stand in awe, my son, of LORD JEHOVAH, and give counsel, and do not mingle with fools.
Their shipwreck comes suddenly, and who knows the end of their years?
I say these things to the wise; to accept persons in judgment is not good
Neither to say to the evil, "You are righteous"; the nations will curse such, and the peoples will revile him.
And the reprovers will be delighted, and a good blessing will come upon them.
And the lips will kiss those that reprove.
Prepare your works in the outdoors and make ready in your field, and after that build your house.
Do not be a false witness against your neighbor and do not tear him to pieces with your lips.
And do not say, "I shall do to him as he does to me and I shall pay him according to his works.”
I passed by the field of a lazy man and by the vineyard of a son of man lacking heart.
And all of that climbed up, filled with a tangle of thorns, and they covered the face of the gates.
And I saw and I set my heart, and I received instruction.
And if you slumber a little and sleep a little, and for a little you put your hand upon your chest
Poverty will walk before you, and want will overtake you like a man who is a runner.
These also are the deep proverbs of Solomon which the friends of Hezekiah king of Judah wrote:
It is the glory of God that hides the word, and the glory of the King that seeks for the word.
The Heavens are high and the Earth deep, and the heart of the King is unsearchable.
Select dross from silver so that a pure item may come forth.
Evil ones will be scattered from before the King and he will establish his throne in righteousness.
Do not boast before the king, and do not stand in the place of great men.
For it is better for one to say to you, "Come up”, than that you be humbled in front of a Ruler. Whatever your eyes see, that tell.
Do not go out for anything hastily, lest when you bring a lawsuit, your friend will revile you at the end.
Judge your judgment with your neighbor and do not reveal the secret of another
Lest he that hears will revile you, and many complain against you.
He that speaks the word is an apple of gold in a vessel of beaten silver.
An earring of gold and precious Sardius, so is the reproof of the wise to the ear of the hearer.
As whenever falling snow in a day of harvest cools the heat, so is a faithful messenger to his senders, and the soul of his Masters returns.
As when there are clouds and wind without rain, so is a man that boasts of false gifts.
In patience soothe a Ruler, and with a soft tongue break bones.
Whenever you have found honey, eat enough for you, lest when you are full, you vomit it.
Let not your foot frequent the house of your friend, lest he be weary of you and hate you.
Like a club, a sword and a sharp arrow, so is a man that testifies false testimony against his neighbor.
Like a bad tooth and a foot out of joint, so is trust in a liar in the day of trouble.

He that takes the cloak from his neighbor in the day of cold as like he that casts dirt upon wealth, and to chastise a grieved heart is like a moth to a garment and like a boring worm to a tree; so grief wounds the heart of a man.
If your enemy hungers, feed him, and if he thirsts, give him drink.
And when you do these things to him, you welcome coals of fire upon his head, and LORD JEHOVAH rewards you.
As the North wind gives birth to rain, so also is an evil face to a lurking tongue.
It is better to dwell on the corner of a roof than to dwell with a contentious woman in a house of divisions.
Like cold waters to a thirsty soul, so is good news that comes from a distant land.
Like a stopped up fountain and a stream of sewage, so is a righteous one that falls down before an evil one.
To eat much honey is not good, neither to seek words of praise.
Like a city breach without a wall, so is an impatient man.
Like snow in summer and like rain in harvest, so is honor unbecoming for a fool.
Like a bird and a flying creature in the air, so an empty curse wanders.
A whip for a horse and a goad for a donkey, so also is a rod for the fool.
Do not give answer to a fool according to his foolishness, lest you also be like him.
But speak with a fool according to your wisdom lest he think in his soul that he is wise.
He that sends a message by the hand of a fool is asking for trouble.
If you make a cripple walk, you may take the word of a fool.
Like a stone in a sling, so is he that praises a fool.
Thorns are sprung up in the hand of a drunkard and madness in the mouth of the fool.
The flesh of the fool suffers much and a drunkard crosses the sea.
Like a dog that returns to its vomit, so is a fool that is insane in his foolishness.
If you have seen a man that is wise in his own eyes, a fool is more useful than he.
Whenever a lazy man is sent, he says, "A lion is in the road"; "A lion is in the midst of the streets.”
Like a door that swings around on its hinges, so the lazy man turns on his bed.
The lazy one that hides his hands in his chest will labor to bring them to his mouth.
The lazy one is wiser in his own eyes than seven men who make sense.
He that meddles in a case that is not his is like he that takes a dog by its ears.
Just as a braggart casts words like sharp arrows of death
So is the man that deceives his neighbor, and when it is known, says, "I was just joking.”
When there is no wood, the fire is extinguished, and where there is no troublemaker, strife ceases.
A gridiron for coals and wood for fire, and a malicious man provokes dispute.
The words of the malicious stir up strife and they go down to the inner chambers of the heart.
Like tried silver plated over a clay vessel, so are fiery lips and an evil heart.
By his lips a hateful man is known, and he plots an ambush within him.
And if he makes his voice gentle, do not believe him, because seven evils are within him.
The evil of him that hides hatred in his heart will be revealed in the assembly.
He that digs a ditch will fall into it, and he that rolls a stone, it will return upon him.
A lying tongue hates the truth and the malicious mouth works trouble.
Do not boast about tomorrow, for you do not know what it brings forth.
Let a stranger praise you, and not your own mouth, and another, and not your own lips.
Stone is heavy and sand is weighty, and the wrath of the fool is heavier than both.
Presumption is rage and impetuosity is passion, but who shall stand before envy?
Public rebuke is better than secret love.
The wounds of a friend are better than the kisses of an enemy.
The soul that is full treads on honeycombs, and even bitter things are sweet to the hungry soul.
Like a bird that leaves its nest, so is a man that is removed from his place.
As oil and fragrances give joy to the heart, so is he that is sweet to his neighbor in the counsel of his soul.
Do not forsake your friend or the friend of your father, and do not enter the house of your brother in the day of your brokenness; better is a neighbor that is near than the brother that is distant.
Be wise my son, and gladden my heart, and remove from me the reproach of those who reproach me.
A cunning man sees evil and is hidden, but fools have passed by it and have suffered loss.
The cloak of him who is security for a stranger is taken, and for the person of a stranger he pledges it.
He that blesses his neighbor with flattery in a loud voice is not different from him that pronounces a curse.
Like a dripping that drips abundantly on a day of heavy rain, so is a contentious woman.
The North wind is severe, and it is called by name "Propitious.”
Iron sharpens iron, and a man sharpens the person of his neighbor.
He that keeps a fig tree will eat of its fruit and he that is watchful of his Master will be praised.
As faces are not alike, so hearts are not alike.
Sheol and destruction are not full, neither are the eyes of men satisfied.
A refining pot proves silver, and a furnace: gold, so is a man proven by the mouth of his praisers. The heart of the evil seeks evil, and the heart of the pure seeks knowledge.
If you strike a fool in the assembly, you do not help him, neither do you remove his foolishness.
Whenever you shepherd, know the faces of your sheep, and set your heart upon your flock.
Because dominion is not forever, neither is it delivered generation to generation.
Grass sprouts and new grass appears, and the green herb is gathered from the mountains.
Lambs for your clothes and kids for your food
And the milk of goats for your food and for the food of your house.
The evil will flee when there is no one chasing them, and the righteous are as confident as a lion.
Among the evil of the Earth are its many Rulers, and the righteous children of men who know righteousness endure.
A poor man that harms the poor is like a beating rain with no benefit in it.
Those that forsake the Law praise evil and those that keep the Law strengthen their souls.
Evil men do not understand judgment, and they that seek LORD JEHOVAH know all good things.
The poor walks in integrity and the rich perverts his way.
He that keeps the law is an intelligent son and he that serves worthlessness shames his father.
He that increases his property by contract and from usury will leave it to him that shows mercy upon the poor.
He that shuts his ears that he will not hear the Law, even his prayer is defiled.
He that leads an upright one astray into the way of evil will fall into a ditch and the innocent will inherit good things.
The rich man is wise in his own eyes and a poor intelligent one condemns him.
By the strength of the righteous ones, glory increases, and in the rise of the evil ones it is diminished.
A son of man that will hide his evil will not succeed, and God will be moved with compassion upon him that confesses to his sins and passes from them.
Blessed is the man that senses awe always, and he that hardens his heart will fall into evil.
The lion roars and the bear has given a sound because an evil one rules over poor people.
Many are the griefs of the Ruler lacking a mind and whoever hates deceit prolongs his days.
A son of man that is guilty of the blood of a soul will flee unto the elected ones and they will not help him.
He that walks without blemish will be redeemed and he that perverts his ways will fall into a pit.
He that cultivates the soil will be satisfied with bread and he that runs after worthlessness will be filled with poverty.
Many are the blessings of a faithful man and an evil one will not be spared in his evil.
A man that favors persons is not good, for by a piece of bread he betrays a man.
A man with an evil eye hastens to be rich and he does not know that loss has come upon him.
He that rebukes a son of man finds more favor than he that is duplicitous with his tongue.
He that plunders his father and his mother and says, "I have no sin”, is a partner with an evil man.
The covetous man stirs up contention and he that is assured by LORD JEHOVAH will grow fat.
He that trusts upon his own heart is a fool and he that walks in integrity will be delivered.
He that gives to the poor will not lack it, and he that turns his eyes from the poor will increase his curse.
In the rise of the evil a son of man is hidden, and in their destruction the righteous will be multiplied.
The man that does not receive rebuke and hardens his neck will quickly be ruined and there will be no healing for him.
By the multitude of the righteous, the people increase, and by the authority of the evil, the people will groan.
A man that loves wisdom gladdens his father and he that traffics with prostitution will destroy his possessions.
The king by judgment establishes the land, and the evil one deprives it.
A man that is duplicitous with his neighbor spreads a net for his steps.
An evil man is caught in his evil and the righteous will sing and rejoice.
For the righteous knows the justice of the poor and the evil does not recognize knowledge.
Men that are mockers burn up fortress cities and the wise turn back wrath.
A wise man debates with a fool, and he is passionate and laughs and is not enraged.
Men that are shedders of blood hate The Pure One and the righteous love him.
The fool expresses all his anger and the wise thinks with his mind.
All the Servants of a Prince that hears a lying word are evil.
The poor and the oppressor have met each other, and LORD JEHOVAH enlightens the eyes of both.
The throne of the King that judges in truth will be established in justice.
A rod and reproof give wisdom and a boy without discipline shames his mother.
In the abundance of the evil ones, evil increases, and the righteous will rejoice in their fall.
Discipline your son, and he will comfort you, and delight your soul.
In the multitude of the evil ones a people is breached, and he that keeps the law is blessed.
A Servant is not disciplined by words, for he knows that he will not be beaten.
If you have seen a man hasty in his words, know that a fool is better than he.
He that is pampered from his childhood will be a Servant, and at his end he will groan.
An angry man stirs up judgment and a furious man increases evil.
The pride of a son of man will humiliate him and his humility will increase honor to him.
He that partakes with the thief hates his soul and they put him under oath and he does not confess.
The evil of a man makes him a scandal and he that trusts upon LORD JEHOVAH will prevail.
Many seek the face of a Ruler and the judgment of a man proceeds from LORD JEHOVAH.
An evil man is defiled to the righteous, and the right way is defiled to the evil ones.
The words of Agur, son of Yaqay, who received the prophecy and was visited by power and he said to Ithliel:
Because my thoughts are deficient and I do not have the understanding of the children of men,
And I have not known wisdom neither have I learned the knowledge of The Holy Ones,

He said to me: "Who went up to Heaven and came down, and who is holding the wind in his palms? Who has bound the waters in a napkin and who has established all the ends of the Earth? What is his Name, and what is his Son's Name, if you know?"
All the words of God are chosen and he helps those that trust upon him.
Do not add to his words, lest he rebuke you, and you be proven a liar.
I have requested two things of you; do not deny me them before I die.
Take emptiness and lying speech far from me, and do not give me poverty or wealth. Give me a living sufficient for me.
Lest I be full and I lie and say, "Who is LORD JEHOVAH?”, or lest I become poor and I steal and swear in the name of my God.
Do not hand over a Servant to his Master, lest he curse you and you be condemned
To a generation that its fathers will revile and its mother will not bless;
A generation that is pure in its own eyes and is not washed from its own filthiness;
A generation: how haughty are its eyes and its eyelids raised!
A generation whose swords are its teeth, and daggers its molars, to consume the poor of earth and the afflicted of men.
Three leeches have three beloved daughters that are not full, and a fourth does not say, "It is enough":
Sheol and her barren womb, the ground that is not filled with water, and fire does not say, "It is enough.”
Ravens of the valley will tear out the eye that laughs at its father and despises the old age of its mother, and the young Eagles will eat it.
Three things are hidden from me, and a fourth I have not known exists:
The way of an eagle in the sky, the way of the snake upon a rock, the way of the ship in the heart of the sea and the way of a man in his youth.
So is the way of an adulterous woman; she eats and she wipes her mouth and she says, "I have done nothing.”
The Earth quakes under three things, and under four, it cannot endure:
Under a Servant when he will reign, and under a fool when he is filled with bread,
And under a hateful woman that is married to a man, and under a Maidservant that dismisses her mistress.
Four things are small in the Earth, and they are wiser than the wise:
Ants that have no power in them and from the harvest they prepare their bread,
And the rabbits that are lacking in power and make themselves houses in the rocks,
And locusts that have no King over them and they all assemble together,
And the lizard that clings with its hands and dwells in Kings' houses.
There are three that are right in their walking and the fourth is beautiful walking:
The whelp of a lion is champion of all beasts, neither is he afraid nor does he turn from any beast,
And a rooster that struts among hens, and the hegoat that goes at the head of the flock, and the King that speaks among the peoples.
Do not covet, lest you be despised, and do not stretch your hand to your mouth in depravity.
From the fat of the milk butter comes forth, but if you squeeze your hand upon wheat, blood will come forth, so from contention among people judgment goes forth.
The words of Moayl, The King, the Prophet, which his mother taught him and she said to him:
Oh, my son, oh, son of my body, and oh, son of my vows!
Do not give your strength to women and your ways for the provisions of Kings
Beware of Kings, Moayl, of Kings that drink wine, and of Rulers that drink strong drink
Lest you drink and you forget the Prophet, and you forget the judgment of all the poor children.
Strong drink is given to mourners and wine to bitter souls,
That they may drink and they may forget their sorrows, and their miseries would not be remembered anymore.
Open your mouth with the word of truth and judge all the sons of evil.
Open your mouth in righteous judgment and judge the poor and the afflicted.
Who finds a diligent woman? For she is precious beyond incomparable precious stones.
The heart of her husband trusts in her and she lacks no provision.
She has done good things for him and not bad things all the days of her life.
She has sought wool and fine linen and her hands have worked according to his pleasure.
She has been like a merchant's ship that brings its merchandise from far away.
She is risen in the night and has given food to the children in her house, and work to her girls.
She has looked at a field and bought it, and from the fruit of her hands she planted a vineyard.
She has girt her waist with strength and has strengthened her arms.
She has tasted that her merchandise is good and her lamp does not go out all night.
She stretched out her arms with diligence and her hands to the spindle.
She has stretched her hands out to the poor and she stretched out her arms to the afflicted.
The children in her house have not been afraid of the snow, because all of them wear scarlet.
She has made a carpet for herself and a robe of fine white linen and of purple.
Her husband is known among the cities as he sits among the Elders of the land.
She has made and sold fine linen and has given girdles to the Canaanites.
Strength and excellence are her clothing and she will rejoice in the last day.
She has opened her mouth in wisdom and the law of compassion is upon her tongue.
The ways of her house are open and she has not eaten the bread of laziness.
Her children stand up and give her blessings and her husband praises her.
And many of her daughters have retained wealth, and you have far surpassed all of these.
Beauty is false and favor is empty, but a woman that is in the awe of LORD JEHOVAH, she will be praised.
Give her of the fruit of her hands, and her deeds will praise her in the gate.

name: Ecclesiastes
chapters: Sayings of the Preacher the son of David, King of Jerusalem
Futility of futilities, says the Preacher; futility of futilities, everything is futility!
What profit has a son of man in all the toil that he toils under the sun?
A generation goes and a generation comes, and Earth stands to eternity
The sun rises and the sun sets, and to the place where it rises it is returning; from there it will rise again.
It goes to the South and is circled to the North and the wind goes to its circuits and the wind returns
All rivers run sea into the sea and the sea is not filled. The place to which the torrents depart, there they return
All accounts are wearisome; a man will not be satisfied to speak and the eye is not satisfied to see and the ear is not filled with hearing
Whatever was, is, and what was done, that is done. and there is nothing new under the sun
Everyone who will speak and will say, "see, this is new", surely it was from the ages which were before us
There is no memory for former things, neither will they who are afterwards have memory for later things
I, The Preacher, have been King over Israel in Jerusalem
I have given my heart to seek and to understand by wisdom all that is done under the sun; an evil task LORD JEHOVAH gives the children of men to practice in it
I have seen all the work that is done under the sun, and behold, everything is futility and agitation of spirit
The disturbed cannot be set in order and the lacking cannot be numbered.
I spoke with my heart, saying: "behold, I am great and I have increased in wisdom above all who have been before me in Jerusalem", and my heart saw a multitude of wisdom and of knowledge.
And I gave my heart to know wisdom and knowledge, proverbs and understanding, and I have known that this is also agitation of spirit
Because with abundance of wisdom is much grief, and he that increases knowing increases sorrow.
And I said I to my heart: come, "prove yourself with joy and see good", and, behold , this also is futility
I said to laughter: "What are these things?", and to joy", what have you done? ", and I turned that I would quiet my heart

I thought in my heart to give pleasure to my flesh with wine, and my heart meditated in wisdom and held to understanding until I would see what good there is for the children men that they may do under the sun for the number of days of their lives
I multiplied Servants for myself, I built houses for myself , I planted for myself vineyards
I made for myself gardens and paradises, and I planted in them trees of every fruit
I made for myself pools of waters to water the woods of fruit trees by them
I got for myself male Servants and female Servants and I had a multitude of children of houses, and also I had more property of bulls and of sheep than all who were before me in Jerusalem

I gathered to me also silver and gold and the property of Kings and of cities; I made for myself male Singers and female Singers and delight among the children of man. I made for myself male Cupbearers and female Cupbearers
And I was great and I had gained more than all that had been before me in Jerusalem, and also my wisdom remained with me
And all that my eyes asked I have not withheld from them, and I did not withhold my heart from any joy, because my heart rejoiced in all of my labors, and this has been my portion of all my labor
And I gazed at all the work that my hands had made and at all the labor that I had labored to make, and behold, everything was futility and agitation of spirit, and there is no profit under the sun
And I gazed to see wisdom and vice and folly, for who is the man that shall enter after the King into judgment, and then with him who made him?
And I have seen that there is more profit in wisdom than foolishness, as light is greater than darkness
A wise man’s eyes are in his head and a fool goes in darkness, and I have known that one event happens to them all
I said in my heart: "surely, according to the misfortune of a fool, it will happen also to me, and why am I more wise? And I said in my heart: "this also is futility!"

For the fool speaks from excess, because there is no remembrance of the wise with the fool for eternity, like days which already are coming and all are forgotten, and so the wise will die with the fool .
And I hated life because the work that is done under the sun is evil to me, because everything is futility and agitation of spirit
I hated all my labor that I have labored under the sun, because I leave it to a man who comes after me
And who will know if he shall be wise or a fool, and he will be authorized over all my labors that I wrought and when I was made wise under the sun, and this also is futility
And I turned to convince my heart about every labor that I labored under the sun
Because there is a man whose labor is in wisdom and in knowledge and in success, and a man who does not labor in it gives him his portion, and this also is futility and great evil!
And what does a son of man have in all his labor and in the desire of his heart which he wrought under the sun?
For all his days are sorrow and his work is grief, also his heart at night does not lie down, and this also is futility
There is no good for a man but to eat and to drink and to show good to his soul in his work; this also I have seen that it is from the hand of LORD JEHOVAH
For who is going to eat and who will drink apart from him?

For to the man who is good before him he gives wisdom and knowledge and joy, and to sinners he gives the task to increase and to gather and to give what is good before LORD JEHOVAH, and this also is futility and agitation of spirit
To everything there is a time, and a time for every matter under the sun
A time to be born and a time to die, the time to plant and the time to uproot the plant
A time to kill and a time to save life, a time to tear down and a time to build
A time to weep and a time to laugh, a time to dance and a time to mourn
A time to cast away stones and a time to gather stones, a time to embrace and a time to refrain from embracing
A time to lose and a time to seek, a time to bind up and a time to loose
A time to rip and a time to sew, a time to be silent and a time to speak
A time to love and a time to hate, a time of war and a time of peace
What profit has a son man in the work that he labors under the sun?
I have seen the task that LORD JEHOVAH gives the children men in which to be employed
All that he does is beautiful in its time, and he puts eternity in their heart, so that a man will not find the work that LORD JEHOVAH does from the beginning and until the latter end
I have known that there is no good in them but to gladden and do what is good in their lives
And also that every man may eat and drink and see good in all his work, this is the gift of LORD JEHOVAH
I have known that everything LORD JEHOVAH does shall be for eternity; there is nothing to add to it and nothing to subtract from it, and LORD JEHOVAH works that they shall be in awe of him
Whatever has already been, is, and all whatever shall be, has been for a long time, and God will seek the persecuted who is driven away
And again I have seen under the sun the place of judgment: there is wickedness, and the place of righteousness: there is evil
I said in my heart:" the righteous and the wicked God judges, because there is a time for every matter and every work"
I spoke in my heart about the speech of the children men whom God created , and to show that they are as beasts

Mishap meets the children men and mishap of beasts; one mishap occurs to all; like the death of one, so dies also the other, and they all have the same Spirit, and there is no superiority of a son of man compared to beasts, for everything is futility
All go to one place; everyone was from dust and each returns to dust.
And who knows if the spirit of the children of men ascends above, and the spirit of beasts descends under the earth?
I have seen there is nothing better for them but that a man rejoice in his works, because that is his portion, for who will bring him to see all that will be after him?

And I turned and I have seen all the oppressions that are committed under the sun, behold, the tears of the oppressed, and there is no comforter for them from the hand of their oppressors of power, and they have no helper
And I praised the dead who have died long ago, more than the living, those who are alive now
But better than both of these is the one who is not yet, because he does not see the evil work that is done under the sun
I have seen all the labors and all successes of the owners of a work, because the zeal of a man is more than his neighbor’s, and this also is futility and agitation of spirit
The fool clasps his hands and eats his flesh
Better a handful in quiet than handfuls with toil and agitation of spirit
And I turned and I saw futility under the sun

There is one and there are not two, and he has neither a son nor a brother, and there is no end to all his labor, and also wealth does not satisfy his eyes; and for whom do I labor and deprive my soul of good? And this also is futility and evil business
Two are better than one, for they have a good reward in their labor
For if one falls, his companion lifts him up; but grieve for him who is alone, for if he falls, there is no one to lift him up
And if two will lie down they will get warm, and how will one be warm alone?
And if one will prevail, two of them will stand against him, and a threefold cord is not quickly broken
Better a boy who is poor and wise than an old King who is foolish and still does not know how to take warning
Because from prison he goes forth to reign, for also in his kingdom he was born poor
I have seen All Life walking under the sun, with The Second Young Man that will arise in his place
And there is no end to all the people, all who have been before him, neither will the latter ones rejoice in him, because even this is futility and agitation of spirit
For keep your foot when you go to the house of God, and come near; to hear is better than gifts of the offerings of fools, because fools do not know to do what is good
Do not be quick with your mouth and your heart will not be quick to send out a word in front of God, because God is in Heaven and you are in the Earth; because of this let your words be few
Because a dream comes in a multitude of business, and the voice of a fool in a multitude of words
When you vow a vow to God, do not delay its accomplishment, because there is no pleasure in fools, but pay what you vow
It is better that you do not vow than that you vow and you do not fulfill
You shall not give your mouth to cause your flesh to sin and you should not say in front of God: "It is my error", lest God will be angry against your voice and he will destroy the work of your hands
Because in many dreams and empty things and many words of deception you worship God
And if you shall see defrauding of the poor and rape and judgment in the city, do not marvel at the matter, because he who is higher than the high watches, and they are exalted over them
And the profit of Earth in all is a King cultivating a field.
He who loves silver will not be satisfied with silver and he who loves money will not keep it, and this also is futility
In the abundance of good its consumers have increased, and what is the profit to its owner except only the sight of his eyes?
A laborer has sweet sleep, whether he eats little or much, and the fullness of the rich man does not allow him to sleep
There is a disease of evil that I have seen under the sun: wealth that is kept by its owner to his harm
And wealth is lost in bad business, for he begets a son and there is nothing in his hand
Just as he goes forth from the womb of his mother naked, he will return to go as he came, and he will take nothing of his labor to go in his hand
And this also is an evil disease, for as he comes, so he goes, and what is the profit in all that he labored for the wind?
And also because he consumes his days in darkness and in much anger and in rage and in mourning and in disease and in rage

This thing I, the Preacher, have seen: it is good and beautiful to eat and to drink and to see good in all his labor that he labored under the sun, the numbers of the days of his life, the days LORD JEHOVAH gives to him, for that is his portion
And also every man to whom LORD JEHOVAH gives wealth and property and authorizes him to eat of it and to take his portion and to rejoice in his work, this also is the gift of God
Because he will not much remember the days of his life, because God occupies him with the joy of his heart
There is evil that I have seen under the sun, and it is great upon the children of man

A man to whom God will give wealth and goods and honor and his soul lacks nothing of all that he desires and God does not enable him to consume it, but a man who is a stranger will consume it who comes after him; this is futility and a disease

If a man will beget a hundred and he will live many years and the days of his years will be multiplied, and his soul will not be satisfied from precious things, and also he will have no burial, I have said that a miscarriage is better than he
Because he came in futility and in darkness he will go, and in darkness his name will be hidden
Also he does not see the sun and he knows no rest, one no more than the other
And if he lives two thousand years and he sees no good, do not all go to one place?
All that a man labors goes into his mouth and his soul is not full
Because there is more profit to the wise than to the fool. Why does the poor man know to go to The Life?
Better the sight of the eyes than the going of the soul, and this also is futility and agitation of the soul
Whatever has been from the first is called by its name, and it is known what a son of man is, and he cannot judge with whomever is more powerful than he
Because there are many matters that increase futility. What profit does a son of man have?
For who knows what good a son of man has in his life, the numbers of the days of life of his futility, and he passes them as a shadow? Who will show a son of man what will be after him under the sun?
A good name is better than good oil, and the day of death is better than the day of birth
Better to go to the house of weeping than to go to the banquet house, because this is the end of all the children of men, and The Living One gives good to his heart
Grief is better than laughter, because in the sadness of the face the heart will improve
The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning, and the heart of fools in the house of joy
Better to hear the reproof of the wise than a man who hears the song of fools
For like the sound of thorns under a metal pot, so is the laughter of fools, and this also is futility
Because oppression destroys the wise and a gift destroys the heart
Better is the end of a matter than its beginning, and better is patience of spirit than pride of spirit
Do not be quick in your spirit to be angry, for anger rests in the breast of fools
Do not say: "Why is it that the former days were better than these"?, because it is not in wisdom you ask about these things
Wisdom is better than a supply of arms, and it is greater for those seeing the sun
Because the shade of wisdom is like the shade of money, and the profit of knowledge is that wisdom will save its owner
See the work of God, for who is able to restore him that is disturbed?
In the day of goodness be in prosperity, and in the day of evil look after your soul; God also makes this one opposite that one, so that a son of man will not find anything to say after him
Everything I have seen in the days of futility: there is a righteous one who is destroyed in his righteousness and there is an evil one who is prolonged in his evil
Do not be too righteous, and do not be too wise, lest you be stupid
You shall not be greatly irreverent so that you be not greatly hated; do not be foolish, lest you die when not in your time
It is good that you take hold of this, neither let your hand go from this, because whoever is in awe of God will cling to all these things
Wisdom strengthens the wise more than ten rulers who are in the city
For there is not a righteous man in Earth who will do what is good and will not sin
And also do not give your heart to all statements that the wicked will utter; do not listen to your servant who insults you
For your heart knows you also have insulted others many times
All these things I tested by wisdom; I said: "I shall be wise," and this was far removed from me
Beyond all that is distant and the depth of depths; who will find it?
I and my heart have traveled about to know and to perform and to seek wisdom and calculation and to know the wickedness of the fool and foolishness and crime

And I have found that more bitter than death is a wife who is a snare and her heart a net; she has bound the hands of him who is good; he who is good in front of God is delivered from her, and he who sins is caught by her
See this that I have found, says the Preacher, one by one, to find reasons
And again my soul searched and I have not found; one man of a thousand I have found, and a woman among all these I have not found
However, see this that I have found: "God has made men upright, and they have searched out many schemes"
Who is like The Wise One and who knows the interpretation of the Word? The wisdom of a man will enlighten his face, and he whose face is shameless will be hated
The mouth of the King keep, and do not be hasty concerning the word of the oath of God
Go from before him and do not stand in an evil matter, because all that he will desire he will do
Just as the King has spoken, he is authorized, and who will say to him: "What are you doing?"
He who keeps the commandment does not know an evil matter, and the heart of the wise knows time and judgment
Because for every desire there is a time and judgment, because the affliction of man is great upon him
Because there is no one who knows what has been, and what will happen to him. Who shows him what comes after him?
There is no man who is authorized over the wind to withhold the wind, and there is no authority in the day of death, and there is no escape in the day of war, and evil does not save its masters
And I have seen all this and I have given my heart to know all work that is done under the sun, and the time when a man is authorized over a son of man to afflict him
And then I saw the wicked being buried, and they came, and they went from the place of holiness and were forgotten in the city where they had thus worked, and this also is futility
Because punishment of evil doers is not done quickly, therefore the heart of sons of men is filled in them to do what is evil
Whoever sins does a hundred evils and prolongs himself, and I know that it will be good for the worshipers of LORD JEHOVAH who will stand in awe before him
And the wicked one will have no good, and he will not lengthen days like a shadow, because he is not in awe before God

There is futility that is done upon Earth because there are righteous ones to whom it happens as the work of the wicked, and there are the wicked to whom it happens like the work of righteous ones; and I said that this also is futility

And I praised joy, for there is no good for a son of man under the sun but to eat and to drink and to rejoice, and this accompanies him in his labor all the days of his life that God gives him under the sun
Because of this, I have given my heart to know Wisdom and to see the business that is done upon Earth, because by day and by night with his eyes he sees no sleep

And I have seen all the work of God, for a son of man will not master the work that is done under the sun. All that a son of man toils that he may seek he will not find, and none who will claim to know The Wise One will be able to find

For all these things I have put into my heart, and my heart saw all this: that the righteous ones and the wise and their works are in the hand of God; also a son of man does not know love or hatred; everything that is before him is futility

All alike happens to all; the same thing to the righteous ones and to the wicked, to the good and to the evil, to the pure and to the defiled, to the sacrificer and to the non- sacrificer, like the good the sinner is also, like the oath taker also is the one who fears oaths

This evil is in all whatever is done under the sun, because one thing happens to them all, and also the heart of man is full of evil and the evil wandering in their heart, and in their lives, and their end is with the dead
Everyone who shall be joined to All Life has trust, because a living dog is better than a dead lion
Because the living know that they are dying, and the dead do not know a thing, and there is no more reward for them, because their memory is forgotten
Also their love and their hatred and also their envy is now destroyed, and there is no more portion for them for eternity in all whatever is done under the sun
Come, eat your bread with joy and drink your wine with a good heart, because God is pleased with your works!
Always let your garments be white and let not your head lack oil
And see life with the wife whom you have loved all the days of the life of your futility, because she is your lot in life and in your labor that you labor under the sun
All that your hand finds to do, do in your power, for there is no work or reasoning or knowledge or wisdom in Sheol, because you are going there

I turned and I saw under the sun that the race is not to the swift , neither to a mighty man the battle, neither to the wise bread and not to the learned riches, and not to the intelligent glory, because time and mishap happens to them all

For a son of man does not know his time, like fish that are caught in an evil net; like the fowl that are taken in a snare, so are the children of men taken in an evil time when it will fall upon them from the stillness
This wisdom also I have seen under the sun, and it is great to me
There was a small city and a few children of men in it, and a great King came to it and he surrounded it and he built bulwarks against it
And a poor wise man was found in it and that poor man delivered the city by his wisdom, and no person remembered that poor man
And I said in my heart: "Wisdom is better than might, and the wisdom of the poor is despised and his statements are not heeded
Words of the wise in quiet are heeded more so than the shout of a prince who is a fool
Better is wisdom than instruments of war, and one sin destroys many good things
Like dead flies putrefy a vessel of sweet oil, a little foolishness is weightier than wisdom and than great glory
The heart of the wise is at his right hand and the heart of a fool at his left
And also a fool whenever he goes on the road, lacks his heart, and all that he thinks is foolishness
If the spirit of a prince rises against you, do not leave your place, because healing dissolves many sins
There is an evil I have seen under the sun, like an error that goes forth before a ruler
For a fool sits in a high mighty place, and the rich will be seated in humility
I have seen Servants upon horses and Rulers walking like Servants upon the Earth
He who has dug a pit will fall into it and he that breaks a hedge, a snake will bite him
He who removes stones will be hurt by them, and he who splits wood is wearied by it
If the iron is dull and the surface is rough and it multiplies those killed, also wisdom is a profit for diligent ones
And if a snake will bite without a charm, there is no profit in a charmer
The words of the mouth of the wise one are glory, and the lips of a fool ruin him
The beginning of the words of his mouth is foolishness, and the end of his mouth is evil shame
And a fool multiplies words; a son of man does not know what has been, and who shows him what will be after him?
The labors of fools weary them, for they do not know how to go to the city
Woe to you, oh city, when your King is a boy and your princes eat at dawn!
Your blessing, oh city, when your King is a son of the free and your Princes in their time eat with diligence and not with drunkenness!
By slackness a roof will be lowered and by slackness of hands a house will drop
For joy, bread and wine are made, and oil that gladdens life; and money humbles and seduces them in all things
And also do not revile the King in your mind, and in your bedroom do not insult a rich man, because a bird of the sky will transmit your voice, and the Lord of the bird will show your statement
Send your bread upon the face of the waters, for after many days you will find it
Give a portion to seven, even to eight, because you will not know what evil may be upon Earth
If clouds will fill up, they will cast rain upon Earth, and if a tree has fallen unto the South or unto the place of the North, where the tree has fallen, there it will be
He who watches the wind does not sow, and he who observes the clouds does not reap
Because you do not know the way of the wind, for as the way of her who is pregnant of conception, so you will not know the works of LORD JEHOVAH, all that he made
Every dawn sow your seed, and at evening do not remove your hand, because you do not know what will prosper, whether this or that, or if both are good together
Sweet is the light and good for the eyes, and it is better to see the sun
Because if a son of man will live many years, may he rejoice in all these and be reminded of the days of darkness, for they will be many. All that comes is futility

In the days of your youth rejoice, young man, and it will be well for you, and walk in the ways of your heart and in the vision of your eyes, and know that in all these LORD JEHOVAH brings you into judgment
Remove anger from your heart and separate evil from your flesh, because youth and ignorance are futility
Remember your Creator in the days of your youth until the evil days will come, and the years will approach when you say: "I have no pleasure in them"
Until the sun will darken and the light of the moon and of the stars, and the clouds return after the rain
In the day when the Housekeepers will shake and the Masters of power are agitated, and the grinders will fail because they are few, and the watchers in the windows will grow dark
And the gates of the streets will be shut by feebleness of the sound of the grinders, and he will rise to the voice of a bird, and all the daughters of song will be brought low

And also he will be afraid of height and he will shake in his ways, and wakefulness will seek for him and the bitter almond will bud and the locust will multiply and henna plant will be scattered and evil will cease, because a son of man goes to the house of his labor* and mourners go about in the streets
Until the cord silver is cut and the gold bowl will be shattered, and the pitcher will be shattered over the fountain and the wheel will run over the well
And the dust will return to Earth like it was and the spirit will return to LORD JEHOVAH who gave it
Futility of futilities, says the Preacher; futility of futilities, everything is futility!
And moreover, because the Preacher was wise who had been teaching knowledge to the people, and he listened and he investigated and he has set in order many proverbs
The Preacher sought much to find statements of pleasure and he wrote in truth statements of truth
The words of the wise are like goads and like nails with which Masters nail door posts; they are given from one Shepherd
And especially pay attention to them, my son. To the writing many scrolls there is no end, and much speaking is weariness of the flesh
The end of the matter in its sum: listen to everything and be in awe before LORD JEHOVAH, and keep his commandments, for this is of One Craftsman which is given to every person
Because LORD JEHOVAH brings all works into judgment concerning everything that is hidden, and it is revealed whether it is good or evil

name: Song of Solomon
chapters: The Song of Songs of Solomon, son of David, King of Israel, which is Song of Songs, and called in Hebrew, Song of Songs
He shall kiss me from the kisses of his mouth, for your affections are better than wine
For fragrance of your sweet ointments your name is fine oil of Myrrh, because of this the girls love you

Pull me after you, let us run. The King brought me to his bedroom. We will rejoice and we will leap for joy with you. Your love will be remembered more than wine, and your affection more than the upright
I am black and beautiful, daughters of Jerusalem, like the tents of Qedar and like the curtains of Solomon
You shall not look at me, because I am black, because the sun has darkened me. The children of my mother fought hard with me; they made me keeper of vineyards, and my vineyard I have not kept
Show me him whom my soul has loved. How have you fed and how have you dined at noonday?, lest I shall be as a stray in the flock of your sheep
But you shall know yourself; you are beautiful among women. Come into the footprints of the sheep and feed your kids in the tents of Shepherds
I have compared you to a mare in the chariot of Pharaoh, my kinswoman
Your cheeks are beautiful with braids and your neck with necklaces
We shall make chains of gold for you with studs of silver
With the King on his seat my spikenard gives its fragrance
A pouch of myrrh is he who loves me; between my breasts he will stay
Bouquets of henna flowers is my beloved in a vineyard of Ain Gad
Behold, you are beautiful, my kinswoman, behold, you are beautiful! Your eyes are of doves!
Behold, you are beautiful, my love, also you are sweet, and our bed is canopied
The beams of our house are cedars, the joists of cypress
Like the lily of Sharon, the lily of the valley
Like lilies among thorns, so is my intimate woman among the daughters
As apples in the trees of the forest, so is my love among the sons; for his shadow I lusted and I sat; his fruit is sweet to my palate
He brought me to the house of wine; he appointed love for me
Sustain me with delicacies, surround me with fruit, for I am love sick
His left hand is under my head and his right will embrace me
Swear, oh daughters of Jerusalem, by the deer or by the fawns of the field, you will not wake up and you will not wake up the beloved until she pleases
The voice of my love: behold he comes skipping on the mountains, leaping on the hills
My love is like a deer or to the fawn of a hart on the mountains of Bayth Eil, behold he is standing behind our wall; he watches from the windows; he stoops at the thresholds
My beloved answered and he said to me: "arise, for you, my intimate one, you are beautiful, and go
For , behold, the winter has passed and is gone!
The blossoms have appeared in the earth. The time of pruning has arrived! The voice of turtle-doves is heard in our country
The fig tree has given its figs and the vines and the vine buds give a fragrance. Arise! You are beautiful my intimate one and come!
My dove in the clefts of the rock, in secret places of a hedge, show me your beauty, your appearance, and make me hear your voice, for your voice is sweet and your appearance is beautiful!
Catch for us foxes, small foxes ruining the vineyards and our vines of vine buds
My beloved is mine and I am his, who feeds among the lilies
While the day will grow cold and the shadows shall lengthen, return, my love, be like a deer or to a fawn of a hart on the mountains of spices
On my bed in the nights I begged him whom my soul has loved; I sought him and I did not find him. I called him and I did not find him
I shall arise now and I shall go around in the city in the streets, and in the broad ways I shall seek him whom my soul has loved. I sought him and I did not find him
The guards found me who walk around in the city: "Have you seen him whom my soul loves?"
When I passed from them a little until I found him whom my soul loved, I seized him and I did not let go of him until I brought him to the house of my mother to the bedroom of her who bore me
I charge you daughters of Jerusalem by the deer or by the stags of the field, do not rise and wake up love until she pleases
Who is this who comes up from the wilderness like a fume of smoke? He is sweet with myrrh and with frankincense from every powder of sweet spices!
Behold the bed of Solomon! Sixty mighty men surround it of the mighty men of Israel
All of them hold a sword and are teachers of war, each man’s sword on his thigh from fear of the night
King Solomon made himself a tower from wood of Lebanon
Its pillars he made of silver, its floor of gold and a covering of purple; inside it is paved with love from the daughters of Jerusalem
Go forth and see, daughters of Zion, King Solomon with the crown with which his mother crowned him in the day of his nuptials in the day of the joy of his heart
Behold, you are beautiful my intimate one, behold, you are beautiful! Your eyes are of a dove; outside of your silence, your hair is like a flock of goats that goes up from Mount Gelad
Your teeth are like a flock that is shorn going up from their washing, all bearing twins and none is barren among them
Your lips like a cord of scarlet and your speech beautiful as the blossoms of a pomegranate
Your neck, in your stillness, is like the tower of David; your throat built with belts, one thousand shields hanging upon it, all quivers of mighty men
Your two breasts as two gazelles, twins of deer that feed among the lilies
While the day will grow cool and the shadows of evening will decline, I shall go to the mountains of myrrh and to the hills of frankincense
You are entirely beautiful, my intimate one, and there is no defect in you
Come from Lebanon, my sister bride, come from Lebanon! Come and cross over from the top of Amana and from the top of Sanir and Khermon, from the den of lions and from the mountains of leopards
You have given me heart, my sister bride, you have given me heart with one of your eyes, with one necklace of your neck
How beautiful are your breasts, my sister bride! How much better are your breasts than wine, and the fragrance of your sweet spices than all sweet spices!
Your lips drip honeycomb! All honey and milk are under your lips and the fragrance of your vesture is as the fragrance of Lebanon!
A garden that is enclosed is my sister bride! A garden that is enclosed, a spring that is sealed
Your nudity is a paradise of pomegranates with the fruit of produce; henna flower with spikenard
Spikenard and saffron, crocus, cane and cinnamon with trees of frankincense, myrrh and aloes, with all the best of sweet spices
A spring garden, a well of living waters that flows from Lebanon
Awaken, oh North, and come south wind! Blow in my garden! My sweet fragrances shall flow, and my beloved shall come to his garden, and he will eat from the fruit of his produce

I have come to my garden, my sister bride; I have come to my garden. I have picked my myrrh with my sweet spices. I ate my sweetness with my honey. I drank my wine with my milk. Eat, my friends, and drink and be drunk, my beloved

I was sleeping and my heart was awake. The voice of my love who was knocking: "Open to me, my sister, my intimate, my innocent dove, for my head is filled with dew and my curls from drops of the night
I took off my coat; how shall I put it on? I have washed my feet; how shall I get them dirty?
My beloved reached his hand from the opening and my bowels stirred for him
I stood that I would open to my beloved, and my hand dripped myrrh and my fingers dripped myrrh on the handle of the bolt
I opened to my beloved and my beloved had declined, he passed on; my soul went out at his speaking. I searched for him and I did not find him; I called him and he did not answer me
The guards who walk around in the city found me; they struck me and they beat me; the keepers of the wall took my bridal veil from me
I charge you daughters of Jerusalem, if you find my beloved, tell him that I am sick with love
Who is your beloved more than a beloved, beautiful among women? Who is your beloved more than a beloved, beautiful among women? Who is your beloved more than a beloved, that thus you have charged us?
My beloved is white and blood red, and choice of ten thousand
His head is like rock of gold, his locks are straight and black as ravens
His eyes are like those of doves by pools of waters that are washed in milk and sit with harmony
His cheeks are as beds of sweet spices; his lips like the lilies that drip myrrh and spikenard
His hands are like a fortress city of gold that is full of stones of gold; his belly the work of ivory on the blowing of sapphire
His legs are pillars of marble which were set in order on sockets of gold; his chest is like Lebanon and choice as cedars
His palate is like honeycombs that are sweet and his garments are desirable; this is my beloved and this is my intimate friend, daughters of Jerusalem!
Where has your beloved gone, beautiful one among women? Where has your beloved turned, that we may seek him with you?
My beloved went down to his garden and to the garden beds of sweet spices, that he may feed in the gardens and he may gather the lilies
I am my beloved’s and my beloved is mine, who feeds among the lilies
You are beautiful, my intimate one, as desire; you are beautiful as Jerusalem, and you are awesome as the chosen one
Turn your eyes from me, for they make me flutter; you hair is like a flock of goats going up from the Mountain of Gelad
Your teeth are like a flock that is shorn going up from washing, all of which are bearing twins, and none is barren among them
Your neck out of your silence is like blossoms of pomegranate
Sixty are Queens and eighty are Concubines and the young girls there have no number
One is my innocent dove; one she is to her mother, and chosen she is to her who bore her. The daughters saw her and praised her, and the Queens and Concubines, and they praised her
Who is she, this one who looks forth like the morning, beautiful like the moon, pure like the sun, awesome like a Princess?
I have come down to the garden of walnut trees that I may see the fruit of the valley, to see if the vine has borne fruit and if the pomegranates have budded
I had not known; my soul set me in a chariot of the people that was prepared
Return! Return, Shilumite! Return, return, and we shall gaze at you! What will you see in the Shilumite who comes down as rejoicing, like the rejoicing of army camps?
How beautiful are your feet in sandals, daughter of preparation, and the form of your thighs as a necklace, the work of the hands of a Craftsman!
Your navel is a goblet that is round that lacks no mixture in it. Your belly is a heap of wheat that is enclosed in lilies
Your two breasts are as two fawns, twins of deer that feed in the lilies
Your neck is like a tower of ivory. Your eyes as the pools of Kheshbon at the gate of the palace of the great ones. Your nose is like the tower of Lebanon that gazes at the face of Darmsuq
Your head upon you is like Karmela and the tresses of your head like the purple garment of the King that is tied in rows
How beautiful you are! How sweet you are, oh beloved daughter of delights!
And this your stature is like a palm tree and your breasts are like grape clusters!
I said:"I shall go up into the palm tree and I shall seize on its branches, and your breasts shall be as clusters of the vine, and the fragrance of your face is like apples
And your palate is like fine wine that goes to my beloved for uprightness that shakes my lips and my teeth
I am my beloved's and his inclination is toward me
Come, my beloved, let us go out to the field! We will stay in a village!
And we shall go early into the vineyard; we will see if the vine has borne fruit and the buds have come out and the pomegranates have budded; there I shall give you my breasts
The mandrakes have given a fragrance, and all our fruits, new ones and old ones, my beloved, I have kept for you upon our gates
Who shall give you as a brother to me? My breasts nursed my lambs. I will find you in the street. I will kiss you and they will not despise me
I shall lead you and I shall bring you to the house of my mother and to the bedroom of her who bore me. I shall give you drink from my sweet wine and from the sweetness of my pomegranates
His left hand is under my head and his right hand will embrace me
I charge you, daughters of Jerusalem, do not wake up and do not wake up love until she pleases!
Who is this coming up from the wilderness and supported by her beloved under the apple tree? I awakened you and there your mother was in labor with you; there your mother labored with you!
Set me as the seal on your heart and as a seal on your arm, because love is as strong as death, and jealousy hard as Sheol! Its blazing is like the blazing of fire and the flame!
Many waters are not able to quench love, and rivers do not overwhelm it! If a man will give all the riches of his house for love, mocking they will make a mockery of him
We have a little sister and she has no breasts; what shall we do for our sister in the day in which she is spoken for?
If she is a wall, we will build up on her battlements of silver, and if she is a door, we will bind upon her a title of cedar
I am a wall and my breasts are towers; then I became in his eyes as she who found peace
There was a vineyard for Solomon and its fruit was abundant. I have given a vineyard to the keepers; a man gave a thousand pieces of silver for its fruits
My vineyard is mine before me. A thousand coins for you, Solomon, and two hundred to the keepers of the fruit
Make me hear those dwelling in the gardens and they listen to your voice!
Return my beloved! Be like a roe or a fawn of a hart on the mountains of spices!

name: Isaiah
chapters: The vision of Eshaiya, son of Amuts, that he saw about Yehuda and about Jerusalem in the days of Uzzia and Yotham and Ahaz and Khezeqia, Kings of Judea
Listen, Heaven, and pay attention, Earth, because LORD JEHOVAH has spoken: “I have raised and I have exalted children, and those have risen up against me
The bull knows his owner and the male donkey the stall of his Master, and Israel does not know, nor have my people understood
Woe to the sinner nation! The nation grows an evil seed doing evil, corrupt children. You have forsaken LORD JEHOVAH and you have angered the Holy One of Israel, and you have turned your back
Why will you again be destroyed and will you add to being punished? All the head is for illness and all the heart for sorrow
From the sole of the foot and unto the brain there is no sound place in it; there are wounds and sores and plagues of tumors not bound, neither bandaged, neither are they softened with oil
Your land is desolate and your cities burn in fire. Foreigners consume your land near you, and it is a desolation like the ruin of foreigners
The daughter of Zion was blackened as a hut in a vineyard, and like a booth in a cucumber garden and like a besieged city
And if LORD JEHOVAH of hosts had not left to us a survivor, we would have been as Sadom and we would have been like Amora
Hear the response of LORD JEHOVAH, Rulers of Sadom, and pay attention to the law of our God, people of Amora
”Why were the abundance of your sacrifices to me, says LORD JEHOVAH? I am filled with sacrifices of rams and fat of fatlings, and the blood of oxen and of lambs and of goats I have not desired
When you will come to see my face, who desires these things from your hands to tread my courts?

You shall not continue to bring to me worthless savor offerings. It is disgusting to me. At the first of the months and on the Sabbath you assemble an assembly. I will not eat something from depravity or from a siege.
The beginning of your months and your feasts my soul has hated. They have been upon me for a burden; I am weary to bear them
When you spread your hands, I shall turn away my eyes from you; even though you will multiply prayers, I will not hear; your hands are filled with blood
Wash and be purified and remove the evil of your works from before my eyes; cease from evil
And learn to do good. Search for judgment and do good to the oppressed; do justice for orphans and do justice for widows
And come, we shall speak with each other, says LORD JEHOVAH, and if your sins will be as scarlet, they will be white as snow, and if they turn red as scarlet dye, they shall be as wool
And if you will obey and you will listen to me, you shall eat the good of the Earth
And if you will not obey and you will contend, by the sword you shall be consumed. The mouth of LORD JEHOVAH has spoken
How has the faithful city become a whore, that was full of justice and righteousness, and now murderers are lodging in it!
Your silver is rejected; your tavern wines are mixed with water

Your Princes are rebellious and are partners of thieves; all of them love a bribe and they hurry to reward corruptions; for the orphans they do not execute justice, and the justice of the widow does not come into them
Because of this, thus says THE LORD OF LORDS of Armies, The Mighty One of Israel: ‘Woe! I am repaid by my haters and I am avenged of my enemies!
And I shall turn my hand against you and I shall punish your rebels to purge them, and I shall remove all your evils
And I shall raise up your justice as that from former time and your Counselors as that from in front of us, and after this you shall be called The City of Righteousness and The City of Faith.
Zion shall be redeemed in judgment and her captivity in righteousness
And the ruin of the evil and of sinners as one, and those who have forsaken LORD JEHOVAH, they shall be consumed
Because they will be ashamed of the idols for which they lusted and they shall ashamed of the hiding places that they chose
Because they will be as the oak that sheds its leaves and like a garden that has no water
And their strength will become linen and their works sparks, and both shall burn as one, and there is none who extinguishes’”
The matter that Eshaiya, son of Amuts, saw about Yehuda and about Jerusalem
It shall be in the last days, he sets in order the Mountain of The House of LORD JEHOVAH at the top of the mountains, and higher than the hills, and all the nations shall look for it

And the many nations shall go and they shall say: “Come, let us go up to the mountain of LORD JEHOVAH, and to the house of The God of Yaqob, and we shall learn of his ways and we shall walk in his steps, because The Law has gone out from Zion and The Word of LORD JEHOVAH from Jerusalem”

And he shall judge among the nations and he shall rebuke the many nations who are distant, and they shall beat their swords to the blades of a plow, and their spears to sickles, and nation shall not take up the sword against nation, and they shall learn war no more
House of Yaqob, come, let us go into the light of LORD JEHOVAH

Because you have forsaken your people of the house of Yaqob, because they were full like that of former time, and they practice divination like the Philistines, and they have raised a multitude of estranged children
And their land was filled with silver and gold and there is no end to their treasures, and their land was filled with horses and there is no limit to their chariots
And their land was filled with idols. They were bowing down to the work of their hands, to that which their fingers made
And a man is humbled, and the mighty man is fallen, and you shall not forgive them
Enter into the rock and fall like rain into the dust from before the awe of LORD JEHOVAH, and from the excellency of his strength
The lofty eyes of man shall be humbled and the high place of man shall be laid low, and LORD JEHOVAH alone shall prevail in that day
Because the day of LORD JEHOVAH is upon everyone who is proud and lifted up and upon everyone who is exalted, that he shall be humbled
And upon all the cedars of Lebanon that are high and exalted, and upon all the oaks of Bayshan
And upon all the mountains that are high and upon every high hill
And upon every high tower and upon every wall that is fortified
And upon all the ships of Tarshish, and upon all the desirable sights
And the loftiness of man shall be laid low, and the pride of man shall be fallen, and LORD JEHOVAH alone shall prevail in that day
And the idols shall completely pass away from the Earth
And they shall enter into caves of stone and into holes of the dirt from before the awe of LORD JEHOVAH and from the excellence of his strength, whenever he rises to tread the Earth
In that day men shall throw idols of gold and of silver, that they had made for themselves to worship, to nothingness and to the bats
To enter into caves of flint and into cliffs of stone from before the awe of LORD JEHOVAH and from the excellence of his strength, whenever he rises to tread the Earth
Withdraw yourselves from man whose breath is in his nostrils, for as what is he esteemed?
Because, behold, THE LORD OF LORDS takes away from Jerusalem and from Yehuda the male supporter and the female supporter, every supply of bread and every supply of water
And the mighty man and the Warrior and the Judge and the Prophet and the Soothsayer and the Elder
And the Captain of fifty and the honored faces and the Counselor and the wise Carpenter and the discerning ones in counsel
And I shall raise up young men as their Princes, and mockers shall have dominion over them
And the people shall fall, man against man, a man against his fellow man, and young man shall contend against the old and the dishonorable against the honorable
Because a man will seize a man, his brother, from the house of his father, and shall say to him: ‘The robe is for you; be a Ruler for us; this subversion is under your hand!’
