But you, son of man, the children of your people are plotting against you by the side of the wall, and at the doors of the houses, and they speak one to one, a man with his brother, and they say: ‘Hear what word went out from before LORD JEHOVAH’
And the people are coming to you and are sitting before you and hearing your words, and they do not do them, because a lie is in their mouths, and it is after the mind of their heart they are going
And you are considered by them as a song and like a beautiful voice and like a sweet harp, and they hear your words, and they do not act on them
But when your words have happened, then they shall know that you are a Prophet among them”
And the word of LORD JEHOVAH was upon me, saying:

“Son of man, prophesy against the Shepherds of Israel. Prophesy and say to them: ‘Shepherds, thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: Woe, Shepherds of Israel, who feed themselves! It is not the sheep the Shepherds are feeding!
You eat the fat males and you wear their wool, and you slaughter fatlings, and you do not feed the sheep

Those who are weak you have not strengthened, and those who are sick you have not healed, and that which was broken you have not bound up, and that which strayed you have not returned, and that which is lost you have not searched for, but by violence you have enslaved them
And my sheep were scattered without a Shepherd, and they were food for every animal of the wilderness
And my sheep gone astray in all the mountains and upon every hill that is high, and my sheep were scattered in all the Earth, and there is none who seeks, also none who gathers
Because of this, Shepherds, hear the word of LORD JEHOVAH

As I am alive, says THE LORD OF LORDS, because my sheep were for prey and my sheep were food for every animal of the wilderness which is without a Shepherd, and the Shepherds did not feed my sheep, but the Shepherds fed themselves and they did not feed my sheep
Because of this, Shepherds, hear the word of LORD JEHOVAH

Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: “Behold, I am against the Shepherds, and I shall demand my sheep from their hands, and I shall stop them that they shall not shepherd my sheep again, and I shall save my sheep from their mouths, and they shall not be food for them anymore
Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: ‘Behold, I seek my sheep and I care for them

As when a Shepherd takes care of his flock in the day of violent rain, so I shall take care of my sheep and I shall gather them from all of the regions where they were scattered, in the day of clouds and of thick darkness

And I shall bring them out from the nations, and I shall gather them from the cities, and I shall bring them to their land and I shall shepherd them in the mountains of Israel, and in the valleys, and in every dwelling of the land

In the place of The Good Shepherd I shall shepherd them, and in the high mountains of Israel their fold shall be; there they shall feed in a good fold and they shall graze a heavenly pasture in the mountains of Israel
I shall shepherd my sheep and I shall make them lie down, says THE LORD OF LORDS

I shall seek that which is lost and I shall return that which has strayed, and I shall bind up that which was broken, and I shall strengthen what is weak and that which is fat, and I shall preserve that which is strong, and I shall shepherd them with justice
But to you, my sheep, thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: ‘Behold, I judge between a ewe and a ewe, and between a ram and a ram

Is the good pasture not enough for you, Shepherds, which you graze, and the rest of your pasture you tread on with your feet, and you drink water, and the rest that are left you are troubling with your feet?
And my sheep graze the treading of your feet and drink the waters that your feet stirred up
Because of this, thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: behold I judge between a fat ewe and a sickly ewe
Because they were pushing with their sides and with their shoulders and were goring all the weak with their horns until they scattered them out
And I shall save my flock, and it will not be again a prey to them, and I shall judge between one ewe and another ewe, and between one ram and another ram
And I shall raise up a Shepherd over them, and David my Servant shall shepherd them; he shall feed them and he shall be a Shepherd to them
And I, LORD JEHOVAH, I shall be God to them, and David my Servant shall be the Ruler among them
And I shall make covenant with them, a covenant of peace, and I shall rid the land of harmful animals, and they shall stay in the wilderness in quietness, and they shall sleep in the forest
And I shall give to them my blessing around my hill, and I shall bring down rain in its time, and it will be the rain of blessing

And the trees of the land shall produce fruit, and the Earth shall give its harvests, and they shall dwell in their land in confidence, and they shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH, when I have broken their yoke and I have delivered them from the hand of their slave masters
And they shall not be prey again for the nations, neither shall the beasts of the field eat them, and they shall dwell in confidence, and there shall be none harmful to them
And I shall raise up for them the planting of peace, and they shall suffer no more pain in famine in the earth, and they shall bear no more reproach from the Gentiles
And they shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH, their God, and those are my people of the house of Israel, says THE LORD OF LORDS
But you are my sheep, the flock of my pasture. You are children of men, and I AM LORD JEHOVAH your God, says THE LORD OF LORDS”
And the word of LORD JEHOVAH was upon me, saying:
“Son of man, set your face toward the mountain of Seir, and prophesy over it
And say to it, ‘Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: behold, I am against you, Mountain of Seir, and I shall lift my hands over you and I shall make you a desolation and an astonishment
And your cities I shall make a wilderness and you shall be a desolation, and you shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH
Because hate was yours for eternity, and you handed over the children of Israel to the hand of the sword in the time of their adversity, and at the time of the evil of their cutting off
Because of this, I live says, THE LORD OF LORDS. How shall I do to you? And blood shall pursue you; the blood that you hated, that blood shall pursue you
And I shall make the mountain of Seir a desolation and an astonishment, and I shall destroy from it him who passes through and him who returns
And I shall fill his mountains with his slain, your cliffs and your hills and your valleys. The slain of the sword shall fall in them
And I shall make you a desert for eternity, and your cities shall not be inhabited, and you shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH
Because you said: "Two nations and two kingdoms are mine, and I have inherited them, and LORD JEHOVAH was there.”

Because of this, I live, says THE LORD OF LORDS. I shall do to you according to your wrath and according to your hate that you have committed, and you have hated them, and I shall be revealed among them when I judge you
And you shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH. I have heard all your blasphemies that you spoke against the mountains of Israel: “We have destroyed them and they are our food"
And you thrust out your mouth against me, and you multiplied your words against me, and I have heard”
Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS to the mountain of Seir, which dwells in the joy of all the Earth: ‘I shall make you a desolation’

Because you rejoiced against the inheritance of the house of Israel that was desolate, thus I shall make you. You shall be for destruction, Mountain of Seir, and all of Edom, and they shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH
And you, son of man, prophesy over the mountains of Israel, and say to the mountains of Israel: ‘Hear the word of THE LORD OF LORDS
Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: because the enemy said against you, ‘Aha!’, and he said, ‘The hills are our inheritance from eternity’

Because of this, prophesy, and say, ‘Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: because you were shamed and you were laid waste, and you were blasphemed by those around you, and you were an inheritance to the rest of the nations, and you have fallen into the speaking of languages, and into the mockery of the nations’

Because of this, hear the word of THE LORD OF LORDS: ‘Mountain of Israel, thus says THE LORD OF LORDS to the mountains, and to the hills, and to the rivers and to the valleys, and to the ruins that are laid waste, and to the cities that are left, and they were prey and mockery to the rest of the nations around them

Because of this, thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: ‘In the fire of my zeal I have spoken against the rest of the nations, and against all of Edom, because they have made my land an inheritance for themselves, in the joy of their heart. They have disgraced souls, that they would take them captive and would plunder them

Because of this, prophesy against the land of Israel, and say to the mountains, and to the hills, and to the rivers, and to the valleys:, ‘Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: ”Behold, I have spoken in my zeal and in my anger, because you have borne the reproach of the nations"’
Because of this, thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: ‘I have lifted a hand against the nations around you, that they shall bear their shame
But you, mountains of Israel, you shall give your branches, and you shall grow your fruit for my people Israel, because they are near to come
And behold, I turn to you, and you shall be plowed, and you shall be sown
And I shall multiply children of men for you, the entire house of Israel, and the cities shall be inhabited and the desolations shall be built

And I shall multiply children of men and beasts for you, and they shall grow and they shall multiply, and I shall settle you as from former time, and I shall do good to you as from the beginning, and you shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH
And I shall make children of men walk among you, my people Israel, and they shall inherit you and you shall be to them an inheritance, and you shall not continue to be bereaved anymore
Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: because they say to you, ‘You consumed children of men, and you bereaved her people’
Therefore, you shall not devour children of men again, neither shall you bereave your people again, says THE LORD OF LORDS
And I shall not hear the reproach of the Gentiles against you again, and you shall not bear their shame again, neither shall you bereave your people again, says THE LORD OF LORDS”
And the word of LORD JEHOVAH was upon me, saying

“Son of man, when the house of Israel was dwelling in their land, and they defiled it in their ways and in their schemes, and according to the uncleanness of a menstruous woman, so was their way before me
And I poured my anger upon them, because they poured out blood in the earth, and they defiled it with their idols
And I scattered them among the Gentiles, and I dispersed them into the cities, and I judged them according to their ways and according to their schemes
And they went to the place of the Gentiles and they defiled The Name of my holiness, and the Gentiles were saying to them: ‘These are the people of God, and they have gone out from his land’
And I had pity on The Name of my holiness, because they of the house of Israel defiled it among the nations to which they went

Because of this, say to them of the house of Israel: ‘Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: it is not because of you I act, house of Israel, but because of The Name of my holiness, which you have defiled among the Gentiles, to whom you went

For I shall hallow my great name which is defiled among the nations, which you have defiled among them, and the nations shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH, says THE LORD OF LORDS, whenever I am hallowed among you in their sight
Also, I shall bring you from the nations, and I shall gather you from the cities, and I shall bring you to your land
And I shall sprinkle upon you pure waters, and I shall purify you from all your filth and from all your idols
And I shall give to you a new heart and a new spirit I shall put within you, and I shall remove the heart of stone from your flesh and I shall give to you a heart of flesh
And I shall put my Spirit within you, and I shall cause you to walk in my commandments, and you shall keep my judgments, and you shall do them
And you shall dwell in the land that I have given to your fathers, and you shall be to me the people, and I shall be to you, God
And I shall save you from all your filth, and I shall call to the grain and I shall multiply it, and I shall not bring a famine upon you
And I shall multiply the fruit of the tree and the produce of the Earth, and you shall not bear the reproach of famine among the nations again
And you shall remember there your evil ways, and your insane schemes, and your persons shall be seized in your evil and in your abominations
And know that it is not because of you that I do these things, says THE LORD OF LORDS. Be ashamed and restrain yourselves from your evil ways, and you shall not die, those of the house of Israel
Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: ‘In the day that I cleanse you from all your evil, I settle the cities and I build the desolations
And the land that was desolate shall be cultivated, which was desolate in the eyes of everyone who passed
And they shall say, ‘This desolate land has become like the Paradise of Eden, and the cities that were put to the sword and overthrown are strong fortress cities!’
And the nations which are left around you shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH. I build ruins and I plant desolations. I, LORD JEHOVAH, I have spoken and I act
Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: in this I again avenge those of the house of Israel, for I shall do for them, and I shall multiply them like flocks of children of men

Like holy flocks, like the flocks of Jerusalem in the time of her feasts, so shall be the cities that were destroyed and they shall be filled with flocks of the children of men, and they shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH
And the hand of LORD JEHOVAH was there upon me, and he brought me in the Spirit of LORD JEHOVAH, and he set me in a plain, and it was filled with bones
And he made me pass over them and around them, and I saw that there were very in the plain, and they were very dry
And he said to me: “Son of man, are these bones living?” And I said, “You know, LORD OF LORDS!”
And he said to me: “Prophesy over these bones, and say to them: ‘Dry bones, hear the word of LORD JEHOVAH!
Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS to these bones: “Behold, I am bringing The Spirit into you, and you shall live!

And I shall put sinews upon you, and I shall bring flesh upon you, and I shall spread skin over you, and I shall put a spirit within you, and you shall live, and you shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH’
And I prophesied as he commanded me, and when I prophesied, there was a sound and an earthquake, and the bones approached, each bone unto its joint
And I saw that sinews and flesh came up upon them, and skin was spread over them from above, and The Spirit was not in them

And he said to me: ‘Prophesy unto The Spirit, prophesy, son of man, and say to the Spirit: “Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: come, Spirit, from the four Spirits (or “Winds”), and breathe into these slain, and they shall live!”’
And I prophesied as he commanded me, and The Spirit entered into them, and they were alive, and they stood on their feet, an army that was very great!
And he said to me: “Son of man, these are all the bones of the children of Israel, who say, ‘Our bones have dried up and our hope is destroyed, and we have destroyed ourselves!’
Because of this, prophesy, and say to them: ‘Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: behold, I open your graves and I bring you up from your graves, and I bring you to the land of Israel
And you shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH, when I have opened your graves and when I have brought you out from your graves
I shall put my Spirit into you, and you shall live, and I shall set you free in your land, and you shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH. I have spoken and I act, says THE LORD OF LORDS’
And the word of LORD JEHOVAH was upon me, saying:

“But you, son of man, take one stick for yourself, and write upon it ‘Yehuda and the children of Israel his companions’, and take another stick and write upon it, ‘Yoseph’ on the stick of Aphreim, ‘And all the children of Israel his companions’
And bring them near, one to another, and they shall become one stick in your hand
And when the children of your people have said to you, ‘Show us what these are’

Say to them, ‘Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: behold, I take the stick of Yoseph in the hand of Aphreim, and the tribes of Israel his fellows, and I shall put them with the stick of Yehuda, and I shall make them one stick, and they shall be one in my hand’
And the sticks upon which you write, hold fast in your hand in their sight
And say to them: ‘Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: behold, I have taken the children of Israel from among the nations to which they went, and I shall gather them, and I shall bring them to their land
And I shall make them one people in the earth, in the mountains of Israel, and they shall have one King, and they shall not again be two nations, and they shall be divided no more into two kingdoms

And they shall be defiled no more with their idols and with their abominations and with all their evil, and I shall deliver them from every house of their dwelling in which they had sinned, and I shall cleanse them, and they shall be to me a people, and I shall be God to them
And my Servant David shall reign over them, and there shall be the one Shepherd for them all, and they shall walk in my judgments, and they shall keep my commandments, and they shall do them

And they shall dwell in the land that I have given to my Servant, Yaqob, in which their fathers dwelt, and they shall dwell in it, and their children, and the children of their children for eternity, and David my Servant shall be their Leader for eternity
And I shall raise up to them a covenant of peace and an eternal covenant shall be with them, and I shall multiply them and I shall put my holy place among them for eternity
And the house of my dwelling will be in them, and those shall be a nation to me
And the nations shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH, I, who hallow Israel, when my holy place is in them for eternity
And the word of LORD JEHOVAH was upon me, saying:
“Son of man, set your face against Gog and against the land of Magog, the Ruler and the Head of Meshek and of Tubil, and prophesy upon him and say
Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: ‘Behold, I am against you, Gog, Ruler and Head of Meshek and of Tubil

And I shall gather you and I shall cast a bridle in your mouth, and I shall bring you out from your place, you and all your army, horses and horsemen who wear armor, all of them a great multitude with spears and with shields and handling swords
All Persians and Kushites and Putians with them, with shields and with helmets
Gomar and all his army, and the house of Togarma and the borders of the North and all their armies, and many nations that are with you
Be ready, you and all your multitude who assemble with you, and be a Guard to them

From the first days you were commanded, and in the end of years you shall come on the mountains of Israel, and upon the land that is at peace from the sword and gathered from many nations, and all of them dwell in it in quietness
And you shall come up like a tumult and like a cloud that hides the land, you and all your army, and many nations with you
Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: ‘In that day words shall come up on your heart, and you shall consider evil ideas
And you shall say: “I shall ascend against the wealthy land, and I shall go against those dwelling in quietness without walls, and they have no bars and no gates”

For you shall capture captivity, and you shall loot the loot, and you shall turn your hand against the desolations that are inhabited, and against the people that were gathered from the nations, who have acquired domestic animals and possessions, and are dwelling in the beauty of the land

Sheba and Daran and the Merchants of Tarshish and all the villages shall say to you: ‘Have you come to take captivity captive and to loot the loot, and have you gathered your gatherings that you would take silver and gold, and will you take beasts and possessions, and will you take great prey?’
Because of this, prophesy, son of man, and say to Gog, ‘Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: in the day that my people Israel will dwell in quietness, you shall know
And you shall come from your place, from the outskirts of the North, and many who are with you, all riders of horses, a great assembly and a great army

And you shall come up against my people Israel like a cloud that covers the land. At the end of days you shall come, and I shall bring you to my land, that the nations shall know me, whenever I have been hallowed by you in their sight’

Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: “You are he of whom I said: in former days by the hand of my Servants the Prophets of Israel, who were prophesying in these days and in these years, that I would bring you against them
In that day when Gog will come on the land of Israel, says THE LORD OF LORDS, my passion will burn in my anger
And in my zeal, in the fire of my passion, I have spoken: "In that day there shall be a great earthquake in the land of Israel

And the fish of the sea shall be shaken from before him, and the birds of the sky, and the animals of the wilderness, and every creeping thing that creeps in the earth, and all the children of men that are upon the face of the Earth, and the mountains shall be thrown down, and the towers shall fall, and all the walls in the earth shall fall
And I shall call for a sword upon him in all my mountains, says THE LORD OF LORDS. The sword of a man shall be into his brother
And I shall judge him with death, and with blood, and rain that overwhelms, and I shall send down hail stones and fire and brimstone upon him and upon his Princes and upon the many nations with him
And I shall magnify myself, and I shall be hallowed before the eyes of the many nations, and they shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH
And you, son of man, prophesy against Gog and say: “Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS, ‘Behold I am against you, Gog, Leader and the Head of Meshek and of Tubil
And I shall calm you, and I shall gather you, and I shall bring you up from the borders of the North, and I shall bring you to the mountains of Israel
And I shall turn aside your bow from your left hand and your arrows from your right hand
And in the mountains of Israel you shall fall, you and all your army, and the many nations with you, for to the birds of the wing and to the beasts of the land I have given you for food
And upon the face of the field you shall fall, because I have spoken, says THE LORD OF LORDS
And I shall send fire on Magog and upon the dwellers of islands who are in quietness, and they shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH
And The Name of my holiness I shall teach within my people, Israel, and he shall not pollute The Name of my holiness again, and the nations shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH, The Holy One of Israel
The day that I said was to come has arrived, says THE LORD OF LORDS

And the inhabitants of the cities of Israel shall come out, and they shall set fire to the armor and to the shields and to the spears and to the bows and to the arrows and to the clubs of the hands and to the lances and they shall burn some of them in fire seven years

And they shall have no need for wood of the field or of the forest, but they shall burn some of their armor in fire, and they shall capture those who captured them, and they shall plunder those who plundered them, says THE LORD OF LORDS

In that day I shall give there a place to Gog for a tomb in Israel, the great valley that is to the East of the Sea, and they shall close off the valley and they shall bury Gog there and all his army, and it shall be called ‘The Valley of the End of Gog’
And for seven months, they of the house of Israel shall bury them, and the land shall be cleansed
And they shall bury all the people of the land, and it will be fame for them, in the day that I am glorified, says LORD JEHOVAH
And after seven months there shall be men constantly walking in the land, and burying those who were left in the land, so that it would be cleansed
And everyone who passes in the land and sees the bone of a son of man, he shall set up on its end a heap of stones, until those who bury come picking it up in the Valley of Gog
And the name of the city shall be named, “The Mighty”, and they shall cleanse the land

But you, son of man, say to all the birds of the sky and to every animal of the wilderness, ‘Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: be gathered and come from every place to the great sacrifice that I sacrifice to you in the mountains of Israel, and you shall eat flesh and drink blood
The flesh of mighty men you shall eat and the blood of Princes of the Earth you shall drink, of rams and of calves, and of hegoats and of bulls and the young bullocks of Bayshan, all of them
And you shall eat flesh and you shall be satisfied, and you shall drink blood, and you shall be drunk from the sacrifice that I sacrifice to you
And you shall be satisfied from my table: the flesh of horses and of their riders, and of mighty men and of all the warrior men’, says THE LORD OF LORDS
And I shall accomplish my glory among the nations, and all of the nations shall see the judgment that I have executed, and my hand that I have set upon them
And they of the house of Israel shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH their God, from that day and beyond

And the nations shall know that because of their evil the children of Israel were taken captive, for they have done evil to me, and I turned my face from them and I handed them over into the hands of those hating them, and all of them fell by the sword
And according to their abominations and according to their evil I have paid them, and I turned my face from them”
Because of this, thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: therefore I return the captivity of Yaqob and I show compassion on all the house of Israel, and I am zealous because of The Name of my holiness
And they shall bear all that they have done to me in their shame and their evil when they dwelt in their land in quietness, and there was none molesting them
Whenever I have gathered them from the nations and I have brought them from the cities of their enemies, and I am hallowed by them before the eyes of the many nations
And they shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH their God. I have caused them to be captured among the nations, and I shall gather them to their land, and I shall not leave some of them there
And I shall not turn my face from them, but I shall pour out my Spirit on all the house of Israel, says THE LORD OF LORDS”
In the twenty fifth year of our captivity, at the first of the year, in the tenth of the month in the fourteenth year after the city was defeated, on this day the hand of LORD JEHOVAH was upon me

And he brought me there in a vision of God, and he brought me to the land of Israel, and he set me on a mountain that was very high, and there was upon it, on its south side, like the edifice of a city
And he brought me there, and I saw a man, and his appearance was like the appearance of brass, and a cord of linen was in his hand, and a measuring reed, and he was standing in the gate

And that man said to me: ‘Son of man, see with your eyes and hear with your ears and put in your heart everything that I show you, because it is to show you I have come here, and everything that you see, show to the children of Israel’

And I saw a wall that encircled that house and a measuring reed in the hand of that man, and it was six cubits and a handbreadth, and he measured the width of the building, one reed, and its height, one reed
And he came to the gate that looks to the East and he went up on its steps and he measured the threshold of the gate, one reed to its width, and the other threshold, one reed for its width
And the porch, one reed for its length and one reed for its width, and five cubits between the door posts, and the threshold of the door between the door posts of the gate, one reed
(This redundant verse about the porch’s measurements according to the Hebrew is “He measured also the porch of the gate within, one reed.” see v.7, and is not found in the Peshitta Bible.)
And he measured the door posts of the gate, eight cubits, and the threshold of the gate from within, two cubits
And the inside of the eastern door, three from here and three from there, and one measure to their three, and one measure for the threshold that was from here and from there
And he measured the width of the entrance of the gate, ten cubits, and the length of the gate, thirteen cubits
And the border that was before the door post, a cubit from here and on that side, and the inside, six cubits from here and six cubits from there
And he measured the gate from its roof to its roof, the width twenty and five cubits, and a gate next to a gate
And he made before the face of the gate, sixty cubits, and before the front of the other outside gate, sixty cubits
And before the inner gate, fifty cubits
And windows that are spread out from within, and narrow ones from outside on planks, and upon the door posts of the gate, and windows that were surrounding them from within
And he brought me to the outer courtyard, and I saw thirty steps in a spiral, stepping one above another
And the steps were on the side of the gate that was against the length of the stairs
And he measured its width from before the door of the courtyard, a hundred cubits to the East and to the North
And the gate that is before it from the north of the courtyard, he measured its length and its width
And its measurement, three from here and three from there, like the measurement of the first door, fifty cubits in its length, and its width, twenty cubits and five
And its windows and its lintels were like the measure of the gate that faces to the East, and in seven steps going up to it; and a vestibule is before them
And the gates of the inner courtyard that were opposite the gate of the northeast, he measured from gate to gate, a hundred cubits
And he carried me to the road of the south, and I saw the gate of the south, and he measured its gate posts and its pillars according to these measurements
And it has windows and its gate posts circling like these windows, and fifty cubits was its length and twenty and five its width
And with seven steps going up to it, and a vestibule was before it, and palm trees were with it, one from here and one from there, by its gate posts
And the gates of the inner court which is in the way of the south, he measured from gate to gate, a hundred cubits
And he brought me to the inner court in the gate of the south, and he measured the southern gate according to these dimensions
And the measurements of its inside and its porches and its vestibules are like these, and it has windows and porches in a circle, fifty cubits its length and twenty and five its width
And its porches circling it, their length was twenty and five cubits and their width five cubits
And the porches of the outer courtyard had palm trees by them, and their eight steps
And he brought me to the inner courtyard in the way of the East, and he measured the gate like these measurements
And the inside and its porches and its vestibules and windows are in it, and its porches in a circle; the length was fifty cubits and the width twenty and five
And the vestibules of the outer courtyard, and the palm trees were in its porches, from here and there, and it had eight steps
And he brought me to the north gate, and he measured according to these measurements
And the inside and its porches and its posts and the windows went around it; its length was fifty cubits and its width twenty and five
And the porches of the outer courtyard, and palm trees were in its porches, here and there, and it had eight steps
And there was a chamber door in the porches of the gate, and there they lay burning peace offerings
And in the porches of the gate were two tables here and two tables there, to kill upon them burning peace offerings, and offerings, and the purification of sin
And in the side of the north gate, outside of the burning peace offerings, were two tables, and in the other side of the porches, two tables
Four tables here and four tables there, in the porches of the gate of the land, and they are the eight tables upon which they slaughter

And four tables of cut stone for a burnt peace offering, its length a cubit and a half, and its width a cubit and a half, and lying upon them are the instruments which those slaughtering burning peace offerings and sacrifices have by them
And their edges were a handbreadth going around, and they were laying upon the table the flesh of offerings

And outside of the inner gate were arched chambers of Princes in the inner court, in the side of the north gate, and their faces to the south, one from the side of the eastern gate which was against the north wind
And he said to me: “This chamber that faces to the south is for the Priests who keep guard of the house
And the chamber that faces the north is for the Priests, the sons of Tsadoq, who keep guard of the altar, who come near from the sons of Levi, to do the service of LORD JEHOVAH
And he measured the courtyard, its length a hundred cubits, and its width a hundred cubits, foursided, and the altar that was before the house
And he brought me to the porch of the house, and he measured a post five cubits from here and five cubits from there, and the width of the gate was three cubits from here and three cubits from there
And the length of the porch, twenty cubits, and its width, eleven cubits, and by steps they were going up to it, and the pillars stood by the door posts, one here and one there
And he brought me to the Temple, and he measured a post: its width was six cubits from here, and its width, six cubits from there

And the width of the Tabernacle and the width of the door, ten cubits, and the sides of the door, five cubits from here and five cubits from there, and he measured its length forty cubits, and its width twenty cubits
And he entered within and he measured into the doorway two cubits, and the door six cubits, and its width seven cubits
And he measured its length twenty cubits, and its width twenty cubits next to the temple, and he said to me: “This is The Holy of Holies”
And he measured the wall of the house, six cubits, and the width of the side, seven cubits circling the house
And the side partitions, partition on partition, thirty three of them, and the side partitions entered into the wall of the house in a circle that were enclosed and separate from the wall

And the partitions were joined above that encircled the house above, that enlarged the house from above, and from beneath; they would be going up to the middle ones, and from the middle ones to the upper rooms
And I saw the height of the house as a circle, and they were separate one from another, a full reed of six cubits
And the width of the wall of the side partition outside was five cubits, and from within, five cubits
And the width between the chambers which encircled the house was twenty cubits
And the door of the side partition that was separate, one to the south, and one to the north, and the width of the place that was separate, five cubits

And the building that was before the partition space in the west gate: its width was seventy cubits and the width of the wall of the building was five cubits in a circle, and the length was ninety cubits
And he measured the house, its length a hundred cubits, and the length of the separate partition of the building of the wall was a hundred cubits
And the width next to the house and the eastern partition, a hundred cubits

And he measured the length of the building that was before the separate partition, and in its place and its porches from here and from there, a hundred cubits, and the inner Temple and the porches of the gates of the courtyard
And the door posts and the windows and the porches going around their three gates before the wood as it went around, and its length was as far as the windows
And the windows were covered above the door of the inner house, and outside to all the walls in a circle. He measured from outside and from within
And he made Cherubim and palm trees, and a palm tree between a Cherub and a Cherub, and two faces to each Cherub
And the face of a son of man toward palm trees, from here and from there, was made for all the house in a circle
From the ground and up to above the doors, the Cherubim and palm trees were made
And the wall of the Temple was square, and the front of The Holy Place of the vision was like the appearance of an altar of wood
Three cubits was its height, and its length, two cubits, and it had horns on it, and its length and its railings of wood. And he said to me: “This is the table that is in the presence of LORD JEHOVAH
And two doors each for a folding door
Two for one folding door and two for another folding door
Cherubim and palm trees were made on the doors of the temple like those made in the walls and the face of the porch, overlaid with wood from the outside
And narrow windows and palm trees were here and there on the sides of the porch, and the sides of the house were overlaid with boards
And he brought me to the outer courtyard to the way north, and he brought me to the chamber next to the separate partition and next to the north building
Next to the length of a hundred forearms of the northern gate, and the width of fifty cubits
Opposite the door of the inner courtyard and opposite the steps of the outer courtyard of the building, one upon another for each of three stories ”
And in front of the chambers of the walking place was ten cubits in width, and the length a hundred cubits, and the door of the walking place was toward the north
And the chambers above were small, because the stairs were entering into them
For they were trisected, and there were no pillars for them like the pillars of the courtyard, therefore they were smaller than the lower ones and the middle ones
And in the outside wall that was opposite the rooms, the length was fifty cubits
Because the length of the rooms of the outer courtyard was fifty cubits, and next to the Temple it was a hundred cubits
And the door of the room of the eastern entrance that enters to the outer courtyard
In the width of the wall of the courtyard, the eastern entrance before the separate partition and before the building of the rooms

And the walkway in front of them and the appearance of the rooms which were on the way of the north, their length was like their width, and all their exits and their entrances and their appearance and their doors
Were like the doors of the rooms which were in the way of the south door, at the head of the way, the way at the entrance of the wall which is in the way of the eastern valley as it comes

And he said to me: “The north chambers and the chambers of the south that are next to the partition, these are the chambers where the Priests eat, they who offer to LORD JEHOVAH the Holy of Holies. There they set the Holy of Holies and fine wheat flour and the purification of sin offerings, because that place is holy

And when the Priests enter, they shall not go out from the Holy Place to the outer courtyard, but there they lay their garments in which they serve, because they are Holiness, and they wear other garments and bring offerings in the place of the people
And he finished the measurements of the inner house and he brought me to the way of the gate that faces to the East and he measured it as a circle
And he measured to the east wind on a measuring reed, five hundred reeds
And he measured to the north wind on a measuring reed, five hundred reeds in a circle
And he measured to the south wind, five hundred reeds on a measuring reed
And he measured to the west wind, five hundred reeds on a measuring reed
To the four winds he measured it, and the wall in a circle: its length five hundred and its width five hundred, that will separate the Holy Place and the common
And he carried me to the gate that looks to the East
And, behold, the glory of the God of Israel came from the way of the East, and his voice like the sound of many waters, and the Earth shone brightly from his glory
Like the appearance that I saw when I came to destroy the city, and like the appearance that I saw at River Kebar, and I fell on my face
And the glory of LORD JEHOVAH was over the house in the way of the gate that faces to the East
And The Spirit picked me up and he brought me into the inner courtyard, and I saw that the house of LORD JEHOVAH was filled with his glory
And I heard the thing that he said to me from within the house, and a man was standing with me

And he said to me: “Son of man, the place of my throne and the place of the soles of my feet where I dwell among the children of Israel to eternity, and they of the house of Israel shall not be defiled again and they shall not pollute the name of my holiness again, they and their Kings in their whoredom, and by the corpses of their Kings and by their idols

For they were making their gates by the side of my gates, and their borders on the side of my borders, and a wall was between me and them, and they polluted the name of my holiness in the evil that they had done, and I consumed them in my anger
Therefore let them put their fornication and the corpses of their kings far away from before me, and I shall dwell among them for eternity
But you, son of man, show this house to the house of Israel, and they shall be restrained from their evil that they have done to me, and they shall measure its likeness

And if they are restrained from the thing that they had done, show them the image of the house and its preparation and its entrances and its exits and all its images and all its laws and all its forms, and draw for their eyes, so that they will keep all its forms and its entire design, and they shall make them
This is the appearance of the house on the top of the mountain, and all its borders as they are circling The Holy of Holies
These are the measurements of the altar by the forearm, which is a cubit and a handbreadth, its hollow of a forearm cubit, and the railing is a span to its edge in a circle, and so in its side
And from the ground and unto the lower railing, two cubits, and its width one forearm, and from the lower railing and unto the upper, four cubits, and the width, a cubit
And the altar was four cubits, and from the altar and above, four horns
And the length of the altar, twelve cubits, and its width, twelve, square on its four sides

And its railing, fourteen cubits its length, and fourteen its width on its four sides, and its railing and the border that was around it was half of a cubit, and its hollow is round, and its steps face to the East
And he said to me: “Son of man, thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: these are the laws of the altar in the day that it is made. They shall offer upon it burnt offerings and they shall pour blood upon it
And I shall give to the Priests and to the Levites who are of the seed of Tsadoq, who come near to me, says THE LORD OF LORDS, to serve to me a bull, son of a herd, for the purification of sin
And take from his blood and sprinkle on the four horns of the altar and on the four corners of the railing and on the border that is around it, and sprinkle upon it, and it shall be clean
And take the bull of purification, and they shall offer it up on the side of the house outside of the Sanctuary
And on the second day, bring for purification a kid of the goats without a flaw in it, and they shall purify the altar as they purified it with the bull
And when you have finished purging, bring a bull, a son of a herd without a flaw in it, and a ram from a flock without a flaw in it
And bring them before LORD JEHOVAH, and the Priests shall cast salt upon them, and they shall bring them as burning peace offerings to LORD JEHOVAH
You shall bring one kid per day, seven days, for purification, and one bull, son of a herd, and a ram from a flock without a flaw in it
They shall be offering seven days, and they shall cleanse the altar and they shall consecrate their hands

And when the days are finished from the seventh day and beyond, then the Priests shall bring to the altar their burning peace offerings and their offerings, and I shall be pleased with them, says THE LORD OF LORDS”
And he returned me to the way of the outer gate of the Holy Place that faces to the East, and I saw it that it was shut
And LORD JEHOVAH said to me: “This gate shall be shut and it shall not be opened, and no man shall enter by it, because LORD JEHOVAH, The God of Israel, enters by it. It shall be shut
The Governor shall sit in it and he shall eat bread before LORD JEHOVAH; he shall enter from the way of the gate of the porch, and he shall come forth from it
And he brought me to the way of the northern gate that was before the house, and I saw that the house was filled with the glory of LORD JEHOVAH, and I fell on my face

And LORD JEHOVAH said to me: “Son of man, put into your heart and see with your eyes and hear with your ears everything that I speak with you, and all the laws of the house and all its designs, and put into your heart the entrances of the house and the exits of the Holy Place
And say to the rebellious house of Israel: “Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: ‘Are all your abominations not enough for you, house of Israel?

But you also bring from the children of foreigners who are uncircumcised in their heart and uncircumcised in their flesh, and they are in my Holy Place, and they pollute my house, and you offer with my bread the fat and the blood, and you nullify my covenant with all your abominations
And you do not keep guard of my Holy Place, but you have made the Keepers of the Guard of my Holy Place your Guards

Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: no son of foreigners who is uncircumcised in his heart and uncircumcised in the flesh shall enter into my Holy Place, and no son of foreigners that is among the children of Israel
Neither the Levites who separated from me when the sons of Israel went astray from me after their idols, and they bore their evil

And they were in my Holy Place and they were ministering in the gates of the house. They shall slaughter burning peace offerings and sacrifices of the people, and they shall stand before them and shall minister to them

Because they ministered to them before their idols, and they were a stumbling block of evil to the children of Israel; because of this, I have lifted my hands against them, says THE LORD OF LORDS, and they bear their evil

And they shall not approach before me to minister to me, neither shall they come near to all my holy things among The Holy of Holies, and in their shame they shall bear all of it and all their abominations that they have done
And I shall make them the Keepers of the Guard of the house, and workers of the service of everything that is in it

And the Priests and the Levites, the sons of Tsadoq, who kept guard of my Holy Place when the sons of Israel went astray from me, they shall approach me to minister to me, and they shall stand before me and shall offer to me the fat and the blood, says THE LORD OF LORDS
Those shall enter into my Holy Place, and they shall approach my table so that they shall serve me, and they shall keep my observances
And when they enter into the gate of the court, they shall wear inner garments of linen, and they shall not wear any wool, also when they serve in the gate of the inner courtyard
Also when inside, they shall put a turban of linen on their heads, and they shall tie a girdle of linen on their waists

And when they go out to the outer courtyard to the people, they shall strip their garments in which they minister, and they shall leave them in the chambers of Holiness, and they will wear other garments, so that they will not hallow the people in their own garments
They shall not shave their heads; they shall not grow their curls, but they shall surely crop their heads
And the Priests shall not drink wine when they enter the inner courtyard
And they shall not take for themselves a widow or a divorced woman as wives, but they shall take only a virgin of the seed of Israel, or a widow who is the widow of a Priest
And they shall teach my people between holiness and common, and they shall make known between impure and pure

And they will arise to judge for judgments, and they shall judge in my judgments, and they shall keep my law and my commandments in all the times of my appointed meetings, and they shall hallow my Sabbaths

And they shall not come to the dead body of a son of man, that they shall not be defiled, but only for his father, or for his mother, or for his son, or for his daughter, or for his brother, or for his virgin sister who had no husband, may they be defiled
And after he is defiled, he will number seven days for himself, and then he shall be purified
And in the day that he enters the Holy Place to the inner courtyard to serve in Holiness, he shall offer for his purification, says THE LORD OF LORDS
And it shall be their inheritance. I am their inheritance, and you shall not give a portion to them in Israel. I am their portion
The meal offering and the purification of the sin offering they shall eat, and every devoted thing that is in Israel shall be theirs
And the first of all first born ones of all your offering that you offer shall be the Priests’, and the first of your dough give to the Priests that a blessing may be in your houses
And anything that suffers pain or is broken, of bird or of beast, the Priests shall not eat

And whenever you divide the land, separate a division to LORD JEHOVAH, and hallow some land, and its length will be twenty and five thousand, and its width ten thousand, and all its borders shall be holy around it
And there shall be for the Holy Place five hundred by five hundred, and there shall be an enclosure as a circle, and fifty cubits for its open courtyard
And from this the measure is: the length twenty and five thousand, and the width ten thousand, and The Holy of Holies shall be in it

And the holy place of land shall be for the Priests that serve in the holy place of LORD JEHOVAH and are brought near for his service to LORD JEHOVAH, and they will have a place for houses, and a holy place for the Holy Place
And the length shall be twenty and five thousand and the width ten thousand, and twenty houses will be an inheritance for the Levites who serve in The House
The inheritance of the city is five thousand in width and twenty and five thousand in length. It shall be next to the division of the Holy Place for all of the house of Israel
And for the Ruler of the people, for a division of Holiness and for an inheritance of the city, from the East and from the West, and the length shall be equal to the division from East to West
It shall be an inheritance to the children of Israel, and the Rulers of my people shall not harm them again, and they shall give the land to the children of Israel for their tribes

Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: “It is enough for you Princes of Israel! Put away from you plunder and spoil and execute judgment and righteousness and remove your oppression from my people, says THE LORD OF LORDS!
You shall have a scale of truth and a measure of truth and a weight of truth
Make ready a measure and a weight that you will be taking up tithes, one part of ten, equally
And there shall be twenty shekels, plus a shekel from them twenty and five minas, plus a shekel of fifteen shall be for you one mina
This is the offering: offer one of six parts of a cor of wheat and one of six parts of a cor of barley
And oil of ten measures that is a cor. They shall receive a tenth measure each
And one ewe of every two hundred sheep of Israel for the meal offering, and for the offering, and for the burning peace offering that will purify them, says THE LORD OF LORDS
All the people of the land shall separate this gift for the Ruler of Israel

And there shall be burning peace offerings and fine wheat flour and a drink offering for the Ruler on the first of the month, and on the Sabbaths, and in all the feasts of the house of Israel. He shall offer the purification of sin and fine wheat flour and a burning peace offering, a gift that purifies the children of Israel
Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: “In the first day of the first month, take a bull, a son of a herd that has no flaw in it and purify the Holy Place
And the Priest shall take from the blood of purification and he shall sprinkle on the door posts of the house and upon the four corners of the altar and upon the posts of the gate of the inner court
And thus a man who does wrong and goes astray shall be doing on the seventh of the month, and they shall cleanse the house
And on the fourteenth of the first month shall be Passover and the feast for you, and you shall eat unleavened bread seven days
And the Leader shall offer a bull in that day for the sake of his soul, and for the sake of all the people of the land, for the purifying of sin

And during the seven days of the feast he shall offer burning peace offerings to LORD JEHOVAH, seven oxen and seven rams without a flaw in them, for the purification of sin, and a male kid of the goats by day
And a bushel basket of fine wheat flour for the bull, and a bushel basket for the ram, and a hin of oil for a bushel basket

And in the fifteenth of the seventh month, he shall make a feast like these seven days according to the purification of sin, and according to burning peace offering, and according to the meal offering, and according to the oil

Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: “The gate of the inner courtyard that faces to the East shall be shut the six days in which work is done, and on the day of the Sabbath it shall be opened, and on the day of the first moon it shall be opened

And the Ruler will enter in the entrance of the porch of the gate from outside, and he shall stand by a post of the door, and the Priests shall bring the burning peace offerings and his gifts, and he shall worship at the post of the door, and he shall go out and the gate will not be shut until evening
And the people of the land shall worship in the entrance of that gate on the Sabbaths that are on the beginnings of the months, before LORD JEHOVAH
And burning peace offerings that the Ruler brings before LORD JEHOVAH on the day of the Sabbath: six lambs without a flaw in them, and a ram without a flaw in it
And he shall make a bushel basket of fine wheat flour for the bull, and a bushel basket for the ram, and for the lambs, according to what has arrived in his hands, but a hin of oil to a bushel basket
And on the first day of the month, a bull without a flaw in it, and six lambs without a flaw in them, and a ram without a flaw in it

And he shall make a bushel basket of fine wheat flour for the bull, and a bushel basket for the ram, and for the lambs, according to what has come into his hands, but a hin of oil for each bushel basket
And whenever the Ruler enters the porch of the gate, he shall enter and he shall go out by it

And when the people of the land enter before LORD JEHOVAH in the time of the feasts, he who enters into the north gate to worship shall go out the south gate, and he who enters the south gate shall go out into the north gate, and he shall not return by the gate that he enters, but he shall go out opposite it
But the Ruler who is among you, by which gate he enters he shall go out

And at the time of the feasts there shall be a meal offering: a bushel basket with the bull, and a bushel basket with the ram, and with the lambs, a man according to what has come into his hands, but a hin of oil for each bushel

And whenever the Leader brings burning peace offerings or gifts set apart for LORD JEHOVAH, the door that faces to the East that was shut shall be opened to him, and he shall bring the burning peace offerings and his gifts, and they shall shut the door, and then he shall go out as he did on the day of the Sabbath
And he shall be bringing a lamb, a son of a year without a flaw in it, a burning peace offering to LORD JEHOVAH by day, from dawn to dawn
And he shall offer fine wheat flour upon it from dawn to dawn, one sixth of a bushel basket, and one third hin of oil, to lay the meal offering for LORD JEHOVAH, the constant law to eternity
And they shall bring lambs and fine wheat flour and oil, from dawn to dawn, a burning peace offering constantly
Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: “When the Leader will give a gift to one of his sons, it will be his inheritance to his children

And when he will give a gift from his inheritance to one of his Servants, it will be his until the year that he is freed; it shall be an inheritance of the Leader, but that of his children shall be theirs

And the Leader shall not take from the inheritance of the people, and he shall not defraud them of their inheritance, but he shall cause his children to inherit from his inheritance, that my people will not be scattered, any man from his inheritance”
And he brought me into the entrance that was upon the side of the gate, of the inner room of Holiness of the Priests that faces the north, and I saw that there was a place at its border from the West

And he said to me: “This is the place in which the Priests boil the purification of sin, and in which they bake the meal offering, that they will not go out to the outer courtyard, and they will hallow the people”

And he brought me to the outer courtyard, and he brought me over the four sides of the courtyard, and I saw a courtyard at the side of the courtyard, and another court at the other side of the courtyard
On the four sides of the courtyard were small courtyards, forty cubits their length, and thirty their width
And chambers surrounded the four of them
And he said to me: “This is the house of cooks in which the ministers of the house boil the sacrifice of the people

And he moved and he brought me to the door of the house, and I saw the waters that went out from under the eastern porch of the house, and those waters descended from the side of the house, from the south of the altar
And he brought me out by the north gate, and he took me around from outside by the gate of the courtyard that faces to the East, and I saw the waters going out its right side
And while a glorious man went out and a measure in his hand, also he measured one thousand cubits and he brought me into the water unto the ankles
And he measured one thousand cubits and he brought me into water unto the knees, and he measured one thousand cubits and he brought me in water unto the groin
And he measured one thousand cubits, a stream that no man can cross, because the waters had increased, and the stream had become powerful, and no man was able to cross it
And he said to me: “Behold, son of man!” And he carried me and he set me on the bank of the torrent
And as I sat, I saw on the bank of the torrent very many trees, on this and on that side
And he said to me: “These waters that go to Galilee to the east and go down to the north, and they go down into the Sea into stagnant water, and they sweeten the waters
And every living soul that is brought forth where the waters of the torrent are, lives, and there are many fish, because of those waters that go down there, and they sweeten the waters
And fishermen were standing by it from EynGad and unto EynEglyn. It shall be the spreading the nets, and there shall be many fish in it, like that of the Great Sea
And its exits and its entrances shall not be sweetened, but they shall be salt

And in the torrent, all food trees shall grow up on its sides, from here and there. Their leaves shall not fall and their fruit shall not rot, and on the first of every month they shall be bringing new fruit, because the waters that they drink are going out from The Holy Place. Their fruit shall be for eating, and their leaves for healing
Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: ‘The valleys of the border which they inherit for the land, for the twelve tribes of Israel, and for a portion of Yoseph
For you shall inherit it, a man from his brother, for I have lifted my hands to give it to your fathers, and the land shall be divided to you for an inheritance
And this is the border of the land to the north of the Great Sea: the road of Khethrun that enters to Tsadad
And Khamath and Beruth and Sepharvaim that is between the borders of Darmsuq and the borders of Khamath and Khatsar, the middle of which is on the border of Khuran
And the border will be from the Sea to Khatsar Ainan that is on the border of Darmsuq and Tsephon that is in the North, which is on the border of Khamath; this is the north side
And from the east wind from between Khuran and from between Darmsuq and from between Galeed and the land of Israel, and Jordan the border on the Sea east of Tamar; this is the East side
And the south wind of Tamar and unto the waters of Maribath Qadish, the inheritance of the Great Sea
And the border unto Nakakh that is in the entrance of Khamath; this is the West side
And you shall divide this land for the tribes of Israel

And when you shall divide it as an inheritance for yourselves, and those who are converted to me who dwell among you, and they give birth to children among you, they also shall be as the children of Israel and they shall divide the inheritance with you among the tribes of Israel
And the tribe that has in it one who is converted to me shall give him an inheritance in it, says THE LORD OF LORDS

And these are the names of the tribes from the end of the North on the side of the road of Khethrun that is in the entrance of Khamath and of Khetsar Ainan, and the border of Darmsuq that is in the North that is upon the side of Khamath; this is the East side of the Sea of Dan
And opposite the border of Dan from the east wind and unto the west wind of Ashayr
And opposite the border of Ashayr from the east wind and unto the west wind of Naphtali
And opposite the border of Naphtali from the east wind and unto the west wind of Menashe
And opposite the border of Menashe from the east wind and unto the west wind of Rubil
And opposite the border of Rubil from the east wind and unto the west wind of Ephraim
And opposite the border of Ephraim from the east wind and unto the west wind of Yehuda

And opposite the border of Yehuda from the east wind and unto the west wind there shall be a division that you dedicate; the width twenty and five thousand and the length like one of the portions, and from the east wind and unto the west wind shall be The Holy Place within it
The division that you dedicate to LORD JEHOVAH: the length, twenty and five thousand, and the width, ten thousand

And a division of Holiness shall be for the Priests to the North, twenty and five thousand, and the width, ten thousand, and to the West, ten thousand, and to the East, ten thousand, and the South, twenty and five thousand, and the holy place of LORD JEHOVAH shall be within it

And it will be for the Priests who are sanctified from the sons of Tsadoq, for they kept my observances and they have not strayed as the children of Israel have strayed, and like the Levites have strayed
And they shall have a division from the division of the land, The Holy of Holies, in the border of the Levites
And the Levites next to the border of the Priests: the length, twenty and five thousand, and the width, ten thousand, and all their space, twenty and five thousand, and the width, ten thousand
They shall not sell any of it and they shall not exchange it, and they shall not do away with the first fruit of the land, because its holiness is of LORD JEHOVAH
And five thousand that is left next to the entrance, twenty and five thousand. The place is for the city, for the inhabitants and for the suburbs, that the city will be within it

And these are the measurements toward the north wind: four thousand and five hundred; and to the east, four thousand and five hundred, and to the south, four thousand and five hundred, and to the west, four thousand and five hundred
And the suburbs of the city will be to the north, two hundred and fifty, and to the south, two hundred and fifty, and to the east, two hundred and fifty, and to the west, two hundred and fifty

And what is left next to the dedication of Holiness: ten thousand to the east, and ten thousand to the west; it shall be next to the dedication of Holiness, and its harvest food shall be for those who work for the city
And he who works for the city shall be enlisted from all the tribes of Israel
The whole division is twenty and five thousand by twenty and five thousand, and set the division of Holiness apart from the inheritance of the city

And what is left shall be for the Ruler of the city, and from the dedication of Holiness and from the inheritance of the city to the border of the east, twenty and five thousand, and to the West, twenty and five thousand, to the border of the west, next to the division of the Ruler, and the dedication of Holiness and the Sanctuary of the house shall be within it
And some of the inheritance of the Levites, and some of the inheritance of the city, the portion of the Ruler, will be in the city between the border of Yehuda and the border of Benyamin
But the rest of the tribes from the East wind and unto the west wind of Benyamin
And opposite the border of Benyamin from the east wind and unto the west wind of Shemon
And opposite the border of Shemon from the east wind and unto the west wind of Issakar
And opposite the border of Issakar from the east wind and unto the west wind of Zebulon
And opposite the border of Zebulon from the east wind and unto the west wind of Gad
And opposite the border of Gad, from the south wind, shall be the border of Tamar unto the waters of Maribath Qadish, his inheritance unto the Great Sea
This is the land that you shall divide an inheritance to the tribes of Israel, and these are their divisions, says THE LORD OF LORDS
And these are the exits of the city from the north wind: the measurement, four thousand and five hundred
And the gates of the city by the names of the tribes of the children of Israel: on the north, three gates: one of Rubil and one of Yehuda and one of Levi
And on the east, the measure is four thousand and five hundred, and three gates: one of Yoseph and one of Benyamin and one of Dan
And on the south, the measure is four thousand and five hundred, and three gates: one of Shemon and one of Issakar and one of Zebulon
And on the west, the measure was four thousand and five hundred, and three gates: one of Gad and one of Ashayr and one of Naphtali
And around it eighteen thousand, and the name of the city is from the days LORD JEHOVAH named it

name: Daniel
chapters: In year three of the kingdom of Yoiqim, King of Judea, Nebukadnetsar, King of Babel, came to Jerusalem, and he surrounded it

And LORD JEHOVAH delivered it and Yoiqim, King of Judea, into his hand, and he carried the implements of the house of LORD JEHOVAH to the land of Senar, to the house of his idol, and the implements he brought to the treasure house of his idol
And the King said to Ashphaz, the High Eunuch, that he would bring some children of Israel, and some seed of the Kingdom, and some Parthians

There were no boys of blemish among those who were beautiful in their appearance, and were understanding in wisdom and acquainted with knowledge, and were edified in the mind, those who were able to stand in the temple of the King for his administration, and to teach them the writing and the language of the Chaldeans
And the King set up for them a daily statute of the delicacies of the King and of the wine that he drinks, and to nourish them three years, and then they would stand before the King
And there were among them some sons of Yehuda: Daniel and KhananYah and MishEil and Azarayah
And the High Eunuch gave them names; he called Daniel (Beltshatsar) and KhananYah (Shadrak) and MishEil (Mishak) and Azarayah (Abednego)
And Daniel set in his mind that he would not eat from the delicacies of the King, and he would not drink from the wine that he drinks, and he begged of the High Eunuch that he would not feed him
And God gave grace and love to Daniel before the High Eunuch

And the High Eunuch said to Daniel: “I am afraid of my Lord the King who commanded concerning your food and about your drink, lest he would see your faces more sickly than that of the boys, the sons of your age, and the King will cut off my head!”
And Daniel said to Mentsar, him whom the High Eunuch authorized over Daniel, and over KhananYah, and over MishEil and over Azarayah
“Test your Servants ten days, and let them give us grains to eat and water to drink
And you shall see our complexion and the complexion of the boys who eat of the delicacies of the King, and according to what you see, so do with your Servants”
And he submitted to this their word, and he tested them ten days
And after ten days he saw their faces were better, and their flesh fatter than that of all the boys who ate of the delicacies of the King
And Mentsar was taking their provision for himself, and the wine that they drink, and he was giving them grains to eat and water to drink
And to these four boys God gave knowledge and understanding in all writing and wisdom, and Daniel understood all visions and dreams
And after the days that the King had commanded were ended, the High Eunuch brought them before Nebukadnetsar the King
And the King spoke with them and he had not found among all of them any like Daniel and KhananYah and Mishail and Azraya, and they stood before the King
And every word of wisdom and of understanding that the King asked them, he found them excelling ten times more than any Sorcerer and Enchanter that was in his kingdom
And Daniel remained until year one of Kurash the King
In year two of the kingdom of Nebukadnetsar, Nebukadnetsar dreamed a dream, and his spirit was disturbed and his sleep was upon him
And the King said to call the Sorcerers and the Enchanters and the Magi and the Chaldeans to show to the King his dream, and they came and they stood before the King
And the King said to them: I have seen a dream, and my spirit is disturbed to know the dream
And the Chaldeans spoke before the King in Aramaic, and they were saying: “King, live to eternity! Tell the dream to your Servants, and we shall show its interpretation!”

The King answered and said to the Chaldeans: “The matter of which I spoke is firm, that unless you shall show me the dream and its interpretation, you shall be cut up limb by limb, and your houses shall be plundered!
But if you shall show me the dream and its interpretation, gifts and wealth and great honor you shall receive from me, only show me the dream and its interpretation!”
They answered again, and they were saying to him: “Let the King tell his Servants the dream, and we shall show its interpretation!”
The King answered and said: “Truly I know that it is time you seek, because you see that my word is firm

Because if you shall not show me the dream, your judgment is one. You have contemplated to speak a lying and worthless word before me until time will pass, but tell me the dream that I may know that you will also show me the interpretation”

The Chaldeans answered and they were saying before the King: “There is not a man on Earth who is able to show the King’s matter, neither has a High King or Ruler ever asked a matter like this of any Sorcerer, Enchanter or Chaldean
And the matter that the King asks is deep, and there is not a man to show it before the King, but only the gods, whose dwelling is not with children of flesh!”
Then the King was greatly enraged, and in a great rage he said to destroy all the wise men of Babel
And the command went out and the wise men were to be killed, and they were seeking to kill Daniel and his companions
Then Daniel contemplated and he took counsel and said to Ariuk, the High Guard of the King, he who went out to kill the Wise Men of Babel:
“Why is this commandment from before the King so urgent?” And Ariuk revealed the matter to Daniel
And Daniel begged of the King that he would give him time, and he would show the interpretation to the King
Then Daniel went to his house, and he revealed this matter to KhananYah and to MishEil and to Azarayah, his companions
That they would seek mercy from before The God of Heaven concerning this mystery, that Daniel and his companions would not be destroyed with the rest of the Wise Men of Babel
Then the secret was revealed to Daniel in a vision of the night, and Daniel blessed The God of Heaven
Daniel answered and said: “The name of LORD JEHOVAH shall be blessed from eternity and unto eternity, because wisdom and heroic power is his
And he changes times and the seasons. He removes Kings and raises up Kings. He gives wisdom to the wise and knowledge to those knowing understanding
And he reveals profound and hidden things, and knows whatever is in the darkness, and light is with him

To you, God of my fathers, I give thanks and I give praise, because wisdom and heroic power you have given to me, and now you have shown me what we seek from you, and you have shown me the matter of the King!”

And in that moment Daniel went into Ariuk whom the King had commanded to kill the Wise Men of Babel, and he went on, and thus he said to him: “Do not destroy the Wise Men of Babel, but bring me before the King, and I shall tell the interpretation to The King!”
Then Ariuk diligently brought Daniel before the King and said to him: “I found a man of the sons of the captivity of Judea who will make the interpretation known to the King!”
The King answered and said: “Daniel, whose name is Beltshatsar, can you tell me the dream that I have seen and its interpretation?”
Daniel answered and said before the King: “The mystery that the King asked, neither wise men, nor Magi, neither Sorcerers, nor Enchanters can show the King
But there is The God of Heaven who reveals secrets to make known to King Nebukadnetsar when your dream will be in the last days, and the vision of your head that entered your sleep is this:
You, oh King, your thoughts went up upon your heart unto whatever will be at the end, and he has revealed mysteries; he has made known to you whatever will be

And it was not because I have more wisdom in me than all living that this mystery was revealed to me, but so the interpretation would be known to the King, and you will know the thing that was considered in your heart
You, oh King, you were seeing, and behold, one image that was very great, its brightness was extreme and it stood before you. Its appearance was awesome!
Its head was of gold fine, its chest and its arms of silver, its belly and its thighs of brass
Its legs of iron, its feet part of them of iron and part of them earthen
And you saw that a stone was cut out that was not by hands, and it struck upon the image on its feet of iron and earthenware, and it ground them to powder exceedingly

And the iron and brass and earthenware and silver and gold were ground to powder together, and they were as the chaff that is from the summer granary, and a mighty wind took them and a place was not found for them, and the stone that struck the image became a great mountain, and it filled all the Earth by itself
This is the dream, and its interpretation I shall speak before the King
You, oh King, are a King of Kings; God The Most High has given to you a mighty kingdom and honor

And every place in which the children of man dwell, and birds of the sky and the animals of the wilderness, he has given into your hands, and he has authorized you over all of them. You are the head of gold
And after you another kingdom shall be established which is lesser than you, and another third kingdom, which is the brass. It shall rule in all the Earth
And a fourth kingdom will be mighty as iron, and like iron that hammers and pounds everything, so also it shall break up and tread on everything

And because you saw the feet and the toes, that part of them was of earthenware of a potter, and part of them of iron, the kingdom will be divided, and there shall be in the base of iron just as you have seen, iron that was mixed with earthenware of clay
And the toes of the feet that you saw, part of which were of iron and part of them earthenware: part of the kingdom will be strong and part of it will be broken
And because you saw iron that was mixed with earth of clay, in this way they will be mixed with the seed of man, and they shall not be joined to one another, as iron does not mix with clay

And in the days of those Kings, The God of Heaven shall raise up the Kingdom that is for eternity that shall not be destroyed, and the kingdom shall not be left to another people, but it shall pulverize and it will consume all these kingdoms, and it shall be established for eternity

Just as you saw that a stone was cut out without hands from the mountain, and it pulverized the iron and brass and earthenware and silver and gold, The Great God has made known to you what will be in the last days. The dream is true, and the interpretation, trustworthy”
Then Nebukadnetsar the King fell on his face. He bowed down to Daniel, and he said to bring to him incense and gifts
The King answered and said to Daniel: “In truth, your God is the God of gods and The Lord of Kings, and he has revealed secrets, for you were able to reveal this secret

Then the King exalted Daniel and he gave him many gifts, and he gave him authority over all the land of Babel, and presented him Head over all, Chief over all the Generals of the armies and over all the wise men of Babel
And Daniel implored the King, and he authorized Shadrak, Mishak and Abednego over the Servants of the province of Babel, and Daniel was at the gate of the King
Nebukadnetsar the King, made one image of gold, its height sixty cubits, and its width six cubits, and he stood it in the plain of Dura in the land of Babel

And Nebukadnetsar the King sent to gather the Generals of the armies, and the Lords, and the Governors, and the Chief Justices, and the Treasurers, and the Counselors, and the Magistrates and every authority of the city to come to the festival of the new image which Nebukadnetsar the King had raised

Then were gathered all the Generals of armies, and Lords, and the Governors, and the Chief Justices, and the Treasurers, and the Counselors, and the Magistrates and every authority of the city, and they came to the festival of the new image which Nebukadnetsar King had raised, and they were standing before the image which King Nebukadnetsar had raised up
And a Herald called with power and said: “You are commanded, nations and peoples and languages

In the moment that you hear the sound of the trumpet, and the piping, and lyre, and harp and all kinds of music, you shall fall and you shall worship the image of gold which King Nebukadnetsar has raised up
And whoever will not fall and worship, at that moment he will be thrown into a burning furnace of fire!”

And at that moment the nations were hearing the sound of the trumpet, and the piping, and lyre, and harp, and bag pipes and all kinds of music, all the nations and peoples and languages were falling down, and they were worshiping the image of gold which Nebukadnetsar the King had raised
And at that moment Chaldean men approached and they accused the Jews
And they were saying to Nebukadnetsar the King: “Oh, King, live to eternity!
